symptom: Purview Ingestion Service billing charges unexpectedly high; customer does not have many scanning jobs but sees large ingestion quantity in BillingLogEvent
rootCause: ADF, Synapse, or Power BI pushing lineage information to Purview without customer awareness, causing high ingestion volume
solution: Verify ingestion source in Purview Studio (Data Map > Monitor links). If lineage is the cause, disconnect the Synapse/ADF connection to Purview. Use BillingLogEvent Kusto query filtered by Component=Ingestion Service to confirm charges. Query GatewayEvent with IngestionJobId from AdditionalProperties to identify source (DataFactory/Synapse/etc.)
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Purview Billing/Billing Refund/Look Up Billing Charges

symptom: Salesforce scan fails in Purview; test connection or credential errors when scanning Salesforce data source
rootCause: Salesforce connector uses REST API with app created in Salesforce (not Azure). Credential requires 2 Key Vault secrets: (1) user password (or password+security token if IP not trusted), (2) consumer key with consumer secret. Misconfiguration of these secrets or Salesforce app permissions/IP restrictions causes failures
solution: Verify Salesforce app has OAuth enabled and correct IP restrictions. Check 2 KV secrets: Secret 1 = user password (append security token if SHIR IP not in Salesforce trusted ranges), Secret 2 = consumer key + consumer secret. Test with cURL: curl -v -k https://[ENDPOINT]/services/oauth2/token -d grant_type=password -d client_id=[KEY] -d client_secret=[SECRET] -d username=[USER] -d password=[PWD]. If cURL fails → Salesforce config issue; if succeeds → Purview credential issue
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Scanning/3P Data Source Salesforce

symptom: Asset deleted from data source is not automatically removed from Purview catalog after subsequent scan
rootCause: Scan scope is set to individual items (tables/files) rather than their direct parent or higher level. Auto-deletion detection requires the scope to include the direct parent folder/container or higher in the hierarchy
solution: Adjust scan scope to the direct parent level or higher (e.g., database level for SQL, parent folder for storage). Look for a checkbox/tick icon at parent level instead of square icon (partial selection). After adjusting scope and rescanning, deletion takes ~6 hours to reflect in catalog. Verify scan traversal with Kusto: DataScanAgentLinuxEvent | where ScanResultId == "<id>" | where Message has "OpInfo" | extract URI pattern
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Scanning/Asset deletion auto detect

symptom: AWS S3 test connection fails with error about missing external ID in AWS role configuration
rootCause: AWS IAM role trust relationship does not include the ExternalId condition required by Purview credential
solution: Go to Purview credential page, copy External ID. In AWS IAM, Roles, select role, Trust relationships, edit, add Condition: {StringEquals: {sts:ExternalId: copied-id}}. Save and retest.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Scanning/Creating Data sources/AWS S3 test connection failures

symptom: Purview scan credential test fails with Key Vault authentication issues; misconfigured Key Vault URI, secret name, or MSI permissions
rootCause: Multiple possible misconfigurations: wrong KV URI, Purview MSI lacks Get/List secrets, secret name mismatch, or SP missing data source permissions
solution: Verify: (1) Key Vault URI matches actual AKV, (2) Purview MSI has Get+List secret permissions in AKV Access Policies, (3) Secret name in credential matches KV secret exactly, (4) SP has correct data source permissions. If unresolved, escalate via ICM.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Scanning/Creating Data sources/KeyVault Auth Issues

symptom: Purview cannot connect to Key Vault behind private endpoint; AKV connection issues in private network
rootCause: Purview communicates with Key Vault directly (NOT through SHIR). AKV behind VNET requires Trusted Services enabled on firewall
solution: Enable 'Allow trusted Microsoft services to bypass this firewall' in AKV Firewall settings. Do NOT look for SHIR logs for KV issues (Purview-KV traffic bypasses SHIR). For PG escalation provide: AKV firewall screenshot, AKV logs, SHIR logs.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Scanning/Creating Data sources/Keyvault and private endpoints

symptom: Purview (private network) scan of Azure Synapse dedicated pool (public endpoint) fails with Internal system error when using Auto-resolve IR
rootCause: Auto-resolve IR cannot connect from a private-network Purview to a public-endpoint Synapse; requires Self-hosted IR with proper RBAC (Reader on workspace, Storage Blob Data Reader) and SQL login/user created for Purview MSI
solution: 7-step fix: (1) Add Purview MSI as Reader on Synapse workspace IAM, (2) Grant Storage Blob Data Reader on Synapse resource group/subscription, (3) CREATE LOGIN for Purview in Synapse serverless SQL, (4) CREATE USER + db_datareader on each database, (5) Set up Self-hosted IR, (6) Create Key Vault (whitelisted or same VNet as SHIR), (7) Create credential in Purview using Key Vault
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Scanning/Creating or using Credentials/Connect Purview (Private Network) to Azure Synapse Workspace (Public Network)

symptom: Purview scan test connection fails with "Failed to access the [Data Source] account with the given credentials" when using Key Vault authentication
rootCause: Key Vault firewall blocking Purview access, VNET misconfiguration, incorrect credential/secret selection, access policy not configured for Purview, Key Vault Access Control misconfigured, or secret expiration
solution: Verify 7 items: (1) Key Vault has no firewall blocking Purview, (2) VNET config allows connectivity, (3) Correct secret with account key value is selected, (4) Access policy grants Purview MSI Get/List on secrets, (5) Access Control (RBAC) is correct, (6) Secret has not expired, (7) Review detailed scan logs. If all checks pass, open ICM with screenshots.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Scanning/Creating or using Credentials/Key Vault Authentication

symptom: Salesforce test connection fails in Purview with invalid_grant or authentication failure; need to diagnose OAuth Username-Password flow and verify Salesforce credentials
solution: See排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-salesforce-test-connection-failure.md
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Scanning/Creating Data sources/Salesforce Test Connection Failure

symptom: Purview scan test-connectivity fails; general debugging approach using Kusto ScanningLog queries and correlation IDs to diagnose credential and connectivity issues (20501/20502/20503 errors)
rootCause: Most common: credentials not properly added to AKV (mistyped key), or Purview MSI not granted access to Azure Key Vault. Errors 20501/20502/20503 indicate AKV access issues (can take 10 min for ACLs to propagate)
solution: Use Kusto queries: (1) Find accountId via ScanningLog where Message contains account name, (2) Find correlation IDs via ScanningLog where Uri contains /testConnectivity, (3) Debug with ScanningLog filtered by CorrelationId. Verify credential setup per authentication method (account key, SP, SQL auth).
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Scanning/Creating Data sources/Test Connection Issues

symptom: ADLS Gen 2 scan with MSI authentication fails when storage account firewall is enabled; connection fails with firewall + whitelisted client IP, scan fails with firewall + Purview public IP
rootCause: ADLS Gen 2 Storage RP MSI resource match is case-sensitive and does not match the MSI details Purview provides. MSI auth only works when 'all networks' access is enabled.
solution: Use Account Key authentication instead of MSI when ADLS Gen 2 firewall is enabled. MSI+all networks works for both connection and scan. With firewall enabled, MSI fails regardless of IP whitelisting.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Scanning/Creating or using Credentials/Product Specific Security (MSI, AKV, PE, SHIR, VNET)

symptom: Azure Databricks connection issues after metastore ID migration; ADB scan or connection not working after November 2024 update
rootCause: ADB connection was updated to include metastore ID in FQN. All accounts migrated starting November 15. Some connections need manual refresh to pick up new metastore info.
solution: Navigate to ADB Connection under Connection tab, update the description field (any change), and click Save. This action refreshes the metastore information on the backend.

rootCause: Microsoft Purview does not have a fixed client IP range to whitelist in SQL Server firewall rules

symptom: Oracle stored procedures not appearing in Purview scan or lineage results
rootCause: Microsoft Purview does not support query or stored procedure for lineage or scanning of Oracle sources. Lineage is limited to table and view sources only.
solution: Inform customer this is a known limitation. Purview only supports table and view sources for Oracle scanning and lineage. Stored procedures are not supported.

symptom: PowerBI scan fails with error 3870: Unable to access the PowerBI tenant with the specified connection parameters
rootCause: Misconfiguration of Purview-PowerBI integration: Purview and PowerBI not in same tenant, service principal not properly configured, or security group membership incorrect
solution: Verify: (1) Purview and PowerBI are in the same Azure tenant, (2) Customer followed setup doc (register-scan-power-bi-tenant), (3) Power BI admin portal has 'Allow service principal to use read-only Power BI admin APIs' enabled, (4) AAD Security Group has correct Purview MSI as member and type is Security. Collect ScanningLog traces with scan run ID for further diagnosis.

solution: Investigate connectivity issues. If using Self-Hosted IR (SHIR), check SHIR connectivity troubleshooting guide. Verify network connectivity between SHIR and Purview backend services.

solution: Upgrade SHIR to version > 5.8.7866.8 to mitigate the issue

symptom: Scan executor task stuck in queued state for days, scan never starts or completes
rootCause: Insufficient capacity in the Azure Batch Pool backing the scan executor; not enough compute nodes available to schedule the task
solution: Use Azure Batch Diagnostics tool (https://azurebatchdiagnostics.azurewebsites.net/) to check task status with the activity ID. Compare AddedSuccessfully vs ScheduledSuccessfully timestamps. If large gap, the issue is insufficient Batch Pool capacity — check pool node count and scale settings. If scheduling is prompt, investigate further in ADMS

symptom: Unable to complete scan of on-premise SQL Server Named Instance with System Error, connectivity test succeeds
rootCause: Known issue in Integration Runtime 4.xxx when connecting to SQL Server Named Instance; DataScan agent version < 252
solution: Upgrade Integration Runtime to version 5.1.7655.1 or higher. Verify DataScan agent version >= 252 in nuspec.xml

symptom: Assets not visible in Browse page after scan completes; customer clicks 'Browse by Asset Type' but cannot find server/account/top-level assets
rootCause: Scoped scans have a 3-4 hour delay before assets appear in Browse; full scans should show assets immediately after completion
solution: Use Search functionality (keyword + filters) as interim workaround. For scoped scans, wait 3-4 hours. If assets don't appear after 6 hours, file CRI to Data Map team. For ADLSGen1/Gen2/Azure File/Blob/AWS S3 offline job issues, transfer ticket to Offline Tier team. Use Kusto query on DataScanAgentEvent to check if scan was full or scoped.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Search and Browse/Cannot find my assets in Browse/Browse cannot find expected assets

symptom: Search returns HTTP 400 error 'The search request is invalid' when searching keywords
rootCause: Search keywords contain special characters (/, +, -, {, etc.) that are treated as complex query syntax, causing syntax errors
solution: Remove all symbol characters from the search keyword or double-quote the keyword. Use Kusto (OnlineTierDetails with RequestId) to find actual keywords used. If customer needs complex query or result is still unexpected, file CRI to PG team with account name, request ID, target asset, and keyword used.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Search and Browse/Cannot search or browse assets after a successful scan/Browse failure

symptom: Search by column name does not return column entities in search results; searching 'address' returns entities containing 'address' in content but not the column named 'address'
rootCause: Keyword search matches content within columns but not column names themselves; column names are properties of parent entities (e.g., parquet files) and are not indexed as separate searchable entities (tracked by PBI 1135273)
solution: Known limitation. Search for the parent entity (e.g., the parquet file or table) instead of the column name directly. Column names appear as properties of the parent entity in search results.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Search and Browse/Known Issues (Search & Browse Purview)

symptom: Search does not return expected assets when searching by keyword or applying filters
rootCause: Multiple causes: camelCase asset names not matched by partial lowercase keywords; words >10 chars need wildcard; special characters in keywords treated as query syntax; low relevance score pushing results to deep pages; asset may not be searchable
solution: Adjust search keywords: split camelCase names (e.g., 'product detail' instead of 'productde'), append '*' for wildcard on words >10 chars (e.g., 'productdetail*'), remove or double-quote special characters. For other cases, file CRI to PG with account name, request ID, target asset, and keyword used.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Search and Browse/Search results does not return an asset/Search result does not return expected asset

symptom: Portal Private Endpoint not working after Purview GA: customer cannot open Purview portal in VNET; deprecated PE DNS zone privatelink.purview.azure.com
rootCause: Portal PE created before Purview GA uses deprecated DNS zone privatelink.purview.azure.com. After GA the correct zone is privatelink.purviewstudio.azure.com.
solution: 1) Check if private DNS zone is privatelink.purview.azure.com (deprecated). 2) Delete current portal PE. 3) Create new portal PE with DNS integration -> new zone privatelink.purviewstudio.azure.com. 4) If custom DNS, update record from web.privatelink.purview.azure.com to web.privatelink.purviewstudio.azure.com. 5) Verify with nslookup web.purview.azure.com.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Security and Access Control/Private Endpoints/Portal Private Endpoint not working

symptom: Cannot access Purview portal with Private Endpoints when using proxy: error says account is behind PE, please access from client in same VNet
rootCause: Customer outbound traffic routes through corporate proxy, Purview portal sees proxy IP instead of VNET private IP.
solution: 1) Identify proxy via F12 Network tab Remote Address. 2) Edit SHIR diahost.exe.config and diawp.exe.config. 3) Add bypasslist entries for managed EventHub (.servicebus.windows.net), storage (.blob.core.windows.net, .queue.core.windows.net), and web.purview.azure.com.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Security and Access Control/Private Endpoints/Proxy considerations while using SHIR along with Private endpoints

symptom: Browser cache conflict causes Not Authorized error after enabling Private Endpoints on Purview account
rootCause: Stale browser cache from pre-PE session conflicts with post-PE state causing authorization failure.
solution: Perform browser hard refresh (Ctrl+Shift+R) or open Purview account in new tab/InPrivate window from within the VNet configured for Account + Portal PE.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Security and Access Control/Private Endpoints/Troubleshooting Network Connection

symptom: 403 Not Authorized when accessing Purview portal with Private Endpoints even when client is in same VNET
rootCause: Missing Account PE and/or Portal PE configuration. Custom DNS A records for account and portal domains not properly configured.
solution: 1) Create both Account PE and Portal PE matching VNET/subnet. 2) Custom DNS: create privatelink.purview.azure.com zone with A records for Web and AccountName. 3) Add DNS conditional forwarding for purview.azure.com to 168.63.129.16. 4) Add ingestion PE DNS for blob, queue, servicebus. 5) Verify with nslookup and Test-NetConnection port 443.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Security and Access Control/Private Endpoints/Troubleshooting Network Connection

symptom: Cannot add tags on Purview account: HTTP 409 error with deny assignment and policy violation on managed resources
rootCause: Azure Policies block tag addition on Purview managed resources (managed RG storage account and eventhub namespace). Tags on Purview account flow to managed resources, and if policies block tag modifications on those resources, operation fails.
solution: 1) Collect browser HAR file. 2) Look for POST to management.azure.com/batch failing with 409. 3) Run Kusto: RpHttpApiRequests | where AccountName==accountname | where HttpMethod==PATCH to get CorrelationId. 4) Run ProjectBabylonLogs | where CorrelationId in (id) to identify blocking policy. 5) Engage Azure Policy team to help customer modify/exempt the blocking policy for managed resources.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Security and Access Control/Unable to add tags on Purview account

symptom: Snowflake scan URL mismatch: JDBC driver translates underscores to hyphens in Snowflake account URL causing connection failure
rootCause: Snowflake JDBC driver version 3.13.25+ behavior change translates underscores (_) to hyphens (-) in account identifiers, causing DNS resolution failure for privatelink URLs.
solution: Create DNS entry for the hyphenated version to match the original underscore version (e.g. DNS for indusind-edp-nonprod.privatelink.snowflakecomputing.com pointing to indusind-edp_nonprod.privatelink.snowflakecomputing.com). Prerequisites: JDK 11 + Visual Studio C++ on SHIR machine.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Snowflake troubleshooting

symptom: Azure Synapse Analytics scan fails when using Allowed Trusted Services with Private Purview: trusted service connection insufficient
rootCause: Allowed Trusted Services option is not sufficient for Synapse Analytics connections from private Purview. Synapse requires explicit Firewall rule.
solution: Add a Firewall rule in Azure Synapse Network settings to allow Purview IP/VNET. Alternatively use Managed VNet IR with managed PE for Synapse.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Security and Access Control/Security Options

symptom: Purview network connectivity patterns reference: Public-to-Public, Private-to-Private, Public-to-Private, Private-to-Public scenarios

symptom: Customer cannot set custom scan level when using Self-Hosted Integration Runtime (SHIR) in Purview. Scan level configuration is only available for Azure IR and Managed VNet IR.
rootCause: By design limitation. Scan level support is not implemented for SHIR connector; only Azure IR and Managed VNet IR support scan level selection.
solution: Use Azure IR (public connections) or Managed VNet IR (private connections) if scan level customization is required. SHIR does not support this feature; feature request filed (FR 2775/2796).
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Known Issues/2025 Feb FR Known Issues

symptom: UAMI (User Assigned Managed Identity) authentication is not supported for scanning Azure Databricks Unity Catalog data source in Purview.
rootCause: By design. UAMI authentication has not been implemented for the ADB Unity Catalog connector.
solution: Use supported authentication methods for Unity Catalog scan (e.g., Access Token). Feature request filed (FR 2781) for UAMI support.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Known Issues/2025 Feb FR Known Issues

symptom: Fabric/PowerBI scans generate duplicate assets in Purview, causing confusion. Multiple duplicate entries appear for the same Power BI objects.
rootCause: Known product issue with Fabric/PowerBI scanning creating duplicate asset records.
solution: Known limitation with PowerBI/Fabric scan. Feature request filed (FR 2786). No current workaround to prevent duplicates.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Known Issues/2025 Feb FR Known Issues

symptom: Scanning views on Azure Synapse Serverless SQL database is not supported in Purview. Only tables are scanned; views are not captured.
rootCause: By design limitation. Purview scanner does not support scanning views on Synapse Serverless SQL pools.
solution: Not supported. Use alternative approaches (e.g., materialize views as tables) if view metadata is required. Feature request filed (FR 2812).
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Known Issues/2025 Feb FR Known Issues

symptom: Purview scanning captures top-level JSON fields but does not recognize nested fields/objects during scanning. Nested JSON attributes are not surfaced as columns in the schema.
rootCause: By design limitation. Purview scanner primarily extracts top-level JSON fields but does not fully parse or flatten nested JSON structures.
solution: No workaround available. Purview does not traverse nested JSON objects during schema recognition. Feature request filed (FR 2813) to enhance JSON parsing capabilities.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Known Issues/2025 Feb FR Known Issues

symptom: Azure Databricks Unity Catalog connector in Purview does not support Managed Private Endpoint (MPE) or MSI credential. Customer cannot scan Unity Catalog using MPE with MSI authentication.
rootCause: By design limitation. Unity Catalog connector only supports Access Token authentication; MPE and MSI are not available. The Hive Metastore connector supports MSI but not MPE.
solution: Use Access Token authentication for Unity Catalog scans. For private network scenarios, consider Hive Metastore connector (supports MSI but not MPE) as partial workaround. Feature request filed (FR 2831).
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Known Issues/2025 Feb FR Known Issues

symptom: Data Quality (DQ) scans fail for VNet-enabled Azure Databricks data source, even after enabling public endpoint access. The workspace endpoint is accessible but backing cloud storage remains private.
rootCause: Enabling public endpoint on Databricks only makes query JDBC endpoints accessible, but the backing cloud storage account remains private. DQ scans require access to both the JDBC endpoint and cloud storage.
solution: Use Managed Private Endpoint connection route for DQ scans on VNet-enabled Databricks. MPE support for Azure Databricks DQ was planned for April 2025. Feature request filed (FR 2844).
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Known Issues/2025 Feb FR Known Issues

symptom: PowerBI scan using Azure IR does not support Private Endpoints; scan fails when Purview account uses private connectivity
rootCause: Azure IR for PowerBI scans does not support Private Endpoint connections (known limitation)
solution: Use Self-Hosted Integration Runtime (SHIR) as workaround for PowerBI scans with private endpoints. Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/purview/register-scan-power-bi-tenant?tabs=Scenario1#known-limitations
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Known Issues/2025 Feb FR Known Issues

symptom: Data Quality (DQ) and Profiling scans fail for VNet-enabled Azure Databricks data source even after enabling public endpoint access
rootCause: Enabling public endpoint for Databricks workspace makes JDBC endpoints accessible but backing cloud storage account remains inaccessible publicly; Managed Private Endpoint (MPE) for Databricks DQ scans not yet supported
solution: Enable "Allow public endpoint access" as partial workaround (JDBC only). Full MPE support for Azure Databricks DQ scans planned by April 2025 per PG roadmap. Feature Request FR-2844
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Known Issues/2025 Feb FR Known Issues

rootCause: Purview scanner primarily extracts top-level JSON fields and does not flatten or traverse nested JSON objects (by design limitation in schema recognition)
solution: No workaround. Feature request FR-2813 filed to enhance JSON parsing capabilities for nested field recognition

symptom: Customer requests Purview account quota increase but is denied.
rootCause: Since Aug 2023, Purview moved to single account per tenant model. Quota increases for additional accounts are no longer granted.
solution: Advise customer to leverage Collections instead of creating additional accounts. For other quota types, create COLLAB request with Subscription team (provide SubID, reason, amount, region). Kusto: AccountInfoSnapshotEvent in babylonMdsLogs. IcM template for Platform RP quota issues available.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Administration (Provisioning & RBAC)/Limits and Quota Request

symptom: Merge button is not present in Purview despite having the right permissions. Customer cannot merge Purview accounts.
rootCause: An active merge workflow exists under Workflow -> Authoring (named "Merge Purview account" or "Merge Purview Account from Secondary Account") that blocks the merge button from appearing.
solution: 1) Check Data Map -> Monitoring -> Account merging for merge status. 2) Check logs in babylonMdsLogs ProjectBabylonLogEvent for merge request messages. 3) If nothing found, check Workflow -> Authoring for active merge workflows and have customer cancel/reject them. The merge button should reappear. Note: merge is irreversible once completed.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Administration (Provisioning & RBAC)/Microsoft Purview: Merging Accounts - Troubleshooting Guide

symptom: Purview account merge fails with conflicts: scan rule sets with same name, ADF/Synapse connections in both accounts, term templates with same name, or lineage scans in secondary account.
rootCause: Technical limitations: 1) Scan rule set/term template names must be unique per account (used as internal identifiers). 2) ADF/Synapse connections are per-account, not per-domain, and user may lack Owner/Contributor on ADF. 3) Lineage scans depend on ADF connection which has same limitation.
solution: Resolve conflicts before merge: rename duplicate scan rule sets/term templates in one account, remove duplicate ADF/Synapse connections, remove lineage scans from secondary account. Ref: learn.microsoft.com/purview/merge-domain
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Administration (Provisioning & RBAC)/Microsoft Purview: Merging Accounts - Troubleshooting Guide

symptom: Customer unable to upgrade Purview from Free-Tier to Enterprise-Tier in GCC M365 tenant.
rootCause: Purview Data Governance app is not supported in GCC M365 Tenants. Upgrade is blocked for GCC environments.
solution: Verify GCC tenant by calling https://login.microsoftonline.com/{tenantId}/.well-known/openid-configuration and checking tenant_region_sub_scope field for "GCC" value. Advise customer GCC is not supported. Ref: learn.microsoft.com/purview/new-portal-faq
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Administration (Provisioning & RBAC)/New Purview Unified Portal scenarios/FAQs for account upgrade, free-tier and enterprise-tier

symptom: Customer chose wrong Purview account for enterprise upgrade. Needs to switch to a different account.
rootCause: Upgrade process once completed cannot be rolled back. Customer did not verify which account should be promoted.
solution: Contact Purview PG to reconcile to a different account. Provide: tenant ID, current (incorrect) enterprise account, correct enterprise account details. Upgrade is irreversible but PG can promote a different account in exceptional cases.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Administration (Provisioning & RBAC)/New Purview Unified Portal scenarios/FAQs for account upgrade, free-tier and enterprise-tier

symptom: Customer cannot see Purview account in Azure Portal after upgrade to enterprise account.
rootCause: User lacks permissions to view the enterprise account. Subscription-level permissions are not related to enterprise upgrade; only root collection admin or account owner permissions matter.
solution: Verify user permissions per learn.microsoft.com/purview/catalog-permissions. User needs to request access from root collection admin or account owner. Check root collection admin via Data Map -> Collections.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Administration (Provisioning & RBAC)/New Purview Unified Portal scenarios/FAQs for account upgrade, free-tier and enterprise-tier

symptom: Customer wants to change region for Free-Tier or Enterprise-Tier Purview account after creation.
rootCause: Free-tier accounts default to tenant region. Enterprise accounts follow specific region mapping rules. Region change for free-tier results in DATA LOSS.
solution: Follow TSG for region change. Free tier can move between supported regions but DATA WILL BE LOST. For enterprise in unsupported region, customer needs business justification. Do NOT create ICM without following TSG first. Escalate to EEEs for exceptions.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Administration (Provisioning & RBAC)/New Purview Unified Portal scenarios/FAQs for account upgrade, free-tier and enterprise-tier

symptom: Customer wants separate Purview accounts for dev and prod environments but single-account-per-tenant model prevents this.
rootCause: With new setup, only one Purview account per tenant is allowed. Multiple accounts within same tenant are not supported.
solution: Options: 1) Create separate tenants for dev and prod, each with their own Purview account. 2) Use Collections within single account to logically separate dev/prod. Discuss with EEEs if customer insists on multiple accounts.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Administration (Provisioning & RBAC)/New Purview Unified Portal scenarios/FAQs for account upgrade, free-tier and enterprise-tier

symptom: Customer wants to move Purview account across subscriptions or resource groups. Or error when trying to move across regions or tenants.
rootCause: Move across subscriptions/resource groups within same tenant: Supported. Move across regions: NOT supported. Move across tenants: NOT supported. Limitation: accounts created before Dec 15, 2023 - managed storage and Event Hubs will NOT migrate with the instance.
solution: For sub/RG move: Azure portal → Resource Group → Select resource → Move. Prerequisites: both subs must be active, same Entra tenant, destination registered for resource provider. For managed storage not migrating: consider Migration from Managed Storage to Ingestion Storage (see guide). Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/azure-resource-manager/management/move-support-resources

solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-migration-managed-to-ingestion-storage.md

symptom: After Azure subscription migration to another tenant, Purview Managed Identity (MI) shows Identity not found error. Old tenant ID still visible in Purview account properties. Cannot add Purview MI to resource RBAC.
rootCause: ARM automatically removes MSI associated with Purview account during tenant switch. All scans using that MSI will break.
solution: 1) Edit the Purview account in Azure portal (make any subtle change and save) to recreate a new MSI in the new tenant. 2) Alternatively, add a User Assigned Managed Identity (UAMI) under Purview → Managed Identities → Add, which also resets the System MI. Then remove the UAMI if not needed. After MSI recreation, update all scan credentials to use the new MSI.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Administration (Provisioning & RBAC)/Purview Upgrade and Migration/Purview account migration across tenants

symptom: Customer wants to rename Purview account. Cannot rename account after creation.
rootCause: Backend limitation: Purview account name is immutable after creation.
solution: Classic portal: Set a friendly name via Purview Studio → Management tab → Change friendly name. Note: actual account name does not change, only display name in Purview Studio. New portal (purview.microsoft.com): not applicable as it operates at tenant level. Renameable components: Domain name (yes), Collection name (yes), Workflow name (yes), Business/Physical assets (yes). Not renameable: Data Source, Scan, Scan rule set, IR, Classification, Glossary, Term, Credentials.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Administration (Provisioning & RBAC)/Renaming components in Purview portal

symptom: Azure Purview upgrade to enterprise fails due to region mismatch between existing free tier account and intended enterprise tier. Free tier was auto-provisioned in a region based on M365 tenant home location (EOP) → PowerBI tenant location → default mapping, and customer selected a different region for enterprise.
rootCause: Free tier account exists in a different region than the region chosen for enterprise upgrade. The system follows a precedence order: M365 tenant (EOP) home location → PowerBI tenant location → default mapping. When customer chooses a region different from the free tier location, upgrade fails.
solution: 1) Use Kusto query on babylonMdsLogs to find existing free tier accounts: _AccountInfoSnapshotEvent | where time > ago(2h) | where TenantId == '{tenantId}' | where Sku contains 'Free' | distinct TenantId, Sku, CreatedAt, Location. 2) If customer wants same-GEO region change (e.g. North Europe→West Europe): auto-approved, raise ICM to MSG Tenant Provisioning → Customer Escalation & Engagement. 3) Cross-GEO not allowed except for compliance/regulatory reasons. 4) Free tier accounts cannot be self-deleted by customers; deletion requires support request to Purview Platform DRI team.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Administration (Provisioning & RBAC)/TSG for Platform Update Process

symptom: Azure Purview account merge failure when merging classic Azure Purview account into Enterprise Account (Unified Purview). Or customer needs to un-reconcile accounts after reconciling wrong account for testing, then wants to reconcile production account instead.
rootCause: Merge or reconcile operation may fail due to configuration limits or wrong account selection during the upgrade/reconcile process.
solution: 1) Merge failure: No PM approval required as long as within supported configuration bounds (max merge limit). Raise ICM directly. 2) Un-reconcile: Raise ICM to un-reconcile (no PM approval needed). Warn customer about DG layer data loss. 3) After un-reconcile, verify billing model is correct — check if pointing to NEW MSP billing (assets protected/governed) or classic billing (CU/vCores). 4) For any doubts, check with EEEs/Leads in AVA before contacting PM directly.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Administration (Provisioning & RBAC)/TSG for Platform Update Process

symptom: Wrong Purview account was upgraded to enterprise account, need to unreconcile and switch to correct account
rootCause: Customer chose wrong account during upgrade/reconcile process
solution: Inform customer about DG layer data loss, get confirmation. Raise ICM to unreconcile the account (no PM approval needed). Customer will revert to classic Purview account. For billing, verify billing is not pointing to wrong method (MSP vs classic CU/vCores).
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Administration (Provisioning & RBAC)/TSG for Platform Update Process

symptom: Azure Purview upgrade failure when attempting to create enterprise account; free tier account blocks upgrade
rootCause: Region mismatch between existing free tier account and intended enterprise tier account. Free tier region auto-provisioned based on: 1) M365 tenant (EOP) home location, 2) PowerBI tenant location, 3) internal mapping.
solution: Use Kusto query on babylonMdsLogs (_AccountInfoSnapshotEvent, filter Sku contains Free) to find free tier accounts and their regions. If same-GEO change needed → auto-approved, raise ICM with MSG Tenant Provisioning → Customer Escalation & Engagement. Cross-GEO not allowed except for compliance reasons. Free tier deletion requires support request to Purview Platform DRI team.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Administration (Provisioning & RBAC)/TSG for Platform Update Process

symptom: Customer wants to change Purview region (free-tier or enterprise account)
rootCause: Purview account provisioned in non-preferred region due to auto-provisioning logic; customer needs different region for compliance or data locality
solution: Free-tier same-GEO: auto-approved, raise ICM to MSG Tenant Provisioning. Free-tier cross-GEO: allowed only for regulatory/compliance reasons. Enterprise account: warn about DG layer data loss, get confirmation, raise ICM to change location (no PM needed). If DG app not available in target region → consult DG PM.

symptom: Customer needs collection limit increase or additional Purview accounts beyond quota
rootCause: Default limits on collections (~750) and single Purview account per tenant for unified accounts
solution: For classic Purview additional accounts: BCDR and environment segregation (test/dev/prod) auto-approved, no PM needed. For collection limit ≤750: check best practice archetypes (https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/purview/concept-best-practices-collections), approve if following standard archetype. For >750 collections: reach out to PM. Note: Unified Account tenants limited to single Purview account, no exceptions.

symptom: Schema of an asset is missing after successful scan due to sampling failure. Purview attempts to sample 128 rows per column; if the sampling query fails (e.g., data files not found in serverless DB pointing to ADLS Gen2), schema will not be available.
rootCause: Purview samples 128 rows per column during scan. If the underlying data source is inaccessible during sampling, the sampling query fails and schema is not populated. This is by design.
solution: 1) Verify the data files referenced by the source exist and are accessible. 2) If data files are missing/moved, fix the source data. 3) This is by-design behavior - Purview requires successful sampling to populate schema.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Data Map/Asset Schema is missing or incorrect/Schema is missing or incorrect in single assets (Non-Resource Set)

symptom: Asset schema or entire table missing from Purview catalog after scan due to entity payload exceeding size limits (1MB payload, 800 columns, 700k characters).
rootCause: Purview has hard limits: 1MB max payload, 800 columns max, 700k+ characters. When entity exceeds these, Purview drops it entirely. CSV requires >= 3 columns and 1MB limit may cut inside a column.
solution: 1) Check if asset has too many columns (>800) or large schema. 2) Short-term PG fix: truncate schema instead of dropping entity. 3) Mid-term: increase threshold. 4) Scan limits: structured files sample 128 rows or 1MB (lower); document files sample 20MB max; CosmosDB reads top 10 docs.

symptom: Deleted asset still visible in Purview catalog after running scan. Scan scope was not set to include the direct parent of the deleted asset or higher.
rootCause: Scan scope must be configured to the DIRECT PARENT or higher level of the deleted asset for deletion to be detected. Scan must be triggered after asset is deleted from source.
solution: 1) Set scan scope to include direct parent folder/container of deleted asset (or higher). 2) Trigger full scan after deletion. 3) Verify using DataScanAgentLinuxEvent with OpInfo/EntityInfo to check traversed assets.

symptom: Asset count mismatch between Purview UI and REST API query results for Root Collection. Sub-collection counts match but root collection shows more records via API.
rootCause: Wrong filter payload in REST API query call. Missing proper AND clause with NOT condition that Purview UI uses internally.
solution: Use correct filter payload matching Purview UI: filter must include AND clause with collectionId filter AND a NOT clause. Compare customer API payload with exact payload Purview portal sends.

symptom: Parquet files schema and classification missing after successful scan using SHIR. Error: System.DllNotFoundException Unable to load DLL jvm.dll (HRESULT 0x8007007E).
rootCause: Bug in older SHIR versions caused jvm.dll loading failure even when Java was properly installed with correct registry and JAVA_HOME. DLL existed but SHIR could not load it.
solution: Upgrade Self-Hosted Integration Runtime to version 5.28.8488.1 or later. Release note fix: fixed bug where users hit JreNotFound Unable to load DLL jvm.dll with Parquet/ORC format.

symptom: Customer requests to change Purview account region to a different Azure region
rootCause: Purview region is auto-mapped from M365 location or nearest Entra ID home region by design. Region is incidental — does not affect data processing, configurations, or data locality. All data processing is done locally at data source; no data is moved to Purview's region.
solution: Only valid exception: security policy constraints preventing account creation in auto-selected region. Collect: 1) detailed constraint explanation (not just 'data location'), 2) resource ID, 3) existing region, 4) target region. Get PM approval from Blesson John (blessonj@microsoft.com) BEFORE raising IcM. Then create IcM for Purview Platform team with TSG link. IMPORTANT: existing free tier data will be DELETED during region change.

rootCause: Central US region is used internally by Microsoft for testing and is not planned for customer deployment
solution: Decline the request. Inform customer: Central US is not available for Purview deployment and there are no plans to enable it. Redirect to available regions: https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/explore/global-infrastructure/products-by-region/?products=purview&regions=all

rootCause: Week of 11/11/2024: DG solution switched from tracking tenant ID to account ID for migration/reconciliation scenarios, causing temporary visibility gap for recently created data products during migration period
solution: Temporary issue during DG migration (ETA completed 12/6/2024). If customer encounters this after migration period, open IcM with DG team.

symptom: Customer requests CU (Capacity Unit) upgrade due to slow scan performance in Purview Data Map
rootCause: CU is usually NOT the root cause of scan performance issues. Common actual root causes: insufficient SHIR memory, large data volume for scanning, DB server throttling or blocking, network-related issues
solution: Do NOT immediately request CU upgrade. Investigation steps: 1) Check SHIR memory utilization, 2) Assess data volume, 3) Check for DB throttling/blocking, 4) Investigate network issues. Work with SME/PG and complete investigation in AVA first. Only raise IcM for CU increase if root cause is confirmed as CU limit. Require business impact justification from customer.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Administration (Provisioning & RBAC)/User request for upgrading or downgrade CU quota

symptom: Customer requests to downgrade Purview CU (Capacity Unit) quota
rootCause: CU downgrade functionality is not implemented in the platform
solution: Inform customer that decreasing CU limit is NOT supported. Advise caution: once CU is upgraded, it cannot be reversed. Ensure thorough investigation confirms CU is truly the bottleneck before approving any upgrade request.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Administration (Provisioning & RBAC)/User request for upgrading or downgrade CU quota

rootCause: Free tier region auto-assigned based on: 1) M365 (EOP) home location, 2) PowerBI tenant location, 3) internal geo mapping. Customer security policy may require a specific region.

Central US region NOT available for customers (internal testing). After PM approval, raise ICM for Purview Platform team. Note: existing free tier data will be deleted and new account created. symptom: Customer reports unexpectedly high Purview billing charges (Classic Billing Model)
rootCause: Classic Purview billing based on 4 components: Scanning Service, Insights Service, Ingestion Service, Data Map. High Data Map = metadata storage (1 CU = 10GB, ~$300/month). High Ingestion = ADF/Synapse/PowerBI pushing lineage.
solution: Run BillingLogEvent Kusto query (babylonMdsLogs) to identify which component: summarize sum(Quantity) by Component. High Data Map: check metadata storage in Portal, reduce by deleting assets. High Ingestion: check Monitor Links for counts, identify ADF/Synapse connections, disconnect if unwanted. Classic billing only; New Billing Model: learn.microsoft.com/en-us/purview/data-governance-billing.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Billing/Billing Troubleshooting Guideline

symptom: Customer reports high Purview billing but few scanning jobs configured; unexpected ingestion charges from ADF/Synapse lineage
rootCause: ADF, Synapse, or PowerBI automatically pushing lineage to Purview. Only Copy Activity and Data Flow Activity generate lineage. Customer may be unaware of these connections.
solution: Step 1: Check Monitor Links in Purview (Monitor > Scans > Links) for asset/relationship counts. Step 2: Verify ADF/Synapse connection (Synapse: Manage > Microsoft Purview). Step 3: Disconnect to stop unwanted lineage. To ID specific ADF jobs: ADF Monitor > Pipeline Run, look for Copy/Data Flow activities. Kusto: BillingLogEvent with Source filter.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Billing/Billing Troubleshooting Guideline

symptom: Purview billing troubleshooting: comprehensive guide for Classic Billing Model charges investigation
solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-billing-troubleshooting-guideline.md

symptom: Need to check billing quantity for Purview Offline Tier (Resource Set) jobs to investigate vCore-hrs charges
rootCause: Offline Tier jobs (Resource Set meters) generate vCore-hrs charges that need verification via PAV2 cluster
solution: Use PAV2 Kusto cluster (pav2data.eastus.kusto.windows.net). Access: 1) Follow onboarding guide, 2) Join ART Read Access SG, contact Sherry Huang if issues. Query: getUAEUsageData | where resource_provider == ProjectArcadia | where metered_resource_id == a7f57f26-5f31-51e5-a5ed-ffc2b0da37b9 | filter by resourceUri and time window | summarize sum(quantity).
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Billing/Get Billing quantity for Offline Tier jobs

symptom: ADF/Synapse copy or dataflow activity reports lineage error: Failed to report lineage to Catalog in 2 min (timeout)
rootCause: Customer created Private Endpoint in Purview and disabled public network access. Azure IR (AutoResolvedIntegrationRuntime) uses public network and cannot reach private-endpoint-only Purview.
solution: Step 1: Check PE in Purview (Azure Portal > Purview > Networking > Private endpoint connections). Step 2: If PE exists, ensure ADF/Synapse uses Managed VNet IR (NOT Azure IR / AutoResolvedIR). Step 3: Ensure ADF creates PE to Purview and all endpoints approved.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Data Map/ADF or Synapse lineage - Timeout out Status

symptom: Unexpectedly high Purview billing charges despite few scanning jobs configured
rootCause: ADF, Synapse, or Power BI is pushing lineage information to Purview without customer awareness. Only Copy Activity and Data Flow Activity push lineage.
solution: Check Purview Monitor → Links to identify lineage sources and asset ingestion volume. Go to ADF account → Monitor → Pipeline Runs to find Copy/DataFlow activities. Disconnect ADF/Synapse from Purview if lineage is not needed. Refer to https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/purview/how-to-monitor-data-map-population#monitor-links
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Billing/Billing Troubleshooting Guideline

symptom: High Data Map capacity unit (CU) billing charges in Classic Purview
rootCause: Large amount of metadata stored in Purview DataMap. 1 Capacity Unit stores up to 10GB metadata at ~$300/month per CU.
solution: Check metadata storage size in Azure Portal under Data Map Capacity Units monitoring. Use Kusto BillingLogEvent query to identify which component (Scanning/Insights/Ingestion/DataMap) drives charges. If metadata storage is the root cause, reduce it by deleting unnecessary assets.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Billing/Billing Troubleshooting Guideline

symptom: High Ingestion Service billing charges in Classic Purview
rootCause: Excessive asset ingestion from scanning or lineage extraction (ADF/Synapse/Power BI). More assets discovered means more ingestion time and higher charges.
solution: Run BillingLogEvent Kusto query with MeterId filter to identify ingestion source (DataFactory vs scan). Check Monitor Links for asset ingestion volume. Reduce scan scope or disconnect external lineage sources if not needed.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Billing/Billing Troubleshooting Guideline

symptom: Need to query billing quantity (vCore-hrs) for Offline Tier jobs mapped to Resource Set meters via PAV2 Kusto cluster
solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-get-billing-quantity-offline-tier-jobs.md
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Billing/Get Billing quantity for Offline Tier jobs

symptom: ADF/Synapse copy or dataflow activity fails to report lineage to Purview with error 'Failed to report lineage to Catalog in 2 min'
rootCause: Private endpoint created in Purview with public network access disabled, but ADF/Synapse is using Azure IR (AutoResolvedIntegrationRuntime) which connects via public network only.
solution: 1) Use Managed Vnet Integration Runtime instead of Azure IR for copy/dataflow activities. 2) Create managed private endpoints from ADF to Purview. 3) Ensure all private endpoints are approved in Purview networking settings.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Data Map/ADF or Synapse lineage - Timeout out Status

symptom: Assets or classifications missing after scan in Purview Data Map. Scan completes successfully but certain assets do not appear in catalog.
rootCause: Customer previously modified the asset (name, field classification, or description), making the entire schema definitive (confidence=1). Per Apache Atlas Behavior section 2.5.3, Data Scan can never change definitive schemas.
solution: This is by design. Verify if customer modified the asset by checking the asset FQN with SME. Once a user modifies an asset, scan will not override it. Customer needs to revert manual changes if they want scan to update the asset.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Data Map/Asset Schema is missing or incorrect/Missing Assets

symptom: Assets missing after scan. Scan OpInfo shows assets were emitted, but they do not appear in the catalog. OfflineTierWarmPathAgentLogs shows "0 resources successfully imported, N resources failed while importing".
rootCause: Ingest request failed due to operation timeout, transient network failure, or concurrent relationship conflicts (HTTP 409) during bulk entity ingestion to the catalog.
solution: Check ingest status: OfflineTierWarmPathAgentLogs | where TraceId contains "{traceId}" | where Message contains "resources processed". Then check OnlineTierWebRequests | where RequestId == "{traceId}" | where RequestUrl == "/api/atlas/v2/entity/bulk" for error details. Check OnlineTierDetails with ERROR level filter for detailed stack traces. Contact SME Charles Shen for deep investigation.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Data Map/Asset Schema is missing or incorrect/Missing Assets

symptom: Assets or classifications missing. Scan emitted assets correctly (OpInfo confirms), but assets not found in Hot Tier ingestion logs.
rootCause: Assets or classifications were lost/dropped in transit between scan emission and Hot Tier (HT) ingestion pipeline.
solution: Get traceId from scan run id (activityId + pipelineId). Check OnlineTierDetails | where RequestId == "{traceId}" for HT payload. Compare scan OpInfo payload with HT received payload to identify dropped items. Filter by asset keyword: OnlineTierDetails | where RequestId == "{traceId}" and Msg has "<keyword>". If no HT logs found, issue is between scan and HT pipeline.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Data Map/Asset Schema is missing or incorrect/Missing Assets

symptom: Assets or classifications missing after scan. Need to verify if scan actually emitted the entities.
rootCause: Scan did not emit the assets or classifications. Asset has no schema (Schema:[]) in scan logs, meaning no detection occurred.
solution: Check scan logs: DataScanAgentEvent | where ScanResultId == "<ScanRunId>" | where Message contains "OpInfo" | where Message contains "<URI>" | where Message !contains "Schema":[]". No schema = no detection. For multi-cloud resources, use MultiCloudIRLog + MultiCloudDataScanLog join query with PipelineId. Also try browser refresh (F5) to rule out stale cache.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Data Map/Asset Schema is missing or incorrect/Missing Assets

symptom: Assets or classifications missing after scan completes in Microsoft Purview Data Map; entities not appearing in catalog
rootCause: Scan did not emit the assets/classifications — entity has empty schema (Schema:[]) in scan logs, meaning the scanner failed to detect them
solution: Check DataScanAgentEvent with ScanResultId and filter for OpInfo/Entity info containing the asset URI; verify schema is not empty. For multi-cloud, join MultiCloudIRLog with MultiCloudDataScanLog using PipelineId
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Data Map/Asset Schema is missing or incorrect/Missing Assets

symptom: Assets or classifications missing after scan — scan emitted correctly but entities not in catalog
rootCause: Assets were lost/dropped between scan emission and Hot Tier ingestion; payload was not delivered to OnlineTierDetails
solution: Get traceId from scan run (activityId + pipelineId). Check OnlineTierDetails with RequestId=traceId and filter by asset keyword. Compare payload with scan OpInfo to identify where assets were dropped
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Data Map/Asset Schema is missing or incorrect/Missing Assets

symptom: Asset import failure: 0 resources processed, 0 resources successfully imported, N resources failed while importing in OfflineTierWarmPathAgentLogs
rootCause: Ingest request to Data Map failed due to operation timeout, transient network failure, or CatalogServiceException (java.util.concurrent.CancellationException)
solution: Check OfflineTierWarmPathAgentLogs with traceId for failure count; then OnlineTierWebRequests for /api/atlas/v2/entity/bulk errors; filter OnlineTierDetails Level=ERROR for details. Contact SME Charles Shen for deep investigation
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Data Map/Asset Schema is missing or incorrect/Missing Assets

symptom: Scan does not update asset schema or classifications even though scan data is correct; previously scanned asset no longer reflects latest scan results
rootCause: Customer previously modified the asset (name, classification, or description), making the schema definitive (confidence=1). Per Apache Atlas behavior section 2.5.3, Data Scan can never override user-modified assets
solution: This is by design. Inform customer that once any schema change is made manually, scan cannot override it. To verify, check the asset FQN with SME Charles Shen to confirm user modification history
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Data Map/Asset Schema is missing or incorrect/Missing Assets

symptom: Missing assets in Purview Data Map — comprehensive diagnostic guide covering scan emission, hot tier delivery, ingest failures, customer modifications, and deletion scenarios
solution: See troubleshooting guide: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-missing-assets-diagnostic.md
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Data Map/Asset Schema is missing or incorrect/Missing Assets

symptom: Classification is missing or incorrect for assets in a Resource Set. Schema not updated after scan.
rootCause: Advanced Resource Sets (ARS) is toggled off. Without ARS enabled, schema will not be updated during in-ingestion for resource sets.
solution: Check ARS status via: ScanningLog | where ScanResultId == "<id>" | where Message contains "CustomProperties" - if result is not "Advanced", ARS is off. Enable ARS in Purview account Overview > Advanced Resource Sets toggle. Note: schema update takes ~12 hours after scan completion.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Data Map/Resource Set/Classification is missing or incorrect in Resource Set

symptom: System classification not applied to columns in Resource Set despite scan completing successfully.
rootCause: System classification rules require at least 8 distinct values per column. If column has fewer than 8 distinct values, classification will not be applied. Also, if SamplingLabel shows "basicinfo" instead of "classify", no classification is applied.
solution: Check classification prerequisites at https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/purview/concept-best-practices-classification. Verify ConvertedEntities: DataScanAgentLinuxEvent | where ScanResultId == "<id>" | where Message contains "ConvertedEntities" — check samplingLabel is "classify" not "basicinfo". Also check for low confidence classifications being dropped (by design).
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Data Map/Resource Set/Classification is missing or incorrect in Resource Set

symptom: Resource Set details, pattern rules, and aggregation are not visible in Purview Governance Portal.
rootCause: Advanced Resource Set feature must be explicitly enabled by customer. For accounts created on or after Aug 20th 2022, ARS is opt-in. Without enabling it, resource set properties (URI, count etc.) and custom pattern rules are not visible.
solution: Turn on "Advanced Resource Set" toggle in Purview account settings. Note: ARS incurs additional compute processing charges. Pricing varies by region. Refer to https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/pricing/details/azure-purview/.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Data Map/Resource Set/Resource Set Changes

symptom: Schema is missing or incorrect in Resource Set after scan. Resource Set asset shows wrong or no columns.
rootCause: Multiple possible causes: (1) ARS not enabled, (2) Scan agent encountered errors like HTTP 403 or unsupported file types, (3) Ingestion service failed to process RS payload, (4) Files in RS folder have inconsistent schemas across files causing confusion.
solution: Step-by-step diagnosis: (1) Verify ARS is on. (2) Check scan agent errors: DataScanAgentLinuxEvent (Azure IR) or TraceGatewayLocalEventLog (SHIR) filtered by ScanResultId with error keywords. (3) Check ConvertedEntities log for the asset FQN. (4) Verify ingestion logs: OnlineTierIngestionDetails with IngestionJobId. (5) Check Data Map service: OnlineTierWebRequests/OnlineTierDetails with RequestId. (6) Verify offline tier notifications. Escalate to respective PG at each step if failures found.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Data Map/Resource Set/Schema is missing or incorrect in Resource Set

symptom: Scan fails to read file schema with error about unsupported Parquet column type (e.g., non-primitive type in column). Schema missing for scanned asset.
rootCause: The scanned file (e.g., Parquet) contains columns with non-primitive or unsupported data types that the scan connector cannot process.
solution: Check scan agent error logs: DataScanAgentLinuxEvent | where ScanResultId == "<id>" | where Message contains "failed" or Message contains "Unsupported". If error is from connector (Source in connectors.*), escalate to connector PG. Customer may need to convert unsupported column types to supported types.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Data Map/Resource Set/Schema is missing or incorrect in Resource Set

symptom: Error Code 15602 "Stale Policy Update. New version already exists, please refresh" when using PUT API to update collection role assignments in Purview.
rootCause: The policy version in the request body is outdated. Policy version increments with each PUT call, so using a stale version number causes a conflict.
solution: Before each PUT call to update metadata policy, first GET the latest policy to obtain the current version number. Use the latest version in the PUT request body. The version field changes every time a PUT call is made.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Data Map/SDK and API/ADD USERS TO COLLECTIONS

symptom: HTTP 404 "Collection {name} not found" (Error Code 6000) when calling Purview Metadata Policy API for a sub-collection.
rootCause: For sub-collections (not root collection), the API requires the collection ID (found in the URL) rather than the display name as the collectionName parameter.
solution: Use the collection ID from the URL (e.g., the 6-char random string) instead of the collection display name in the API call: GET {Endpoint}/policyStore/metadataPolicies?collectionName={collectionId}&api-version=2021-07-01-preview
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Data Map/SDK and API/ADD USERS TO COLLECTIONS

symptom: After enabling new schema feature for delimited/parquet files in Purview, user-defined content (classifications, glossary terms, descriptions, data types) are missing from updated asset schema columns.
rootCause: New feature changes schema structure: nested JSON displayed in separate panel, virtual column :csv removed, column order honored. Existing user-defined annotations on old schema columns are not automatically migrated to the new schema structure.
solution: Run the Java migration program azurepurviewschemamigration_v1.jar provided by Microsoft Support. Prerequisites: (1) Run full scan with scope covering affected assets, (2) Collect subscription ID, Purview account name, data source name, scan name. Command: java -jar azurepurviewschemamigration_v1.jar -account:{name} -datasourcename:{ds} -scanName:{scan}. Applicable only for delimited files (CSV/PSV/SSV/TSV) and parquet files.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Data Map/SDK and API/Migrating User Defined Content for Asset Schema

symptom: Azure Data Share (ADS) metrics alert rule created but notification not fired when condition is met (e.g., Received Share Failed Snapshots alert).
rootCause: Either the ADS metrics are not displayed correctly (platform issue), or the Azure Monitor alert rule configuration is not triggering despite correct metric values.
solution: Check if ADS metrics are displayed correctly in Metrics blade. If metrics display is wrong, raise an AVA and involve SME. If metrics display is correct but alert did not fire, create a collab ticket to Azure Monitoring support team to investigate why the alert was not triggered.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Data Share/Azure Data Share (ADS)/ADS metrics alert not fired

symptom: Error generating bearer token for Purview REST API when using app registration with wrong account type selected.
rootCause: During app registration for Purview REST API access, the wrong supported account type was selected. Must choose "Accounts in this organizational directory only (Single tenant)".
solution: When registering the application in Azure AD for Purview REST API, select "Accounts in this organizational directory only (Single tenant)" as the supported account type. Follow setup guide: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/purview/tutorial-using-rest-apis

solution: See operational guide: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-configure-vscode-rest-api.md

symptom: Cannot delete assets via REST API — returns HTTP 408 (timeout), 429 (throttling), or 500+ (server error) when performing bulk delete operations
rootCause: Too many simultaneous delete requests (over 1000) causing API throttling or timeout. Alternatively, assets have many dependent/related assets requiring cascade delete.
solution: For >1000 requests: use Discovery Query API (https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/rest/api/purview/catalogdataplane/discovery/query) with 1000 response limit per call to batch deletions. For dependent assets: use Delete Hierarchy API (/api/entity/deleteHierarchy) which performs cascade delete. Check request count via: GatewayEvent | where AccountId == "<account-id>" | where Message contains "Delete" | summarize count() by bin(TIMESTAMP, 1d), Status. Also check ARM/ASC for subscription health issues.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Data Map/SDK and API/Deleting assets From the UI or using REST API

symptom: Parent asset reappears in Purview catalog the next day after deleting it from the UI
rootCause: Purview UI does not perform cascade delete. When a parent asset is deleted but child assets remain, the parent is automatically recreated during the next ingestion cycle.
solution: Delete all child assets first before deleting the parent, or use the Delete Hierarchy REST API (/api/entity/deleteHierarchy) which performs cascade delete. UI also does not support bulk deletion — use REST API for that: DELETE https://{catalog_endpoint}/api/atlas/v2/entity/guid/{guid}
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Data Map/SDK and API/Deleting assets From the UI or using REST API

symptom: Manually edited asset in Purview (description, classification, glossary term) is not updated by subsequent scan runs
rootCause: By design — once an asset is manually edited, subsequent scans will not overwrite manual changes. However, scans can still add new classifications and metadata found during scanning.
solution: Inform customer this is by design. Scans preserve manually-set description, glossary term, and classification but will add new classifications discovered. Full manual override capability is planned for future releases.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Data Map/SDK and API/Deleting assets From the UI or using REST API

solution: See operational guide: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-discontinue-postman.md
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Data Map/SDK and API/Discontinue use of Postman

symptom: Purview Search/Discovery API startswith operator does not work as expected — does not always match from the first character of qualifiedName or name
rootCause: The startswith operator is designed for sentence-like attributes (e.g., description) where it matches words starting with given chars anywhere in the sentence. For technical attributes like qualifiedName, it may not match from the first character consistently.
solution: Use the new "prefix" operator instead of "startswith" — it always matches from the first character. Example: {"filter": {"attributeName": "qualifiedName", "operator": "prefix", "attributeValue": "test_blob"}}. Alternatively, use gt/lt range filter: filter qualifiedName >= "testfqdn" and < "testfqdo" (increment last char in ASCII).
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Data Map/SDK and API/Filter Results Returned using REST API

symptom: Purview Search API contains operator returns too many irrelevant results or misses expected assets — case-insensitive, splits by symbol, matches at word-level not partial word, matches any word not all words
rootCause: By design: contains operator splits text by symbols, matches at word-level (not partial), and uses OR logic (any word matches). This differs from exact substring matching that customers typically expect.
solution: Use name-field scoped syntax instead: {"keywords": "name:\"UAT\""} — this is closer to customer expectations (splits by symbol, matches all words, preserves ordering). Note: partial word matching is not supported to avoid irrelevant results. The system also splits long words by camelCase and symbol boundaries for sub-word matching.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Data Map/SDK and API/Filter Results Returned using REST API

symptom: How to get all asset detail information from Query API, batch update assets, or batch delete assets — operational guide for pagination, secondary GUIDs lookup, and scrolling patterns
solution: See operational guide: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-query-api-asset-details-batch.md
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Data Map/SDK and API/How can I get all asset detail information from Query API

symptom: Cannot retrieve more than 100,000 assets via Purview REST API Search/Discovery pagination — offset limit is 100,000
rootCause: Product restriction prevents users from accessing more than 100K assets through standard pagination (offset-based). The maximum offset supported is 100,000.
solution: Use scrolling call pattern: sort by id (orderby: [{"id": "asc"}]), keep offset=0, and use gt filter on id with the last result's id from previous page. Example: {"filter": {"id": {"operator": "gt", "value": "<last-id>"}}, "orderby": [{"id": "asc"}], "limit": 200}. Repeat until no more results.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Data Map/SDK and API/How to fetch more than 100000 assets via REST API

symptom: Deleting an asset via REST API times out even with fewer than 1000 requests, because the asset has many dependent or related assets that also need deletion.
rootCause: The asset has numerous dependent/related child assets. Standard single-entity delete cannot handle the cascade and times out.
solution: Use the Delete Hierarchy API: DELETE /api/entity/deleteHierarchy?guid={parentGuid}. This async operation handles cascade deletion. Monitor progress via GET /api/entity/deleteHierarchy/operation/{operationId}. See: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-delete-hierarchy-operation.md
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Data Map/SDK and API/Deleting assets From the UI or using REST API

symptom: Cannot delete a custom typedef (e.g., business metadata typedef) in Purview even though it is no longer needed.
rootCause: Delete typedef is inherently not supported by Apache Atlas. Purview inherits this limitation.
solution: Deletion only works if the typedef is not assigned to any assets. Remove all assignments first, then attempt deletion. If still fails, this is a known Atlas limitation with no workaround. Feature is on the Purview roadmap.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Data Map/SDK and API/Deleting assets From the UI or using REST API

symptom: Search/Discovery Query API does not support specifying which attributes to return in the response. All default attributes are returned regardless.
rootCause: The Search API does not natively support selecting specific return attributes, but an undocumented selectionList parameter exists.
solution: Add selectionList to the request body to specify desired return fields: [id, qualifiedName, name, description, entityType, classification, term, label, attributes.field, attributes.stringValue, attributes.int64Value].
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Data Map/SDK and API/Filter Results Returned using REST API

symptom: Azure Data Share (ADS) snapshot or dataset operation fails with authentication/permission error on SQL-based datasource (Azure SQL DB, Synapse)
rootCause: ADS managed identity lacks required database roles. Provider needs db_datareader; receiver needs db_datareader, db_datawriter, db_ddladmin. Adding ADS MI to security group is NOT supported for SQL datasources.
solution: Verify ADS MI roles via SQL query on sys.database_principals. Grant missing roles directly to ADS MI (not via security group).
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Data Share/Azure Data Share (ADS)/authentication failure

symptom: Azure Data Share (ADS) operation fails with permission error on storage account or data lake
rootCause: ADS MI missing RBAC roles. Provider MI needs Storage Blob Data Reader; consumer MI needs Storage Blob Data Contributor.
solution: Grant Storage Blob Data Reader for provider MI, Storage Blob Data Contributor for consumer MI on storage account IAM.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Data Share/Azure Data Share (ADS)/authentication failure

symptom: Error adding DataSet in Azure Data Share when storage uses selected networks — Failed to retrieve containers, not authorized
rootCause: Storage firewall blocks client IP from enumerating containers.
solution: Add client IP to storage Firewall settings. Retry adding DataSet.

symptom: Failed to attach share in Purview Data Sharing
rootCause: (1) Path includes container created outside Purview Data Sharing - only containers created during asset mapping are allowed. (2) Container is not new or data link container. (3) Source and target storage account are the same (not supported). (4) Exceeding sharing limits (source: 20 targets max, target: 100 sources max).
solution: Ensure container was created during asset mapping (new or data link container). Use different source and target storage accounts. Contact Support for limit increase if needed. See: https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/purview/how-to-receive-share
