rootCause: Cross-tenant sharing: provider is in a different tenant or different collection. In this case, only the provider's tenant name is displayed instead of full lineage.
solution: This is expected behavior for cross-tenant sharing. Only the provider's tenant name will be visible when the provider is in a different collection.

rootCause: (1) Recipient has not yet mapped the share invite. (2) Cross-tenant sharing or recipient is in a different collection - only the recipient's tenant name is visible.
solution: Wait for the recipient to map the share invite. For cross-tenant sharing, only the tenant name will be visible (expected behavior).

rootCause: (1) Data propagation delay - data may take a few minutes to appear after attach or disappear after delete. (2) Using storage API version prior to February 2020, which is not supported for accessing shared data.
solution: Wait a few minutes for data propagation. Ensure you are using storage API version February 2020 or later. Use latest version of storage SDK, PowerShell, CLI, and Azure Storage Explorer.

symptom: Creating user for Purview Managed Identity in Azure SQL Database fails with error: This principal type is not supported in Azure SQL Database
rootCause: Azure SQL Server unable to perform AzureAD lookup with just the Purview MSI name using standard CREATE USER FROM EXTERNAL PROVIDER syntax.
solution: Use the OBJECT_ID syntax: CREATE USER [AzureADUser] FROM EXTERNAL PROVIDER WITH OBJECT_ID = '<object_id>'. Find the object_id by searching in Entra ID with the Purview managed identity name. Custom alias can be used. If customer requests RCA, raise task to Azure SQL server support.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Data Sources/Fail to add Purview SAMI on Azure SQL

symptom: Purview scan completes with metadata read errors: System Error ReadData (invalid datetime Ticks) or Operation Failed ReadData (non-primitive Parquet type not supported)
rootCause: DataScan agent fails to read metadata from specific files. Common causes: (1) Parquet file contains datetime column with Ticks value outside valid range. (2) Parquet file uses non-primitive/complex data types not yet supported by Purview scanner. (3) Permissions/access issues, corrupt files, connectivity issues, or resource constraints.
solution: Review scan logs for specific error codes. Verify access permissions. Test data source connectivity manually. Check supported file types per Microsoft Purview Data Map documentation. For unsupported Parquet complex types: feature in private preview to address this limitation. Increase resource allocation if resource-constrained.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Data Sources/Handling Metadata Read Issues in Microsoft Purview Scans

symptom: Purview scan fails with System Error or Operation Failed ReadData on Parquet files — invalid datetime Ticks value or unsupported non-primitive type
rootCause: Parquet files contain datetime values outside valid range (Ticks) or complex data types not yet supported by Purview scanner
solution: Review scan logs; verify data source permissions; check supported file types at MS docs; for complex Parquet types — feature in private preview, contact Product Team
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Data Sources/Handling Metadata Read Issues in Microsoft Purview Scans

symptom: Purview scan ReadData error: Parquet file contains non-primitive type not yet supported — complex Parquet data types cause scan failure
rootCause: Purview does not support Parquet files containing complex data types (nested structs, arrays, maps)
solution: Feature in private preview to support complex Parquet types; contact Product Team to join preview; no public ETA for GA
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Data Sources/Handling Metadata Read Issues in Microsoft Purview Scans

symptom: Scan save fails with error: The version of the linkedservice is not supported — during SHIR scan configuration
rootCause: LinkedService API failure during scan save process; SHIR version compatibility issue
solution: Install private build of SHIR (whitelisted for tenant); uninstall old SHIR with keep-data or use new VM; create new scans with new SHIR. Fix only for Snowflake, MySQL, Postgres. Contact pemar/yazanabusair for other sources.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Data Sources/Linked Service Purview Issue

symptom: Missing schema (table/column) details for Power BI dataset after scanning in Purview — no columns or tables shown
rootCause: Power BI dataset not refreshed or republished (prerequisite for scanner API); unsupported model type; browser cache issues
solution: 1) Refresh/republish PBI dataset and get refresh RAID from PBI UI; 2) Check schemaRetrievalError for unsupported model; 3) Clear browser cache; 4) Use Kusto DataScanAgentLinuxEvent to check if schema payload is empty (HasNoSchemaSet=true); 5) If still missing after refresh, work with PBI CSS before raising ICM
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Data Sources/PowerBI - Missing schema(table - column) details for PowerBI dataset after scanning in Purview

symptom: Purview scan does not pick up new columns added to Synapse views or tables — column mismatch between Synapse and Purview
rootCause: Binding errors: Synapse view or function becomes outdated due to changes in underlying objects it depends on (e.g. delta log path issues)
solution: Check if customer changed view/function definition; run Kusto query on DataScanAgentEvent to look for binding errors message; customer may need to recreate/refresh the view to fix binding
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Data Sources/Purview scans not picking up new columns in Synapse views

symptom: Scan test connection fails with Error 20500: Failed to access the provided secret in Azure key vault — Account Key authentication via Key Vault
rootCause: Key Vault Access Policy specifies both objectId and applicationId; non-OBO auth flow should not have applicationId in the access policy
solution: In Key Vault Access Policy, only provide Purview MSI Object ID in Select principal; do NOT add Application ID in Authorized application details

symptom: Oracle scan fails with Error 3804: Unable to access the data source with the given credentials and/or configurations
rootCause: Generic error — actual root cause hidden in SHIR event viewer logs; common causes include Oracle driver issues, credential errors, incorrect Oracle Service name in TNS Listener config
solution: Check SHIR Event Viewer > Application and Service Logs > Connectors Integration Runtime; scroll through ERROR lines then check Information lines below for real error message (e.g. missing JDBC driver). Clear event log before rerunning scan to reduce noise.

symptom: Teradata or Oracle scan completes with no assets; SHIR logs show FATAL: The Model is not consistent (MITI exception)
rootCause: Inconsistent model caused by stored procedure lineage generation in MITI bridge
solution: Option A (if SP lineage needed): Install private SHIR (irWithImportBackup.msi), rerun scan, collect mitibackuplogfortroubleshooting + SHIR logs, file ICM to 3P Data Sources. Option B (if SP lineage not needed): Change Stored Procedure Details to Signature only and restart scan (official SHIR only).
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Data Sources/Third party/3P Data Sources - Oracle || Teradata - Model is not consistent

symptom: SAP ECC scan fails with internal server error or Error 3818 Connector Exception — JRE out of memory on SHIR machine
rootCause: SHIR machine has insufficient RAM for SAP ECC scanning; JRE exhausted memory or assigned memory exceeds spec; SAP ECC requires at least 128GB RAM
solution: Scale up SHIR machine to at least 128GB RAM; use Kusto TraceGatewayLocalEventLog queries to verify machine spec (MIMB_I0032), memory assignment (MIMB_I0031), and JRE exception details
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Data Sources/Third party/3P Data Sources - SAP ECC scan failure

symptom: Snowflake scan fails or stuck in queue; SHIR logs show OCSP certificate revocation warning using fail-open connect
rootCause: OCSP-based certificate revocation checking fails for Snowflake HTTPS endpoint; SHIR cannot obtain valid OCSP response from CA responder
solution: Add insecureMode=true in JDBC URL parameters in Snowflake connection (safe to enable); alternatively check with Snowflake team on certificates or add proxy server details if connection via proxy is required
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Data Sources/Third party/3P Data Sources - Unable to scan Snowflake source

rootCause: Tenant permission for OneLake external access not enabled; causes IncomingOperationUntrusted error during scan
solution: Enable tenant setting: Users can access data stored in OneLake with apps external to Fabric; then rerun the scan

symptom: MongoDB scan fails with Error 3818 Connector Exception: No available collections to import. Underlying error: Cannot start Java Virtual Machine — CreateProcess error=206, The filename or extension is too long
rootCause: Known bug in Windows SHIR where the Java CLASSPATH becomes too long for the Windows CreateProcess API limit (error 206). JVM is installed and PATH is correct but the CLASSPATH string exceeds the OS character limit.
solution: Use Kubernetes (K8s) SHIR instead of Windows SHIR to run the scan. PG confirmed this is a known bug with no ETA for fix on Windows SHIR.

symptom: Test connection fails with error '(5522) Test connection failed for lineage scan' or '(5521)' when enabling Lineage extraction for Azure SQL DB scan in Purview
rootCause: Azure SQL logical server has 'Allow Azure services and resources to access this server' set to No, or public network access is disabled — lineage extraction requires this setting enabled
solution: Set 'Allow Azure services and resources to access this server' to Yes on Azure SQL DB firewall. Lineage extraction does not support servers that disable public access. Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/purview/register-scan-azure-sql-database#known-limitations

symptom: Managed VNET creation appears successful but IR and private endpoints are not established. IR creation fails silently due to VNET not fully provisioned.
rootCause: Race condition in backend: system attempts to create IR and private endpoints immediately after VNET creation, before the Managed VNET is fully provisioned. Also, max 5 Managed VNETs per account (not visible in UI).
solution: 1) Get region and name of the created Managed VNET. 2) Create IR via REST API (Integration Runtimes - Create Or Replace). 3) Create private endpoints via UI under Integration Runtime > Source Management. 4) Approve PEs manually. 5) Wait ~20 minutes for IR to be ready. Check VNET quota via REST API (Managed Virtual Networks - List By Account).
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Scan/Managed Vnet IR/Managed VNET Creation Fails

symptom: Scan fails with 'Name or service not known' or connection errors when using Managed Vnet IR with Private Endpoints. DataScanAgentLinuxEvent logs show HttpRequestException for storage endpoint.
rootCause: Private endpoints are disconnected or in deleted state. Customer may have removed PE from the data source without realizing it, causing the Managed Vnet IR to lose connectivity to the storage/data source endpoint.
solution: 1) Use Kusto to extract jobId from ScanningLog, then check DataScanAgentLinuxEvent for networking errors. 2) Ask customer to check PE status in Azure portal (Networking > Private endpoint connections). 3) If PE state is 'Disconnected' or 'Deleted', recreate the private endpoint and retry the scan.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Scan/Managed Vnet IR/Scan failure in Managed Vnet IR - Networking Issues

symptom: On-prem SQL or Azure Synapse dedicated pool scan completes with exceptions — test connection succeeds but scan fails with timeout error: Timeout expired, the timeout period elapsed prior to completion of the operation
rootCause: Synapse server does not have enough capacity to run the query, causing it to wait for resources and timeout after 30 minutes. Server-side resource exhaustion during scan execution.
solution: Collect scan result logs and Kusto logs (DataScanAgentLinuxEvent for Azure IR or TraceGatewayLocalEventLog for SHIR). Check if errors are SQL server-side. If Synapse capacity issue, summarize symptom/error/timestamp and open collab to SQL team. If SQL confirms client-side (Purview) issue, raise AVA to engage Datasources PG.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Scan/On-Prem SQL or Synapse SQL scan completed with exceptions

symptom: PBI (Power BI) scan causes external assets such as SQL server tables to be ingested into root collection instead of the expected subcollection
rootCause: By design — external assets (non-PBI assets) detected during Power BI scan are stored in the root collection. PBI scan only organizes PBI-native assets into subcollections.
solution: Workaround 1: Set up the respective external data source (e.g., SQL Server) and run a separate scan on it — this moves the assets from root collection to target subcollections. Workaround 2: Use REST APIs (Discovery Query API to list assets + Move Entities To Collection API) to programmatically move assets to target subcollection.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Scan/PBI Scan - Assets are getting ingested to root collection instead of correct collection

symptom: SHIR scan fails with error 9011: Could not establish trust relationship for the SSL/TLS secure channel. Remote certificate is invalid according to the validation procedure. Occurs when using Amazon S3 linked service.
rootCause: Target data source certificate is not trusted by the SHIR machine. The certificate is missing from the Trusted Root Certification Authorities store on the SHIR server.
solution: 1) Run MMC on SHIR machine → Add Certificates snap-in (Computer account). 2) Check Trusted Root Certification Authorities. 3) Download target data source certificate and import it to Trusted Root CA. For DigiCert: download from http://cacerts.digicert.com/DigiCertGlobalRootG2.crt
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Scan/Self-Hosted IR in Windows/Could not establish trust relationship for the TLS secure channel

symptom: Self-Hosted Integration Runtime (SHIR) fails to register on new on-prem machine. Event Viewer shows: Refresh service token from ADF service failed, Authentication failed because the remote party has closed the transport stream.
rootCause: Firewall blocking HTTPS (TLS) traffic to Purview cloud endpoint (*.frontend.clouddatahub.net). Network trace shows TCP handshake succeeds but TLS Hello gets no ACK from server, indicating firewall blocks TLS packets.
solution: 1) Check Event Viewer → Application and Service Log → Integration Runtime for Critical/Error. 2) Capture network trace and look for TLS Hello without ACK pattern. 3) Whitelist *.frontend.clouddatahub.net for HTTPS connection in firewall. Note: whitelisting TCP alone is NOT sufficient - HTTPS must be explicitly allowed.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Scan/Self-Hosted IR in Windows/Fail to register SHIR service

symptom: SHIR scan fails with 403 Forbidden or Internal Server Error after enabling Private Endpoint and disabling public network access in Purview and data source.
rootCause: DNS resolution for PE endpoints still resolves to public IP addresses instead of private IPs. Connections from SHIR go via public network which is disabled. Also managed storage account may return 403 if customer modified ICM/Networking settings.
solution: 1) Check DNS resolution for all PE endpoints using nslookup/Test-NetConnection. 2) If FQDN resolves to public IP, update IP in DNS server or add private IP to host file as workaround. 3) For managed storage 403, open collab to Azure Blob team (PG has no Kusto for customer-sub storage). 4) If unresolved, collect Fiddler (HTTP) + Netmon (TCP, run as admin) traces and escalate via AVA.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Scan/Self-Hosted IR in Windows/How to troubleshoot PE issues in SHIR

symptom: Delete self-hosted or managed VNet integration runtime fails with 400 BadRequest: The document cannot be deleted since it is referenced by {resource-id}.
rootCause: Leaked linked services that were generated when creating Scan still reference the IR. Customer may have deleted Scan/DataSource on UX but linked services were not properly cleaned up.
solution: 1) Confirm with customer if they already changed to another IR or deleted the Scan. 2) If not yet changed, remove those references and retry deletion. 3) If already changed/deleted, escalate to PG via IcM to clean up leaked resources.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Scan/Self-Hosted IR in Windows/IR (integration runtime) cannot be deleted due to referenced by other resources

symptom: ADLS Gen2 scan via SHIR fails with error code 119: System error while attempting to launch datascan process. Portal shows Internal system error with correlationId.
rootCause: DNS resolution for managed queue resource private endpoint returns public IP instead of private IP. When ingestion private endpoints are configured but DNS forwarding for managed resources is not properly set up in Azure Private DNS zones, SHIR cannot reach managed storage through private network.
solution: 1) Run nslookup from SHIR machine for Purview account FQDN, managed storage blob and queue endpoints. 2) Verify FQDNs resolve to private IPs of ingestion private endpoints. 3) If resolving to public IP, update host file on SHIR VM with correct private IP mappings or fix DNS zones for managed resources. 4) Rerun scan after DNS correction.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Scan/Self-Hosted IR in Windows/Scanning with SHIR - DNS Resolution issues

symptom: SHIR test connection to data source fails with "The underlying connection was closed: An unexpected error occurred on a send"; network trace shows TCP retransmission during TLS handshake with managed storage account
rootCause: SHIR must connect to managed storage account before establishing connection to data source. On-prem network environment (firewall/security appliance) blocks HTTPS connection even though TCP handshake succeeds, causing TLS handshake to fail repeatedly
solution: 1. Run nslookup to verify DNS resolution to correct IP. 2. Run telnet <FQDN> <Port> to test connectivity. 3. If both pass, collect network trace (Netmon with admin privilege). 4. Use OpenSSL s_client to verify TLS connection to managed storage endpoint: s_client -connect <managed-storage>.blob.core.windows.net:443. 5. If TLS fails, engage customer network/security team to fix firewall settings for HTTPS traffic
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Scan/Self-Hosted IR in Windows/Test Connection to Data Source Failed

symptom: SHIR registration fails when proxy is enabled; error "Integration Runtime (Self-hosted) node has encountered an error during registration" because on-prem SHIR cannot connect to Purview cloud service endpoint through proxy
solution: See troubleshooting guide: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-shir-proxy-registration.md
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Scan/Self-Hosted IR in Windows/Unable to register SHIR with proxy

symptom: Snowflake scans may fail after November 2025 when Snowflake disables single-factor basic authentication for non-LEGACY_SERVICE users; Purview customers using Snowflake Data Map connector with basic auth will be affected
rootCause: Snowflake deprecated single-factor password authentication starting November 2025. Users without LEGACY_SERVICE type cannot use basic auth.
solution: 1. Transition to OAuth 2.0 (preferred) or Key Pair authentication. 2. As interim measure, alter existing Purview user to LEGACY_SERVICE type before end of October: ALTER USER <existing_purview_user_name> SET TYPE = LEGACY_SERVICE. 3. Note: creation of new LEGACY_SERVICE users will be blocked starting November 2025
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Scan/Snowflake Authentication update

symptom: Workflow approval via actionable email in Outlook shows 403 error.
rootCause: By-design: Approval via actionable emails in Outlook only supported for Purview accounts with public access.
solution: Click View item detail in the email and approve from the Purview portal. Ensure Purview account has public access if email approval is required.

symptom: Workflow approval from Outlook returns HTTP 404 error. Same request works from Purview portal.
rootCause: Tenant mismatch between Purview account tenant and the Outlook login tenant. Approval flow resolves Purview account within the Outlook login tenant.
solution: 1) Approve from Purview Portal (Workflows > Requests and approvals). 2) Ensure approver is logged into Outlook in the same tenant as the Purview account. Cross-tenant approval from Outlook is not supported.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Workflow/Workflow Approval from Outlook returns HTTP 404 Error

symptom: Purview service experiences slow response time and timeouts: browsing/clicking assets timeout, data map not loading, random reloading causing unresponsive pages.
rootCause: Network configuration issues causing Purview to timeout.
solution: 1) Review network configuration with networking teams. 2) Check Azure portal settings. 3) Monitor Purview service. 4) If unresolved, contact support to engage PG.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/AutoContent/Troubleshooting Slow Response Time and Timeouts in Microsoft Purview Service

symptom: AD RMS rights granted to a universal group containing domain global groups work intermittently. Some users get access, others do not, depending on which GC is contacted.
rootCause: Global Catalog servers replicate domain global/local groups but NOT their memberships. When AD RMS queries a GC from a different domain, nested domain global group members return null.
solution: Use universal groups with universal group members (not domain global groups). Or control which GCs RMS queries via registry: HKLM\Software\Microsoft\DRMS, Name: GC, Type: REG_SZ, Value: comma-delimited GC FQDNs from the domain that has the group memberships.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/AD RMS/Troubleshooting Scenarios: AD RMS/Scenario: AD Group Lookups not Working as Expected

symptom: AD RMS Service Connection Point (SCP) fails to register. Error when attempting to register SCP in AD RMS console.
rootCause: 1) Account is not an AD Enterprise Administrator. 2) SCP container object is missing in AD Configuration container under CN=RightsManagementServices.
solution: 1) Ensure AD RMS admin is member of Enterprise Admins group. 2) If SCP container missing, create it via ADSI Edit: connect to Configuration, navigate to CN=Services > CN=RightsManagementServices, create new container named SCP. 3) Register SCP via AD RMS MMC console.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/AD RMS/Troubleshooting Scenarios: AD RMS/Scenario: Failed to register the AD RMS SCP

symptom: AD RMS configuration fails with SQL error: The File DRMS_Config.mdf is compressed but does not reside in a read-only database or filegroup. The file must be decompressed.
rootCause: The folder containing SQL database files has NTFS compression enabled (Compress contents to save disk space).
solution: Uncheck 'Compress contents to save disk space' option on the SQL data folder. Also ensure installation account is domain user with local admin, SQL SA role, and AD Enterprise Admin for SCP registration.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/AD RMS/Troubleshooting Scenarios: AD RMS/Scenario: Installation Error after Confirmation step

symptom: Unable to change AD RMS Service account or password. Permission errors when attempting the change.
rootCause: Insufficient permissions or admin share disabled. Account running wizard must be local admin, AD RMS Enterprise Admin, and SQL SysAdmin. New service account must NOT be database owner on RMS databases.
solution: 1) Ensure account is local admin, AD RMS Enterprise Admin member, and SQL SysAdmin. 2) New service account should NOT be DB Owner on RMS databases. 3) Check admin share accessibility (\\server\C$\inetpub\wwwroot\_wmcs\admin\). 4) Must connect to local server name, not cluster name. 5) Perform on each cluster server. 6) If still failing, collect Procmon log and escalate.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/AD RMS/Troubleshooting Scenarios: AD RMS/Scenario: Unable to change AD RMS Service account

symptom: AD RMS clients fail to connect after disabling TLS 1.0/1.1. MSIPC log shows Send Request Failed with status=12157.
rootCause: AD RMS is a .NET application that defaults to TLS 1.0. After disabling TLS 1.0/1.1, the server refuses TLS 1.2 connections because .NET StrongCrypto is not configured.
solution: 1) Set registry keys to enforce .NET StrongCrypto (TLS 1.2): SystemDefaultTlsVersions=1 and SchUseStrongCrypto=1 under HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\.NETFramework\v2.0.50727 and v4.0.30319 (and Wow6432Node on 64-bit). 2) Verify TLS 1.2 is enabled in SCHANNEL\Protocols. 3) Install .NET updates for TLS 1.2 support. 4) Restart AD RMS server.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/AD RMS/How To: AD RMS/How to: Configure AD RMS for TLS 1.2

solution: See guide: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-aip-keys-tpd-mapping.md
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/AD RMS/How To: AD RMS/How To: Mapping AIP keys to imported TPDs

symptom: Need to recover/rebuild AD RMS cluster from SQL database backups after complete server failure.
solution: See guide: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-adrms-database-restore.md
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/AD RMS/How To: AD RMS/How To: Restore AD RMS Databases

symptom: Users unable to apply AIP/MIP encryption to documents. Desktop apps fail to encrypt content. OCP error. Web apps and opening encrypted content work fine.
rootCause: AIP Service Onboarding Control Policy (OCP) is configured, restricting which users can encrypt. User may not have RMS license, may not be in the specified Entra security group, or app type may be restricted.
solution: 1) Check OCP: Get-AipServiceOnboardingControlPolicy | fl. 2) Verify user has RMS license if UseRmsUserLicense=true. 3) Verify user is in the SecurityGroupObjectId group. 4) Check Scope setting (All/MobileApp/WindowsApp). 5) To modify: Set-AipServiceOnboardingControlPolicy. Note: OCP does not affect opening encrypted content or web apps.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/AIP Service/How To: AipService/How To: Check AIP Service Configuration

symptom: AIP Service super user cannot decrypt content despite being configured. Super user feature not working as expected.
rootCause: Super user was configured with UPN but the service now requires the primary email (SMTP) address instead of UPN.
solution: Use primary email (SMTP) address when configuring super users, not UPN. Check with Get-AipServiceSuperUser. For group-based: Get-AipServiceSuperUserGroup. Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/information-protection/configure-super-users
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/AIP Service/How To: AipService/How To: Check AIP Service Configuration

symptom: Connect-AipService fails or access denied. User has Global Admin role via group membership but cannot connect to AIP Service.
rootCause: AIP Service PowerShell requires Global Admin role assigned directly to the user, not via group membership.
solution: 1) Assign GA role directly to user. 2) Alternative: use Add-AipServiceRoleBasedAdministrator to assign AIP Service GlobalAdministrator role to specific accounts/groups, eliminating Azure role dependency.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/AIP Service/How To: AipService/How To: Check AIP Service Configuration

symptom: Need reference for AIP Service PowerShell commands: templates, configuration, keys, super users, onboarding control.
solution: See guide: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-aip-service-ps-reference.md
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/AIP Service/How To: AipService/How To: AIP Service PS Commands

symptom: Assets deleted via Purview API still appear in the Purview portal. Clicking deleted assets shows no metadata and returns 404 error (ATLAS-404-00-005: Given instance guid is invalid/not found).
rootCause: Search store and underlying data store went out of sync. Large volume of delete requests in short duration caused 429 throttling and race conditions, leaving stale entries in the search store.
solution: As immediate mitigation, make a GET request to each deleted asset from the Purview Portal — this triggers automatic sync between search store and data store. Note: sync only triggers if request comes from Purview Portal.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/UX/Deleted assets showing up on the Purview portal

symptom: Purview Portal displays in a foreign language even after user switches to English in MyAccount settings.
rootCause: Purview inherits language settings from Microsoft 365, not from MyAccount directly. This is by design and not a Purview issue.
solution: Redirect to Microsoft 365/Compliance team. Direct user to change display language in M365 settings: https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/topic/change-your-display-language-and-time-zone-in-microsoft-365-for-business-6f238bff-5252-441e-b32b-655d5d85d15b
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/UX/Purview Portal Showing in Foreign Language

symptom: Need to migrate software CSP-based AD RMS key to Azure Key Vault for AIP BYOK integration.
solution: See guide: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-adrms-software-csp-key-migration.md
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/AD RMS/How To: AD RMS/How To: Software CSP based AD RMS key migration

symptom: Need to migrate AIP Service BYOK key from one Azure Key Vault to another (e.g., subscription migration).
solution: 1) Keep original BYOK key/subscription/AKV intact. 2) Import original BYOK key to new AKV. 3) Configure new AKV permissions for AIP. 4) Use Convert-AipServiceKeyToKeyVault to point key to new AKV. 5) Use Set-AipServiceKeyProperties to activate. 6) Use Use-AipServiceKeyVaultKey for new BYOK. Key identifier stays the same; only AKV URL changes. Rollback: point back to old AKV URL.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/AIP Service/How To: AipService/How To: Migrate AIP Service BYOK key vault keys

symptom: Event not showing in Activity Explorer, DLP alert missing, or label event not visible.
rootCause: Multiple possible causes: 1) Event not in Audit log (source app issue). 2) Filters hiding the event. 3) Admin Unit restricting visibility. 4) UI display issue (data present in PowerShell but not UI).
solution: 1) Check Audit log via Search-UnifiedAuditLog. 2) Verify Activity Explorer filters and date range. 3) Check Admin Unit assignments - AU users only see AU-scoped events. 4) Download via Export-ActivityExplorerData PowerShell. 5) If present in PS but not UI, it is a display issue - escalate.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Activity Explorer/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Activity Explorer/Scenario: Event not showing in Activity Explorer

symptom: Activity Explorer shows no events or limited events for users with Admin Unit (AU) assignments.
rootCause: By-design: Users with Admin Unit only see events for users in their assigned AU. Policies assigned to AU further restrict visibility.
solution: This is by design. To see all events, user needs Global Admin role or AU configuration change. Check if user has AU and if the policy is assigned to an AU.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Activity Explorer/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Activity Explorer/Scenario: Event not showing in Activity Explorer

solution: See guide: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-aip-track-and-revoke.md

solution: See guide: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-aip-dsr-delete-process.md

symptom: Customer needs guidance on AIP tenant migration scenarios (tenant-to-tenant, AD RMS to AIP).
solution: See guide: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-aip-tenant-migrations.md
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/AIP Service/How To: AipService/How to: AIP Service Tenant Migration

symptom: After migrating from AD RMS to AIP, newer Office clients using MIP SDK fail licensing redirection; users cannot open previously RMS-protected content.
rootCause: Newer Office clients use MIP SDK instead of MSIPC, which has known issues with licensing redirection after AD RMS to AIP migration.
solution: Create registry key: HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Office\16.0\Common\DRM, REG_DWORD PreferredRmsPackage=1 to force fallback to MSIPC.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/AIP Service/How To: AipService/How to: AIP Service Tenant Migration

symptom: After partial tenant migration (users only, email domain retained in old tenant), migrated users cannot decrypt files/emails encrypted with ad-hoc protection (user-defined, Do Not Forward, Encrypt Only).
rootCause: Ad-hoc protection is tied to the user's original email address; migrated users have new email addresses in the new tenant and cannot match the original protection recipient.
solution: Decrypt files/mails from the old tenant using an AIP Superuser account and share unprotected copies with migrated users. For label-based protection, edit encryption labels in old tenant to add the new tenant domain.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/AIP Service/How To: AipService/How to: AIP Service Tenant Migration

symptom: After complete tenant migration (users + DNS domains + AIP config), users cannot open/decrypt AIP-protected files from the old tenant.
rootCause: Missing one or more migration prerequisites: AIP keys not imported to new tenant, DNS domain not verified, user ProxyAddress missing original email, or licensing redirection not configured on clients.
solution: Verify: 1) Get-AIPServiceKeys shows old keys in new tenant, 2) old DNS domain is verified in new tenant, 3) migrated users ProxyAddress contains original email, 4) DNS/Registry licensing redirection points to old AIP tenant URL. Obtain old AIP URL via Get-AIPServiceConfiguration or from protected document metadata in Notepad.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/AIP Service/How To: AipService/How to: AIP Service Tenant Migration

symptom: Import-AipServiceTpd fails with password error when importing TPD (Trusted Publishing Domain) to AIP Service.
rootCause: Incorrect password provided for the TPD encryption during import.
solution: Re-export the TPD with a new known password from the source (AD RMS or other tenant) and retry the import with the new password. Run Import-AipServiceTpd with -Verbose for detailed error.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/AIP Service/Troubleshooting Scenarios: AipService/Scenario: Unable to Import TPD to AIP Service

symptom: Import-AipServiceTpd fails when importing a TPD that was already imported to another AIP tenant.
rootCause: A TPD cannot be imported to multiple tenants simultaneously. The same TPD is already active in another tenant.
solution: Delete the TPD from the existing tenant first, then retry importing to the new tenant.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/AIP Service/Troubleshooting Scenarios: AipService/Scenario: Unable to Import TPD to AIP Service

symptom: Export-ActivityExplorerData command is not recognized or fails to connect in PowerShell.
rootCause: ExchangePowerShell module not installed, or Connect-IPPSSession not run before executing the command.
solution: Install ExchangePowerShell module. Run Connect-IPPSSession with admin account first. Then execute Export-ActivityExplorerData.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Activity Explorer/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Activity Explorer/Scenario: Export-ActivityExplorerData cmdlet not working

symptom: Export-ActivityExplorerData returns error with filters, no data, or incorrect/unexpected data.
rootCause: Incorrect filter syntax, unsupported filter parameter, wrong date/time format, or activity filter value mismatch.
solution: Verify filters match supported list in documentation. Check date format matches examples. Try simplified filters (user + date range only). Compare results with Activity Explorer UI. If UI works but cmdlet does not, escalate with Fiddler trace and -Verbose output.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Activity Explorer/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Activity Explorer/Scenario: Export-ActivityExplorerData cmdlet not working

symptom: Activity Explorer shows different labeled item count than Content Explorer
rootCause: By design: Content Explorer counts items with labels; Activity Explorer counts activities on labeled items. One file can have multiple activities (applied, upgraded, auto-applied) generating many Activity Explorer entries but only one Content Explorer entry.
solution: Explain to customer this is expected behavior. Content Explorer = items with labels, Activity Explorer = events/activities on labeled items.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Activity Explorer/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Activity Explorer/Scenario: Information incorrect for an event in activity explorer

symptom: HowApplied field shows value 'none' in Activity Explorer
rootCause: By design: For operations where HowApplied is not applicable (e.g., CopiedToUSB), the default value is 'None'.
solution: Explain to customer that 'None' is the expected default value for operation types where HowApplied does not apply.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Activity Explorer/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Activity Explorer/Scenario: Information incorrect for an event in activity explorer

symptom: LabelEventType field shows value 'none' in Activity Explorer
rootCause: By design: LabelEventType is 'none' when there is no Label Event, e.g., File Discover events.
solution: Explain to customer this is expected behavior for events without a label action.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Activity Explorer/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Activity Explorer/Scenario: Information incorrect for an event in activity explorer

symptom: FileName field in Activity Explorer shows email subject instead of file name
rootCause: By design: When DLP Rule match is in Exchange and there is no attachment, the FileName field is populated with the email subject.
solution: Explain to customer this is expected behavior for Exchange DLP events without attachments.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Activity Explorer/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Activity Explorer/Scenario: Information incorrect for an event in activity explorer

symptom: Policy and Rule columns are blank for Endpoint events in Activity Explorer
rootCause: 'File Created on Removable Media' is an IRM event (not DLP), so Policy/Rule columns are not populated. Only 'File Copied to Removable Media' (DLP event) triggers policy and populates these columns.
solution: Explain the distinction: IRM events (File Created on Removable Media) do not carry Policy/Rule data. DLP events (File Copied to Removable Media, for classified files) do populate Policy/Rule.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Activity Explorer/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Activity Explorer/Scenario: Information incorrect for an event in activity explorer

symptom: Activity Explorer shows label GUIDs instead of label names for deleted labels
rootCause: Known behavior: When labels are deleted from the tenant, GUIDs remain in file headers. Activity Explorer cannot resolve deleted label names and shows GUIDs instead.
solution: This will continue until items get relabeled with a new label. No fix available; known behavior.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Activity Explorer/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Activity Explorer/Scenario: Information incorrect for an event in activity explorer

symptom: Filter not working in Activity Explorer; unable to filter by a specific column
rootCause: Values must be available/populated in the column for them to appear in the Filter dropdown. If no values exist under a column, the filter will not show options.
solution: Click Customize Columns, select the column, verify values are displayed. If values are not populated for the column, filter options will not be available. Check if the underlying audit log event contains the expected data.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Activity Explorer/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Activity Explorer/Scenario: Web page not functioning correctly for Activity Explorer

symptom: Client Side Auto Labeling not applying in Outlook, Word, or Excel Online; label not recommended or auto-applied.
rootCause: User does not have the required license (MIP_S_CLP2) for Auto Labeling. Plans containing this: Microsoft E5, Office 365 E5, Microsoft 365 E5 Compliance, Office 365 Advanced Compliance.
solution: Verify and assign correct license with MIP_S_CLP2 capability using Assist365. Check mailbox capabilities and licensing.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Auto Labeling/Client Side Auto Labeling/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Client Side Auto Labeling/Scenario: Client Side Auto Labeling not applying correctly

symptom: Client Side Auto Labeling configured correctly and SIT matches, but label not auto-applied in OWA.
rootCause: AutoLabeling_SensitiveTypeIds is missing from the label API response (compliancepolicy/api/v1.0/user/label) even though Conditions are set in IPPSSession PowerShell. The DLP/Label API is not publishing auto label settings.
solution: Capture OWA network trace, check label API response for AutoLabeling_SensitiveTypeIds. If missing, escalate to DLP team. Also check WebSocket AugLoop_SecurityClp_LabellingAnnotation calls for errors.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Auto Labeling/Client Side Auto Labeling/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Client Side Auto Labeling/Scenario: Client Side Auto Labeling not applying correctly

symptom: Client Side Auto Labeling not applying correctly - comprehensive troubleshooting (licensing, label config, SIT testing, client routing, network trace analysis).
solution: See排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-client-side-auto-labeling-not-applying.md
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Auto Labeling/Client Side Auto Labeling/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Client Side Auto Labeling/Scenario: Client Side Auto Labeling not applying correctly

symptom: Need to verify Auto Labeling policy evaluation results for a SharePoint or OneDrive document.
solution: See排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-test-dlppolicies-auto-labeling-report.md
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Auto Labeling/Server Side Auto Labeling/How to: Server Side Auto Labeling/How To: Gather an Auto Labeling Test-DlpPolicies Report

symptom: Need required information checklist for Server Side Auto Labeling escalation
solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-required-information-server-side-auto-labeling.md
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Auto Labeling/Server Side Auto Labeling/Required Information: Server Side Auto Labeling

symptom: Document not showing in Server Side Auto Labeling simulation Items for Review after document was modified
rootCause: Simulation mode only returns documents matching conditions at the time simulation started. Documents created or modified after simulation start are not evaluated.
solution: Re-run the simulation after the document modification, or turn on the auto labeling policy to apply labels going forward. SPO/ODB classification only runs when documents are created or modified.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Auto Labeling/Server Side Auto Labeling/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Server Side Auto Labeling/Scenario: Server Side Auto Labeling Simulation not showing expected item

symptom: Custom SIT not matching document in Server Side Auto Labeling simulation
rootCause: Custom SIT was created after the document was last modified. SPO/ODB classification only happens when documents are created or modified, so the SIT is not found on previously existing documents.
solution: Modify the document (e.g. trivial edit) after creating the custom SIT to trigger re-classification, then re-run the simulation.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Auto Labeling/Server Side Auto Labeling/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Server Side Auto Labeling/Scenario: Server Side Auto Labeling Simulation not showing expected item

symptom: Not all matching documents shown in Server Side Auto Labeling simulation results
rootCause: Simulation results are capped at 100 files per site/user per auto labeling policy. Only previously classified files are returned in the initial query.
solution: This is by design. Use Test-DlpPolicies cmdlet to verify specific documents individually. Consider scoping the policy to smaller location groups if full visibility is needed.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Auto Labeling/Server Side Auto Labeling/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Server Side Auto Labeling/Scenario: Server Side Auto Labeling Simulation not showing expected item

symptom: Server Side Auto Labeling simulation troubleshooting - comprehensive guide for SPO/ODB/Exchange
solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-server-side-auto-labeling-simulation-not-showing.md
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Auto Labeling/Server Side Auto Labeling/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Server Side Auto Labeling/Scenario: Server Side Auto Labeling Simulation not showing expected item

symptom: Server Side Auto Labeling not applying correctly - comprehensive troubleshooting (conditions, Exchange message trace, SPO Test-DlpPolicies).
solution: See guides/drafts/ado-wiki-server-side-auto-labeling-not-applying.md
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Auto Labeling/Server Side Auto Labeling/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Server Side Auto Labeling/Scenario: Server Side Auto Labeling not applying correctly

symptom: Server Side Auto Labeling policy simulation is stuck and never completes.
rootCause: Simulation may hang due to transient backend issues.
solution: Restart the simulation and wait up to 12 hours for it to complete. If still stuck after restart, escalate to engineering.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Auto Labeling/Server Side Auto Labeling/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Server Side Auto Labeling/Scenario: Server Side Auto Labeling simulation is stuck

symptom: Server Side Auto Labeling simulation metrics are incorrect; Files to be labeled count not correct.
rootCause: Simulation only shows 100 files per site/user per policy. Files to be labeled counter starts at 0 and updates every 24 hours. Auto label progress by top sites may differ.
solution: Explain the 100 files per site/user limit. Files to be labeled updates every 24 hours; wait for next aggregation cycle.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Auto Labeling/Server Side Auto Labeling/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Server Side Auto Labeling/Scenario: Server Side auto labeling metrics are not correct

symptom: Content Explorer count of Sensitivity Labels or Sensitive Info Types is incorrect or items not showing.
rootCause: Items with Sensitivity Labels or SITs take up to 7 days to populate in Content Explorer (14 days for server-side auto-labeled documents). Data is aggregated once a day, not real-time.
solution: Wait 7 days (14 days for server-side auto-labeling) for items to appear. Content Explorer updates once every 24 hours; live queries will show different counts.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Data Explorer/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Data Explorer/Scenario: Content Explorer count incorrect

symptom: Error while searching an item in Content Explorer; search returns error for Exchange, Teams, or SPO/ODB data.
rootCause: User does not have required Content Explorer role (Content Explorer List viewer or Content Explorer Content viewer).
solution: Assign Content Explorer List viewer role to see items in list view, and Content Explorer Content viewer role to view item contents. See: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/purview/data-classification-content-explorer
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Data Explorer/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Data Explorer/Scenario: Error while searching an item in Content Explorer

symptom: Content Explorer search returns error when using partial email address or partial site name.
rootCause: Content Explorer requires full email address (user@domain.com) for Exchange/Teams workload search, and full site name for SPO/ODB search. Partial values cause errors.
solution: Use full email address (e.g., user@domainname.com) for Exchange/Teams search. Use full site name for SPO/ODB search.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Data Explorer/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Data Explorer/Scenario: Error while searching an item in Content Explorer

symptom: Export-ContentExplorerData command not recognized or fails to run.
rootCause: ExchangePowerShell module is not installed or user has not connected via Connect-IPPSSession.
solution: Install ExchangePowerShell module and connect using Connect-IPPSSession with administrator account. See: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/exchange/connect-to-scc-powershell
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Data Explorer/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Data Explorer/Scenario: Export-ContentExplorerData cmdlet not working

symptom: Export-ContentExplorerData returns incomplete data or fails with large datasets.
rootCause: Default page size is 100. Max 10000 records per single query. Without pagination parameters, large datasets are truncated.
solution: Use -PageSize parameter for >100 records. For >10000 records, use -PageSize with -PageCookie in a script loop.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Data Explorer/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Data Explorer/Scenario: Export-ContentExplorerData cmdlet not working

symptom: Unable to preview file or email content in Content Explorer; encrypted file content not visible in preview pane.
rootCause: For encrypted files, Content Explorer shows the file in the list but cannot display the contents in the preview pane. This is by design.
solution: Explain this is by design for encrypted files. Verify user has Content Explorer Content viewer role for non-encrypted files.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Data Explorer/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Data Explorer/Scenario: Information not accessible in content explorer

symptom: Item with sensitivity label or SIT not visible in Content Explorer.
rootCause: Multiple possible causes: item not yet indexed (7-14 day delay), file type not supported for labeling in SPO/ODB, label not actually applied (check SPO Sensitivity column), or SIT not detected in file.
solution: 1) Wait 7 days (14 for server-side auto-labeled). 2) Verify file type is supported for labeling. 3) Check SPO Sensitivity column to confirm label is applied. 4) Test file against SIT tester. 5) Use full email/site name in search. 6) Check network trace for item presence.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Data Explorer/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Data Explorer/Scenario: Item not visible in Content Explorer

symptom: Engineer needs reference for current MIP/AIP client versions, Office channel versions, and Adobe MIP plug-in versions for compatibility troubleshooting.
solution: See guide: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-mip-client-versions.md
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/MIP SDK/Learn: MIP SDK/Learn: MIP Client Versions

symptom: Office MIP SDK encryption fails, inconsistent behavior across builds/users, or first-time encryption error
rootCause: Corrupted or stale MIP SDK cache files in local app data directories
solution: Delete contents of %localappdata%Microsoft<APPNAME>MIPSDKmip, %localappdata%MicrosoftOfficeMIPSDK, and %LocalAppData%MicrosoftOfficeCLP folders, then restart the Office app
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/MIP SDK/Learn: MIP SDK/Learn: Office MIP Logging

symptom: MIP SDK issue requires engineering escalation — need to collect required information
solution: See data collection template: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-required-information-mip-sdk.md
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/MIP SDK/Required Information: MIP SDK

symptom: Data collection checklist for MIP SDK support cases
solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-required-information-mip-sdk.md
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/MIP SDK/Required Information: MIP SDK

symptom: MPIP PowerShell cmdlets not recognized or error running PurviewInformationProtection module commands
rootCause: PowerShell module not properly installed in ProgramFiles (x86)\PurviewInformationProtection, PSModulePath not set, or using unsupported PowerShell version (PS7)
solution: Verify module installed: Get-Module PurviewInformationProtection. Check installation path: ProgramFiles (x86)\PurviewInformationProtection. Ensure using Windows PowerShell 4.0+ (NOT PowerShell 7). Run $PSVersionTable to verify version.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/MPIP Client/How to: MPIP Client/How to: Check MPIP Client PowerShell module is installed

symptom: MPIP client cannot connect to Azure RMS service, TLS connection terminated before reaching service
rootCause: TLS 1.2 not enabled, packet inspection device terminating TLS client-to-service connections, or proxy/firewall blocking Azure RMS endpoints
solution: Test connection with PowerShell: [System.Net.HttpWebRequest]::Create('https://admin.na.aadrm.com/admin/admin.svc').GetResponse(). Verify certificate issuer. If fails: enable TLS 1.2, check for packet inspection devices, review proxy/firewall configuration.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/MPIP Client/How to: MPIP Client/How to: Check if Client Connection is terminated Before Azure RMS

symptom: User unable to apply sensitivity labels using MPIP client, wrong account signed in or sign-in failure
rootCause: Incorrect user account signed in to MPIP client, or cached credentials for wrong account
solution: Right-click Office file > Apply Sensitivity Label > check Help and Feedback > verify status Connected with correct user. If wrong: Reset Settings, run Clear-Authentication cmdlet, sign out Work/School account from Windows, re-sign in with correct account.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/MPIP Client/How to: MPIP Client/How to: Check the correct user account is signed-in to the MPIP Client

symptom: Need to collect and analyze MPIP client diagnostic logs for troubleshooting
solution: See troubleshooting guide: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-collect-analyze-mpip-client-log.md
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/MPIP Client/How to: MPIP Client/How to: Collect and analyze MPIP Client log

symptom: Need to verify if a sensitivity label has encryption (protection) enabled
solution: In Purview Portal: edit the sensitivity label > Protection Settings > check if Control Access option is enabled. If enabled, encryption is active and permissions are on the next page.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/MPIP Client/How to: MPIP Client/How to: check a label is encryption enabled?

symptom: Need to update MPIP client or disable automatic updates
solution: Update methods: (1) Manual download from Microsoft Download Center, (2) Windows Update (~1 month after release), (3) Enterprise tools (WSUS/SCCM/Group Policy). No option to disable auto-updates - controlled entirely by Windows Update settings on device.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/MPIP Client/How to: MPIP Client/How to update the Microsoft Purview Information Protection (MPIP) client?

symptom: How to update the MPIP client; questions about automatic update behavior
solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-update-mpip-client.md
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/MPIP Client/How to: MPIP Client/How to update the Microsoft Purview Information Protection (MPIP) client?

symptom: Labels appear inconsistent or documents show different labels across Office Clients, Web Apps, and SPO after enabling co-authoring
rootCause: Enabling co-authoring changes labeling metadata format and location (from Custom Document Properties to LabelInfo). Existing labeled files retain old format until opened and saved. Disabling and re-enabling co-authoring can repeat inconsistency.
solution: Inform customer that existing files need to be opened and saved to migrate metadata to new format. Co-authoring is tenant-wide; ensure all prerequisites met before enabling. Opt-out via Set-PolicyConfig -EnableLabelCoauth $False (support-guided). Do not instruct customer to roll back without understanding all implications.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/MPIP Client/Learn: MPIP Client/Learn: Co-Authoring

symptom: Data collection checklist for MPIP Client support cases
solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-required-information-mpip-client.md
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/MPIP Client/Required Information: MPIP Client

symptom: Support boundaries and routing for MPIP Client vs MPIP Scanner cases
solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-support-boundaries-mpip-client.md
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/MPIP Client/Support Boundaries: MPIP Client

symptom: Content markings (header, footer, watermark) not visible after applying sensitivity label via MPIP right-click or PowerShell
rootCause: Content markings are only applied when the label is applied within Office applications. Labeling outside Office (right-click or PowerShell) does not render visual markings — this is by design.
solution: Verify content marking is enabled on the label in Purview portal (Edit label → Content marking settings). Explain to customer that visual markings only apply when label is applied within Office apps per https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/purview/sensitivity-labels-office-apps#when-office-apps-apply-content-marking-and-encryption. If markings still missing in Office, escalate with MPIP client logs.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/MPIP Client/Troubleshooting Scenarios: MPIP Client/Scenario: Content markings are not applied to files

symptom: AIP Office add-in or MPIP client fails to download sensitivity label policy; log shows Policy sync failed with HttpResponse.StatusCode=400 from substrate.office.com
rootCause: Two possible causes: 1) Sensitivity labels not published to the user account via a label policy. 2) AIP Office add-in is retired and blocked from downloading policies (returns 400).
solution: 1) Verify labels are published to user via label policy in Purview portal. 2) If log shows 400 error from substrate.office.com/CompliancePolicy/ClientSyncFile, this is by design — AIP add-in is retired. Migrate to built-in Office sensitivity labeling or MPIP client.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/MPIP Client/Troubleshooting Scenarios: MPIP Client/Scenario: MIP client and AIP Office add-in fail to download policy

symptom: MPIP Add-in and AIP Add-in conflict when both installed on the same machine; labeling not working properly
rootCause: MPIP Add-in for Outlook and AIP Add-in for Office cannot coexist on the same machine
solution: Uninstall AIP Add-in for Office before installing MPIP Add-in for Outlook. Only one can be active at a time. MPIP client installation automatically removes AIP UL v2.X client.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/MPIP Client/Troubleshooting Scenarios: MPIP Client/Scenario: MPIP Add-in for Outlook issues

symptom: MPIP Add-in for Outlook is not supported with Office Perpetual or Office LTSC
rootCause: By design - MPIP Add-in for Outlook only supports Microsoft 365 Apps
solution: Migrate to Microsoft 365 Apps for Enterprise prior to deploying the MPIP Add-in for Outlook. Office Perpetual and LTSC are not supported.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/MPIP Client/Troubleshooting Scenarios: MPIP Client/Scenario: MPIP Add-in for Outlook issues

symptom: MPIP client fails to open AD RMS protected files after migration to AIP; EndUserLicenses request routed to on-premise AD RMS instead of api.aadrm.com
rootCause: _rmsdisc DNS SRV record still configured and pointing to on-premise AD RMS URL, causing MPIP client to route license requests to decommissioned AD RMS server instead of AIP service
solution: Remove the _rmsdisc DNS SRV record after completing AD RMS to AIP migration. Verify in MPIP client MIP SDK logs that EndUserLicenses requests are sent to api.aadrm.com, not the on-prem AD RMS URL.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/MPIP Client/Troubleshooting Scenarios: MPIP Client/Scenario: MPIP Client fails to open AD RMS protected files after the migration to AIP

symptom: MPIP right-click context menu missing or shows as Microsoft.Azip.RightClick instead of Apply Sensitivity Label with Microsoft Purview
rootCause: Registry values under HKEY_CLASSES_ROOTAllFilesystemObjectsshellMicrosoft.Azip.RightClick stripped or not migrated during Windows 10 to Windows 11 upgrade
solution: Export Microsoft.Azip.RightClick registry key from working system and import on affected system. Alternative: reinstall or upgrade MPIP/AIP client. Windows fix available via KB5027318 Dynamic Update (June 2023) - enable Dynamic Update when upgrading to Windows 11 22H2.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/MPIP Client/Troubleshooting Scenarios: MPIP Client/Scenario: MPIP is missing from Right click Context menu

symptom: Error: This file does not have an app associated with it when right-clicking Apply Sensitivity Label with Microsoft Purview
rootCause: A file named Program (or similar) exists in the root of C drive, interfering with the context menu handler path resolution
solution: Check C drive root for a file named Program or similar and delete it. If issue persists, check for missing context menu registry entries.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/MPIP Client/Troubleshooting Scenarios: MPIP Client/Scenario: Unable to apply label using Right click Classify and Protect

symptom: Cannot apply sensitivity label to files in Windows, Program Files, ProgramData, or AppData folders using MPIP right-click option
rootCause: MPIP client automatically excludes critical system folders (Windows, Program Files, ProgramData, AppData) from classification and labeling to prevent modification of system-critical files.
solution: This is by design. Move files to a non-excluded folder before applying sensitivity labels. Excluded folders: Windows, Program Files (x86), ProgramData, AppData. See: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/purview/information-protection-client#excluded-folders-and-file-types
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/MPIP Client/Troubleshooting Scenarios: MPIP Client/Scenario: Unable to apply label using Right click Classify and Protect

symptom: Unable to apply sensitivity label using right-click Classify and Protect / Apply Sensitivity Label with Microsoft Purview
solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-unable-apply-label-right-click.md
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/MPIP Client/Troubleshooting Scenarios: MPIP Client/Scenario: Unable to apply label using Right click Classify and Protect

symptom: Unable to apply sensitivity label on files using MPIP PowerShell Set-FileLabel command
solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-unable-apply-label-powershell.md
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/MPIP Client/Troubleshooting Scenarios: MPIP Client/Scenario: Unable to apply Sensitivity label using PowerShell

symptom: Unable to remove encryption from emails inside PST file using Remove-FileLabel PowerShell command
rootCause: EnableContainerSupport advanced setting not enabled in LabelPolicy — required for removing encryption from .zip, .rar, .7z, and emails inside PST files
solution: Enable EnableContainerSupport in LabelPolicy Advanced setting: Set-LabelPolicy -Identity <policy> -AdvancedSettings @{EnableContainerSupport='true'}. Then use Remove-FileLabel <path> -RemoveProtection. See https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/exchange/client-advanced-settings?view=exchange-ps#enablecontainersupport
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/MPIP Client/Troubleshooting Scenarios: MPIP Client/Scenario: Unable to remove Sensitivity label or Encryption from files using PowerShell

symptom: Unable to remove or change sensitivity label on encrypted file using PowerShell or MPIP right-click — user lacks permissions
rootCause: User does not have Full Control (Owner) permission on the encrypted file. Only users with Owner/Full Control rights can remove or change labels on encrypted files.
solution: Check label encryption settings in Purview portal — verify user has Owner right. If not, configure AIP Super User to perform label removal: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/information-protection/configure-super-users
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/MPIP Client/Troubleshooting Scenarios: MPIP Client/Scenario: Unable to remove Sensitivity label or Encryption from files using PowerShell

symptom: Delete Label button grayed out in MPIP client right-click context menu — cannot remove sensitivity label from file
rootCause: Mandatory Labeling is enabled on the label policy scoped to the user. By design, users cannot delete a label when mandatory labeling is active — they can only change to another label.
solution: This is by design. If mandatory labeling is enabled, label cannot be removed, only changed. Check highest priority Label policy → Require users to apply a label setting. If removal is needed, temporarily disable mandatory labeling or use PowerShell with Super User.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/MPIP Client/Troubleshooting Scenarios: MPIP Client/Scenario: Unable to remove or change label using Right click Classify and Protect option

symptom: Unable to remove sensitivity label or encryption from files using PowerShell (Remove-FileLabel) — multi-step troubleshooting
solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-unable-to-remove-sensitivity-label-powershell.md
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/MPIP Client/Troubleshooting Scenarios: MPIP Client/Scenario: Unable to remove Sensitivity label or Encryption from files using PowerShell

symptom: Unable to remove or change sensitivity label using Right click Classify and Protect option — multi-step troubleshooting
solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-unable-to-remove-label-right-click.md
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/MPIP Client/Troubleshooting Scenarios: MPIP Client/Scenario: Unable to remove or change label using Right click Classify and Protect option

symptom: Need to enable trace level logging for MPIP Scanner to get detailed diagnostic data
rootCause: Default MPIP Scanner logging does not capture trace-level detail needed for advanced troubleshooting
solution: On scanner server: open Registry Editor → navigate to HKEY_CURRENT_USER\Software\Microsoft\MSIP → add REG_SZ value LogLevel=Trace → restart Microsoft Purview Information Protection Scanner service. Subsequent scans write detailed data to msipscanner.iplog.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/MPIP Scanner/How to: MPIP Scanner/How to: Enable trace level logging

symptom: MPIP Scanner report interpretation — understanding summary and detailed report fields, skipped reasons, and scan status
solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-read-scanner-reports.md
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/MPIP Scanner/How to: MPIP Scanner/How to: Read Scanner Reports

symptom: MPIP Scanner DLP policy runs but does not work — no files blocked or quarantined despite policy matching
rootCause: DLP policy for Scanner has no proper action defined. DLP policies for Scanner without actions will run but not work (by design). Alerts alone are not considered proper actions for Scanner DLP policy.
solution: Create DLP policy with proper enforcement action (restrict file / block / quarantine). Do not rely on Alerts as the only action — Scanner DLP requires explicit enforcement actions like restrict/block/quarantine. See https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/purview/deploy-scanner-configure-install?tabs=azure-portal-only#use-a-dlp-policy
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/MPIP Scanner/How to: MPIP Scanner/How to: Scanner DLP

symptom: Wrong or default branding template applied to OME encrypted message — recipient sees default template instead of custom branding
rootCause: Wrapper message has AuthAs Anonymous (from 3rd party connector), causing default OME template to be applied instead of custom branding
solution: Check X-MS-Exchange-CrossTenant-AuthAs header. If Anonymous: adjust mail flow so OME encrypted messages do not flow through the 3rd party connector. If Internal: check Microsoft.Exchange.RMSApaAgent.ProtectionCustomizationTemplate header for correct branding template name, review transport/DLP rule execution order.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Purview Message Encryption/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Purview Message Encryption/Scenario: Branding Template not working as expected

symptom: Cannot open encrypted email or document sent to shared or delegate mailbox — user unable to access protected content in shared/delegated mailbox
solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-encrypted-message-shared-delegate-mailbox.md
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Purview Message Encryption/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Purview Message Encryption/Scenario: Cannot open Encrypted message sent to Shared or Delegate Mailbox

symptom: Journaled encrypted email missing decrypted attachment — JournalReportDecryptionEnabled is False
rootCause: IRM configuration parameter JournalReportDecryptionEnabled is set to False, preventing decrypted copy in journal reports
solution: Run Set-IRMConfiguration -JournalReportDecryptionEnabled $true. Takes time to replicate; only applies to messages received after change.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Purview Message Encryption/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Purview Message Encryption/Scenario: Encrypted email journaling is not working correctly

symptom: Journaled encrypted email missing decrypted attachment — ETR with explicit OME branding applied to message
rootCause: Transport rule applies ApplyRightsProtectionCustomizationTemplate (explicit OME branding), which prevents journal report decryption by design
solution: Remove explicit OME branding (ApplyRightsProtectionCustomizationTemplate action) from the ETR. Use implicit encryption instead. Journal report decryption does not support explicit OME branding.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Purview Message Encryption/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Purview Message Encryption/Scenario: Encrypted email journaling is not working correctly

symptom: Encrypted email journaling not working correctly — multi-step troubleshooting for missing journal reports or missing decrypted attachments
solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-encrypted-email-journaling.md
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Purview Message Encryption/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Purview Message Encryption/Scenario: Encrypted email journaling is not working correctly

symptom: RMS template present in AIP but not visible to on-premises Exchange/FCI via RMS Connector; scoped template not showing in legacy apps
rootCause: Template has ScopedIdentities configured, and scoped templates are not advertised to legacy applications by default.
solution: Run: Set-AipServiceTemplateProperty -TemplateId <id> -EnableInLegacyApps $true. Note: RMS Connector only retrieves RMS templates, not labels; labels without encryption or with user-defined permissions (DNF/Encrypt-Only) will not appear.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/RMS Connector/Troubleshooting Scenarios: RMS Connector/Scenario: Missing AIP templates

symptom: SharePoint takes 30-90 seconds to download files from IRM-enabled document library via RMS Connector; very slow encryption on egress
rootCause: SharePoint is attempting to retrieve RMS templates directly from AIP instead of through the RMS Connector. This is unexpected behavior where SharePoint bypasses the connector.
solution: Create MsipcHostNameRedirection.dat file with mapping: Azure RMS endpoint URL to connector URL (e.g. https://<guid>.rms.na.aadrm.com https://connector.contoso.com). Place in %ProgramFiles%\Active Directory Rights Management Services Client 2.1\ and the x86 path. Run IISReset on each SharePoint WFE.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/RMS Connector/Troubleshooting Scenarios: RMS Connector/Scenario: SharePoint takes a long time to encrypt files on egress

symptom: Sensitivity Label display name or tooltip not shown in localized Office language; labels appear only in default language
rootCause: Locale settings on the label only have default values. Editing via Purview portal only updates default language, not other locales.
solution: Use Set-Label PowerShell with -LocaleSettings to configure display names and tooltips per locale. Example: Set-Label -Identity 'LabelName' -LocaleSettings '{"LocaleKey":"displayName","Settings":[{"Key":"default","Value":"Default Name"},{"Key":"es-ES","Value":"Spanish Name"}]}'. Ref locale IDs: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/openspecs/office_standards/ms-oe376/6c085406
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Sensitivity Labels/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Sensitivity Labels/Scenario: Label Display Name or Tooltip is not displayed in localized languages

symptom: Admin cannot see sensitivity label or label policy in Purview portal; label or policy appears missing
rootCause: User either lacks required Purview RBAC role for label management, or the label policy is scoped to Admin Units (AUs) and the user is not in the AU.
solution: 1) Verify user has required RBAC role per docs. 2) Check if label policy has Admin Units: edit policy and check under Admin Units. If scoped to AUs, add user to the AU and wait 24 hours for Entra sync.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Sensitivity Labels/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Sensitivity Labels/Scenario: Sensitivity Label Missing in Purview

symptom: Sensitivity Label or Label Policy stuck in PendingDeletion state for more than 24 hours; label cannot be fully deleted
rootCause: Backend deletion process has stalled; this is a service-side issue that cannot be resolved by customer or support engineer.
solution: Check label Mode: Get-Label -Identity 'name' | FT Name,Mode,*whench*,GUID,DistributionStatus. If PendingDeletion for >24h, escalate immediately for backend intervention. If <24h, wait and recheck.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Sensitivity Labels/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Sensitivity Labels/Scenario: Sensitivity Label is stuck in PendingDeletion

symptom: Cannot edit or delete Sensitivity Label; error: The default content label GUID cannot be set because its content type Site UnifiedGroup is invalid
rootCause: Misconfigured label - DefaultContentLabel references an incompatible content type.
solution: In EOP PowerShell: Set-Label -Identity <label> -ContentType 'File, Email, Site, UnifiedGroup, Teamwork'; then Set-Label -Identity <label> -DefaultContentLabel ''. Retry edit/delete.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Sensitivity Labels/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Sensitivity Labels/Scenario: Unable to Delete Sensitivity Label

symptom: Cannot revoke encrypted message despite having Premium license; revoke button not shown for multi-recipient or O365-recipient messages
rootCause: By design: revocation only works for messages sent via OWA to exactly 1 external non-O365 recipient. Messages to multiple recipients, internal users, other O365 tenants, or Microsoft consumer domains (hotmail/live/outlook.com) cannot be revoked.
solution: Inform customer of by-design limitation. Requirements: 1) OWA client only, 2) single recipient, 3) non-O365 external recipient, 4) EO/DNF template. Button may take 30+ min to appear; force refresh with CTRL+Refresh.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Purview Message Encryption/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Purview Message Encryption/Scenario: Sender is unable to revoke an encrypted message

symptom: Recipient unable to open encrypted Word/Excel/PowerPoint attachments from PME messages — involves label permissions, client choice (online vs desktop), and conditional access policies
solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-pme-recipient-encrypted-attachment.md
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Purview Message Encryption/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Purview Message Encryption/Scenario: Recipient is not able to open Encrypted Attachment
