symptom: Error 'The generated rule blob is too long' when configuring or saving sensitivity label
rootCause: Label configuration (conditions, auto-labeling rules, settings) exceeds the maximum allowed size for the rule blob.
solution: Remove configurations from the label to reduce its overall size. Simplify auto-labeling conditions or split across multiple labels.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Sensitivity Labels/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Sensitivity Labels/Scenario: Sensitivity Label is not able to be configured correctly

symptom: Cannot change sensitivity label Name in Purview portal; Name field is not editable
rootCause: By design - label Name (internal identifier) is immutable once created. Only DisplayName can be changed.
solution: Change the Display Name instead. Label Name cannot be modified after creation. This is by design.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Sensitivity Labels/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Sensitivity Labels/Scenario: Sensitivity Label is not able to be configured correctly

symptom: Sensitivity Label appears auto-applied in Activity Explorer but user did not manually label; label applied event on replies/forwards
rootCause: When user replies to or forwards a labeled email, Activity Explorer logs a Label applied event. The label is carried over from original message - not actually auto-applied.
solution: Check email subject for Fw: or Re: prefix. If present, label was inherited from original message. This is expected behavior, not a false positive in auto-labeling.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Sensitivity Labels/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Sensitivity Labels/Scenario: Sensitivity Label is unexpectedly applied

symptom: M365 Copilot automatically upgrades sensitivity label on email draft to higher priority label from referenced content
rootCause: By design - Copilot applies the highest priority sensitivity label from referenced files/emails to the chat and email draft to prevent accidental protection downgrade.
solution: Expected behavior. Copilot upgrades label to match the most restrictive referenced content. Cannot be disabled for individual labels.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Sensitivity Labels/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Sensitivity Labels/Scenario: Sensitivity Label is unexpectedly applied

symptom: Content marking (header/footer/watermark) not applied when sensitivity label is applied externally via MPIP client, MPIP scanner, or auto-labeling policies
rootCause: By design - when label is applied outside of Office editing apps (Word, Excel, etc.), only label metadata is applied, not content marking. Content marking is rendered only when the file is opened in an editor.
solution: Expected behavior per docs. Content marking is applied only during label assignment within file editors. For externally-applied labels, open the file in an Office app for content marking to render. Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/purview/sensitivity-labels-office-apps#when-office-apps-apply-content-marking-and-encryption
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Sensitivity Labels/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Sensitivity Labels/Scenario: Sensitivity Label content marking is not working

symptom: Sensitivity label not visible in Office apps (Word, Excel, Outlook, Teams) despite being configured in Purview portal
rootCause: Multiple possible causes: label not published to user via label policy, user lacks required license, label policy scoped to Admin Units, or app-specific configuration missing (e.g. container labels need explicit enablement for SharePoint/Teams/Groups).
solution: Run Purview Portal diagnostic: 'Does the label policy apply to them?'. If label shows in diagnostic but not in app, transfer to app team. If label not in diagnostic: check license, label policy scope, and Admin Units. For containers: enable sensitivity label support per docs.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Sensitivity Labels/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Sensitivity Labels/Scenario: Sensitivity Label is not showing

symptom: Sensitivity Label content marking (headers/footers/watermarks) not applied to emails or documents — multi-step troubleshooting
solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-sensitivity-label-content-marking.md
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Sensitivity Labels/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Sensitivity Labels/Scenario: Sensitivity Label content marking is not working

symptom: Sensitivity Label encryption not giving expected access — cannot open labeled document or email, screen sharing restrictions, email replies become attachments
solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-sensitivity-label-encryption-access.md
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Sensitivity Labels/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Sensitivity Labels/Scenario: Sensitivity Label encryption is not giving access as expected

symptom: Sensitivity Label or Label Policy cannot be created, edited, or configured correctly — multi-step diagnostic for various configuration errors
solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-sensitivity-label-configuration-issues.md
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Sensitivity Labels/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Sensitivity Labels/Scenario: Sensitivity Label is not able to be configured correctly

symptom: Sensitivity Label content marking not applied to documents labeled externally (via MPIP client, MPIP scanner, or auto-labeling policies)
rootCause: By design - content marking is only applied at label assignment time within a file editor app (Word, Excel, etc.). External labeling applies label metadata but not content marking.
solution: Inform customer this is expected behavior per Microsoft docs. The document must be opened in an Office editor app for content marking to be applied. Share public documentation link.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Sensitivity Labels/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Sensitivity Labels/Scenario: Sensitivity Label content marking is not working

symptom: Protected email replies become attachments instead of inline in message thread when using Sensitivity Label encryption
rootCause: Too few rights granted in label protection settings. Edit and Save rights are not included, causing replies to be treated as new attachments.
solution: Add Edit and Save rights to the label encryption settings. This does not allow the user to remove protection. Per RMS usage rights documentation, these rights are required for Reply/Reply All/Forward to work inline.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Sensitivity Labels/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Sensitivity Labels/Scenario: Sensitivity Label encryption is not giving access as expected

symptom: Cannot access document with Dynamic Watermark enabled; access denied on unsupported Office client version
rootCause: Client Office version does not support Dynamic Watermarks. Unsupported versions are denied access to files with dynamic watermark enforcement.
solution: Upgrade to a supported Office client version. Check supported versions at Microsoft docs (sensitivity-labels-versions). Dynamic watermarks require specific minimum client versions.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Sensitivity Labels/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Sensitivity Labels/Scenario: Sensitivity Label encryption is not giving access as expected

symptom: Error 'The generated rule blob is too long' when configuring Sensitivity Label
rootCause: Label configuration is too large, exceeding the maximum allowed size for the rule blob.
solution: Remove configurations from the label to reduce its size. Simplify the label settings (e.g., reduce number of users/groups, conditions, or content types).
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Sensitivity Labels/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Sensitivity Labels/Scenario: Sensitivity Label is not able to be configured correctly

symptom: Sensitivity Label with 'Let users assign permissions' (UDP) not showing in Office Online apps (Word/Excel/PowerPoint Online) or SharePoint/OneDrive
rootCause: By design - User Defined Permission (UDP) labels are not supported in Office Online apps or SharePoint/OneDrive. For Outlook, specific restriction settings must be configured.
solution: For Outlook: set 'In Outlook, enforce one of the following restrictions' to 'Encrypt Only' or 'Do Not Forward'. For desktop apps: set 'In Word, PowerPoint, and Excel, prompt users to specify permissions'. UDP labels will not appear in Office Online apps.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Sensitivity Labels/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Sensitivity Labels/Scenario: Sensitivity Label is not showing

symptom: Parent Sensitivity Label published without child sub-labels cannot be applied; label appears but is not selectable
rootCause: Publishing only the parent label without any of its children is not supported. Parent labels with sub-labels cannot be directly applied to content.
solution: Publish both parent and child sub-labels in the label policy. Do not set a parent label as default label or auto-apply label.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Sensitivity Labels/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Sensitivity Labels/Scenario: Sensitivity Label is not showing

symptom: Sensitivity Labels not shown to members of a Unified Group with hidden group membership scoped in label policy
rootCause: Unified Groups with HiddenGroupMembershipEnabled set to true cannot properly scope label policies - labels will not be shown to the group members.
solution: Disable hidden group membership on the Unified Group, or use a different scoping mechanism (individual users, non-hidden groups, or 'all users').
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Sensitivity Labels/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Sensitivity Labels/Scenario: Sensitivity Label is not showing

symptom: Applying encryption label fails: 'We were not able to find the Information Rights Management template' or 'No logged on Office users are configured for IRM' or 'RMS service is disabled for this tenant'
rootCause: AIP Service is disabled for the tenant, or the user does not meet the AIP Service Onboarding Control Policy (OCP) requirements.
solution: Verify AIP Service is enabled via AIPService PowerShell module. Check for Onboarding Control Policy: if OCP is set, ensure the user meets OCP settings. Enable the service if disabled.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Sensitivity Labels/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Sensitivity Labels/Scenario: Sensitivity Label is not working correctly

symptom: Sensitivity Label shows wrong name in client application; label name differs from expected display
rootCause: LocaleSettings mismatch - the label name in locale settings for 'default' or 'en-US' differs from what was expected. Different locale keys store different display names.
solution: Run: Get-Label 'labelName' | fl *LocaleSettings* to check all locale values. Correct with Set-Label -LocaleSettings to fix the incorrect locale entry, especially 'default' and 'en-US' keys.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Sensitivity Labels/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Sensitivity Labels/Scenario: Sensitivity Label is not working correctly

symptom: Sensitivity Label appears auto-applied but user did not manually label; false positive auto-labeling event in Activity Explorer
rootCause: When a user forwards or replies to an email with a Sensitivity Label, Activity Explorer generates a 'Label applied' event even though the label was just carried over from the original message - not auto-applied.
solution: Check email subject for 'Fw:' or 'Re:' prefixes. If present, the label was inherited from the original message, not auto-applied. This is expected behavior. External users replying to labeled emails also trigger this event.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Sensitivity Labels/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Sensitivity Labels/Scenario: Sensitivity Label is unexpectedly applied

symptom: M365 Copilot Chat/prompts unexpectedly apply higher sensitivity label to email draft or conversation
rootCause: By design - Copilot applies the highest priority sensitivity label from referenced files and emails to the chat conversation and Outlook email drafts to protect sensitive information from being shared with lower protections.
solution: Inform customer this is expected behavior. When Copilot references content with sensitivity labels, it automatically upgrades the label on the output to match the most restrictive label from the source content.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Sensitivity Labels/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Sensitivity Labels/Scenario: Sensitivity Label is unexpectedly applied

symptom: Recipient is unable to read/open encrypted email - comprehensive troubleshooting needed
solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-pme-recipient-cannot-read-encrypted-email.md
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Purview Message Encryption/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Purview Message Encryption/Scenario: Recipient is not able to read an encrypted email

symptom: Sender unable to send encrypted email - comprehensive troubleshooting needed covering labels, permissions, mail flow, conditional access
solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-pme-sender-cannot-send-encrypted-email.md
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Purview Message Encryption/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Purview Message Encryption/Scenario: Sender cannot send encrypted email

symptom: Purview scan fails with 'Internal system error. Please contact support with correlationId' when using private endpoint
rootCause: DNS/connectivity issue between SHIR VM and Purview managed storage account or Event Hubs
solution: Validate name resolution between SHIR VM and Purview managed blob queue / Event Hubs through port 443 using private IP addresses. Use Test-NetConnection to verify.

symptom: Scan fails with StorageException 404 Not Found: 'Unable to setup config overrides for this scan'
rootCause: Older version of self-hosted integration runtime (< 5.9.7885.3) cannot communicate with Purview managed storage
solution: Upgrade self-hosted integration runtime to version 5.9.7885.3 or later

symptom: Purview private endpoint deployment fails with Azure Policy validation error
rootCause: Existing Azure Policy Assignment blocks deployment of required resources (Purview, Private Endpoint, DNS Zones, Event Hubs, Storage)
solution: Review Azure Policy Assignments and ensure deployment is allowed for: Microsoft.Purview/Accounts, Microsoft.Network/privateEndpoints, Microsoft.Network/privateDnsZones, Microsoft.EventHub/namespaces, Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts

symptom: 'Not authorized to access this Microsoft Purview account. This account is behind a private endpoint.'
rootCause: User connecting from a public endpoint while Public network access is set to Deny on the Purview account
solution: Access Purview governance portal from a machine in the same VNet as the portal private endpoint, or from a CorpNet-connected VM with hybrid connectivity

symptom: SQL scan via SHIR fails with 'This implementation is not part of the Windows Platform FIPS validated cryptographic algorithms'
rootCause: FIPS mode is enabled on the self-hosted integration runtime machine, blocking required cryptographic classes
solution: Disable FIPS mode on the self-hosted integration runtime server

symptom: Purview scans that previously worked are now failing
rootCause: Credential rotation, Azure Policy preventing Storage account updates, or outdated self-hosted integration runtime
solution: 1) Update scan credentials if rotated. 2) Create Azure Policy exception using Purview exception tag guide. 3) Update SHIR to latest version.

symptom: Test connection passes but scan fails with connection error when using private endpoints or VNets
rootCause: Network Security Group (NSG) rules blocking scan traffic despite allowing test connection
solution: Confirm network settings and review NSG rules. Ensure all required ports and endpoints are allowed in NSG.

symptom: JDK error (UserErrorJreNotFound) when scanning data sources using SHIR
rootCause: Java Runtime Environment not installed or wrong version/architecture on SHIR machine
solution: Install recommended JDK version matching SHIR architecture (64-bit JRE for 64-bit IR). Verify registry entries at HKLM\SOFTWARE\JavaSoft\Java Runtime Environment and check jvm.dll exists in bin\server folder.

symptom: OutOfMemoryException (OOM) error when running scan with self-hosted IR
rootCause: High memory usage from concurrent scan activity or insufficient memory on the SHIR machine
solution: Check resource usage on SHIR machine, ensure no other heavy software is running concurrently, and confirm machine meets recommended SHIR configurations

symptom: SHIR error: 'Could not load file or assembly System.ValueTuple, Version=4.0.2.0'
rootCause: System.ValueTuple.dll not found in GAC, Shared, or Gateway folder of the Integration Runtime installation
solution: Copy System.ValueTuple.dll from C:\Program Files\Microsoft Integration Runtime\4.0\Gateway\DataScan to C:\Program Files\Microsoft Integration Runtime\4.0\Gateway

symptom: Self-hosted IR goes offline with 'Authentication Key is not assigned yet'
rootCause: SHIR node or logical SHIR in Purview portal was deleted, or a clean uninstall was performed
solution: Check if Configurations file exists at %programdata%\Microsoft\Data Transfer\DataManagementGateway. If deleted, investigate who deleted it and re-register the SHIR node.

symptom: SHIR certificate error: 'Failed to change intranet communication encryption mode... private key missing'
rootCause: User account has low privilege for private key access, or certificate was generated as signature instead of key exchange
solution: Use account with appropriate privileges. Import certificate with: certutil -importpfx FILENAME.pfx AT_KEYEXCHANGE

symptom: SHIR registration error after changing service account: 'Integration Runtime (Self-hosted) node has encountered an error during registration'
rootCause: When service account is changed, permissions on dependent resources (folders, registries) remain with the old account
solution: Check DIAHostService sign-in account permissions on %programdata%\Microsoft\Data Transfer\DataManagementGateway. If UnauthorizedAccessException, clean uninstall SHIR, reinstall, switch service account using dmgcmd.exe -SwitchServiceAccount, and re-register.

symptom: SHIR can't connect to cloud service / SSL/TLS trust relationship error: 'Could not establish trust relationship for the SSL/TLS secure channel'
rootCause: Root CA of service server certificate not trusted on SHIR machine, or proxy replacing server certificate with untrusted cert. DigiCert Global Root G2 may be missing.
solution: 1) Install DigiCert Global Root G2 certificate if missing. 2) Ensure service cert chain is trusted. 3) If proxy is replacing certs, either trust the proxy's root CA or configure proxy not to replace service cert.

symptom: SHIR certificate error 103: 'Failed to grant Integration Runtime service account the access of to the certificate'
rootCause: Certificate uses KSP (key storage provider) storage, which is not supported by SHIR; only CSP (cryptographic service provider) is supported
solution: Use CSP certificates. Convert with: certutil -CSP 'CSP or KSP' -ImportPFX FILENAME.pfx, or use openssl to convert PFX to PEM and back.

symptom: Information Protection Scanner: 'The service didn't accept the auth token' (AccessDeniedException)
rootCause: Set-Authentication command failed due to incorrect permissions in Azure portal for the scanner app registration
solution: Verify permissions are defined correctly in Azure portal. Follow 'Create and configure Microsoft Entra applications for Set-Authentication' guide.

symptom: Information Protection Scanner: 'NoAuthTokenException: Client application failed to provide authentication token' or 'user_interaction_required'
rootCause: Scanner running non-interactively without a valid authentication token
solution: Authenticate using Set-Authentication cmdlet with token parameter on behalf of the scanner service account: Set-Authentication -AppId ... -AppSecret ... -DelegatedUser scanner@contoso.com -TenantId ... -OnBehalfOf $pscreds

symptom: Information Protection Scanner: 'SchemaMismatchException' or 'DB schema is not up to date'
rootCause: Scanner database schema is outdated and doesn't match the current scanner version
solution: Run Update-ScannerDatabase cmdlet to resynchronize the schema

symptom: Information Protection Scanner: 'The underlying connection was closed: An unexpected error occurred on a send' with IOException
rootCause: TLS 1.2 is not enabled on the scanner machine
solution: Enable TLS 1.2 on the scanner machine. See Microsoft's guidance on enabling TLS 1.2 for clients.

symptom: Information Protection Scanner process stuck / times out while scanning large repositories
rootCause: Exhausted dynamic port range or SharePoint list view threshold exceeded, preventing new network connections
solution: Increase dynamic ports for the OS. For SharePoint, increase list view threshold above default 5000. Run Start-ScannerDiagnostics and Export-DebugLogs for further analysis.

symptom: Amazon S3 scan fails: bucket encrypted with KMS and scan returns errors
rootCause: AWS role used by Purview scanner is missing KMS Decrypt permissions for encrypted S3 buckets
solution: Add KMS Decrypt permission to the AWS IAM role used by Purview. Create a new KMS policy allowing Decrypt action and attach to the scanner role.

symptom: Amazon S3 scan connection failure: 'Error found with the Role ARN' or role configuration errors
rootCause: AWS role missing required permissions (AmazonS3ReadOnlyAccess), incorrect Microsoft account ID, or missing external ID in trust relationship
solution: Verify: 1) AWS role has AmazonS3ReadOnlyAccess or minimum S3 read permissions. 2) Trust relationship has correct Microsoft account ID and external ID. 3) Bucket URL uses correct format s3://<BucketName>.

symptom: Purview Data Sharing: 'Failed to attach share' when receiving shared data
rootCause: Permission issue on target storage, unsupported storage config (wrong region/performance/redundancy), container created outside Data Sharing, non-empty folder, or same source and target storage account
solution: Check prerequisites: 1) Storage account must be in same Azure region as source. 2) Need Contributor/Owner/Storage Blob Data Owner on target. 3) Only receive into containers created via Data Sharing. 4) Ensure folder is empty. 5) Source and target must be different accounts.

symptom: Can't access shared data in target data store after share attaching succeeds
rootCause: Data sync delay after attachment, or using storage API version prior to February 2020
solution: Wait a few minutes for data to appear. Ensure using storage SDK/PowerShell/CLI/Azure Storage Explorer with API version February 2020 or later. AzCopy, ADF, and Power BI are not supported for accessing shared data.

symptom: Sensitivity labels missing in Outlook: Sensitivity button not available or labels not displayed
rootCause: User not M365 subscriber, labels not published in Purview portal, Outlook version too old, label policy distribution failed, or UseOfficeForLabelling registry set to 0 (using AIP client instead of built-in)
solution: 1) Verify user is M365 subscriber. 2) Verify labels published via Get-LabelPolicy. 3) Check DistributionStatus is Success. 4) Set UseOfficeForLabelling=1 at HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Office\16.0\Common\Security\Labels. 5) Reset by renaming %localappdata%\Microsoft\Office\CLP folder.

symptom: Sensitivity labels missing in Outlook on the web (OWA): labels not appearing when composing email
rootCause: Label misconfiguration: ContentType missing File/Email, label Disabled=True, Workload missing Exchange, Mode not Enforce, or user not in ExchangeLocation/ModernGroupLocation group
solution: Run Get-Label and Get-LabelPolicy to verify: ContentType contains File+Email, Disabled=False, Workload contains Exchange, Mode=Enforce, and user is member of specified location group. Reconfigure with Set-Label/Set-LabelPolicy if needed.

symptom: DLP policy tips not showing in Outlook/OWA despite policy being configured
rootCause: Multiple causes: policy status mismatch (shows Test it out first), duplicate rules detecting same SIT with same instance count, MailTips disabled in Outlook, or GetDLPPolicyTip call not being made
solution: 1) Ensure only one rule per SIT instance count. 2) Enable MailTips: Outlook > Options > Mail > MailTips Options > enable Policy tip notification. 3) Use Fiddler to verify GetDLPPolicyTip call and DetectedClassificationIds response. 4) Migrate legacy Exchange DLP policies to Purview compliance portal.

symptom: eDiscovery search error: The location is ambiguous
rootCause: Mailbox identifier in search locations used by duplicate or conflicting objects in Microsoft 365
solution: Connect to Security and Compliance PowerShell, run Get-Recipient -Identity to find duplicates, then remove the conflicting object.

symptom: eDiscovery search error: Recipient not found
rootCause: Synchronization delays in Microsoft 365 prevent the system from finding the recipient
solution: Run Get-Recipient -Identity to verify sync status. Wait 30 minutes for synchronization, verify recipient exists, then retry search.

symptom: eDiscovery search/export error: Maximum number of jobs for your organization are currently running
rootCause: Organization reached the limit of 50 concurrent eDiscovery jobs; export jobs typically take longer and fill quota
solution: List running exports via Get-ComplianceSearchAction -Export. Wait for jobs to complete or remove unneeded ones with Remove-ComplianceSearchAction. Then retry.

symptom: eDiscovery export error: File was not exported because it does not exist or File Not Found for SharePoint/OneDrive content
rootCause: File was renamed, moved, or deleted after search completed but before SharePoint Online index updated
solution: Reindex the SharePoint Online or OneDrive location via Manually request crawling and reindexing. Rerun the eDiscovery search to get updated results.

symptom: Audit log search error: Your request could not be completed. Please try again in Security and Compliance Center
rootCause: User not assigned View-Only Audit Logs or Audit Logs role in Exchange Online
solution: Create custom role group in Exchange admin center, add View-Only Audit Log or Audit Log role, then add affected user as member of the role group.

symptom: eDiscovery hold error: PolicySyncTimeout - It is taking longer than expected to deploy the policy
rootCause: Hold policy distribution timed out during sync process
solution: Run Set-CaseHoldPolicy policyname -RetryDistribution to redeploy. Or select Retry in the case hold page in Microsoft Purview portal.

symptom: eDiscovery hold error: FailedToOpenContainer - The mailbox or SharePoint site may not exist
rootCause: Mailbox or SharePoint site specified in hold policy no longer exists or URL changed
solution: Verify mailbox exists with Get-Mailbox, verify site exists with Get-SPOSite, check if site URL changed. Remove non-existent location from the policy.

symptom: eDiscovery hold error: SiteOutOfQuota - SharePoint site does not have enough quota for hold
rootCause: SharePoint site reached its storage quota, preventing hold policy deployment
solution: Add more storage to the site collection via Manage site collection storage limits, then redeploy with Set-CaseHoldPolicy policyname -RetryDistribution.

symptom: Cannot enable sensitivity labels on Power BI tenant or sensitivity labels not working in Power BI
rootCause: Missing Azure Information Protection license, labels not migrated to Microsoft Purview Information Protection unified labeling, or no labels defined in organization
solution: 1) Verify AIP P1/P2 license. 2) Migrate labels to unified labeling platform. 3) Define and publish sensitivity labels in Purview portal. 4) For China customers, enable rights management and add MIP Sync Service principal.

symptom: Sensitivity label greyed out in Power BI Desktop or Service, cannot apply label
rootCause: User lacks correct usage rights to change the label, or missing Power BI Pro/PPU license, or user not in security group with label permissions
solution: 1) Ensure user has Power BI Pro or PPU license. 2) Verify user belongs to security group with label permissions. 3) Contact label owner or M365 admin to grant necessary usage rights for the specific label.

symptom: Endpoint DLP policy not syncing to device: configuration status shows Not updated or Not available
rootCause: Microsoft Defender Antivirus always-on protection or behavior monitoring not enabled, device does not meet minimum OS version, or device was recently onboarded
solution: 1) Check device configuration in Purview portal > Settings > Device onboarding > Devices. 2) Enable Defender Antivirus always-on protection and behavior monitoring. 3) Verify device meets minimum OS requirements. 4) Wait up to 2 hours for policy sync after changes.

symptom: Mailbox audit events not appearing in audit log search for non-E5 users
rootCause: Mailbox audit events only return for E5 users when using unified audit log search methods (Purview portal, Search-UnifiedAuditLog, Management Activity API)
solution: Manually enable mailbox auditing: Set-Mailbox -Identity user -AuditEnabled $true. If already enabled but no results, toggle AuditEnabled to $false then back to $true.

symptom: eDiscovery hold error: RecipientTypeNotAllowed - The Recipient Type is not allowed for holds
rootCause: An Exchange location assigned to the hold policy is not a valid mailbox (e.g., distribution group, mail contact)
solution: Run Get-Recipient in Exchange Online PowerShell to verify the SMTP address is a valid mailbox. Remove non-mailbox addresses with Set-CaseHoldPolicy policyname -RemoveExchangeLocation.

symptom: eDiscovery hold deployment fails repeatedly with bulk updates: hold policy stuck in pending state
rootCause: Script updates hold policy one user at a time in a loop; first update triggers pending state, blocking subsequent additions
solution: Merge updates into single bulk request: Set-CaseHoldPolicy -Identity policyname -AddExchangeLocation User1,User2,User3 in one call. Check pending state with Get-CaseHoldPolicy -DistributionDetail before updates.

symptom: Sensitivity label not protecting exported file from Power BI, data exported without encryption
rootCause: Data exported via unsupported export path; sensitivity labels and file encryption only apply to supported export paths in Power BI
solution: Use only supported export paths for protected data. Block unsupported export paths via Power BI admin export and sharing settings to prevent data leakage.

symptom: Users cannot open/send encrypted emails or the Encrypt button is missing in Outlook and Outlook on the web
rootCause: Microsoft 365 subscription does not support Purview Message Encryption, tenant is misconfigured (IRM not enabled), or the user account lacks the required license for OME
solution: 1) Run OME diagnostic in M365 admin center. 2) Verify subscription supports OME. 3) Run Get-OwaMailboxPolicy to check IRMEnabled=True; Set-OwaMailboxPolicy -IRMEnabled $true if False. 4) Run Set-IRMConfiguration -SimplifiedClientAccessEnabled $true for missing Encrypt button. 5) Verify user license and clear cached credentials. 6) Verify Azure RMS connectivity via PowerShell HttpWebRequest to admin.na.aadrm.com

symptom: External recipient cannot open encrypted email using Purview Message Encryption - message body is blank or shows Read the message link but cannot decrypt
rootCause: Conditional Access (CA) or MFA policy in sender tenant blocks external recipient Outlook client from connecting to AIP endpoint. Or sensitivity label restricts access to internal recipients only
solution: 1) Review and adjust external-facing CA policies in sender tenant. 2) Check MFA policies that may block external authentication. 3) Verify sensitivity label permissions do not scope to internal only. 4) Recipient can use OWA via Read the message link to access OME portal

symptom: eDiscovery cannot preview, review, or export files encrypted with Microsoft encryption technologies (Azure RMS / sensitivity labels)
rootCause: The eDiscovery manager account does not have the RMS Decrypt role assigned, which is required to decrypt content during preview, review, and export
solution: Assign the RMS Decrypt role. In Purview portal this role is assigned by default to eDiscovery Manager role group. Add user to eDiscovery Manager group or manually assign RMS Decrypt role. After export, run ScanPST.exe on exported PST files

symptom: eDiscovery cannot search or decrypt email messages sent with Purview Message Encryption custom branding template
rootCause: OME custom branding templates deliver encrypted emails to the encrypted message portal instead of the recipient mailbox. eDiscovery cannot find messages not in the mailbox
solution: Known limitation by design. Use standard OME without custom branding if eDiscovery access is required. Access existing messages via OME portal directly

symptom: eDiscovery cannot preview or review encrypted files in SharePoint/OneDrive when sensitivity label has user access expiration set to non-Never value
rootCause: eDiscovery does not support decryption of files with sensitivity labels configured with User access to content expires != Never. Documents appear in search but cannot be previewed/reviewed
solution: Known limitation. Use sensitivity labels with expiration set to Never for content that must be discoverable. DKE, HYOK, on-premises RMS, S/MIME also unsupported for eDiscovery decryption

symptom: MIP client cannot label, protect, or decrypt files/emails digitally signed or encrypted with S/MIME or other third-party solutions
rootCause: MIP client only supports Microsoft encryption technologies (Azure RMS, sensitivity labels). S/MIME and third-party encryption are incompatible
solution: By design. Remove S/MIME or third-party encryption before applying MIP labels. If both needed, apply MIP label first then sign separately

symptom: MIP PowerShell module throws Object reference not set to an instance of an object error on PowerShell 7
rootCause: PowerShell 7 is not supported by the Microsoft Purview Information Protection client. MIP PowerShell module only works with Windows PowerShell 5.1
solution: Use Windows PowerShell 5.1 (powershell.exe) instead of PowerShell 7 (pwsh.exe) when running MIP cmdlets like Set-FileLabel, Get-FileStatus

symptom: MIP Scanner cannot scan .msg files with signed PDFs, files matching Trainable Classifiers or EDM SITs, or password-protected files
rootCause: Known limitations: .msg with signed PDFs unsupported; Trainable/EDM classifiers require cloud-based advanced classification unavailable in on-prem scanner; password-protected files unreadable
solution: No workaround for signed PDFs in .msg. For EDM/Trainable SITs use server-side auto-labeling in Exchange/SharePoint. For password-protected files remove password before scanning

symptom: Applying sensitivity labels fails with error after labeling policy is updated while Office app is open (coauthoring enabled)
rootCause: When labeling policy is updated while Office app is open with MIP, new labels display but applying them errors because cached policy state is stale
solution: Close and reopen the Office application to refresh the labeling policy. Labels will then work correctly

symptom: External user with Entra ID guest account gets error 'selected user account does not exist in the tenant' when opening protected doc in MIP Viewer
rootCause: MIP Viewer may not properly authenticate external guest user. Requires Adobe Acrobat DC with MIP extension for cross-tenant access
solution: 1) Install Adobe Acrobat DC with MIP extension. 2) When error appears, select Back to continue opening the document - this is expected. Note: MIP Viewer does not support personal/Windows Live accounts

symptom: SharePoint/OneDrive cannot process labeled+encrypted files containing Power Query, custom add-ins, custom XML parts, cover page properties, bibliography, or Document ID
rootCause: SharePoint content processing engine cannot handle complex file components combined with sensitivity label encryption
solution: Apply label without encryption for these files so users can open in Office for web. Or use desktop Office apps. Files labeled+encrypted only in Office for web are not affected

symptom: Sensitivity labels applied to documents before enabling sensitivity labels for SharePoint/OneDrive are not recognized and encrypted contents not processed
rootCause: SharePoint/OneDrive only process sensitivity labels for files uploaded or modified after the feature is enabled. Pre-existing labeled files not retroactively recognized
solution: Download affected files and re-upload to original location. This triggers the service to recognize labels. Alternatively open and save files to trigger re-processing

symptom: Labeled+encrypted Office file larger than 12 MB after copy/move to different SharePoint site - Sensitivity column blank, cannot open in Office for web
rootCause: SharePoint has 12 MB limit for processing labeled+encrypted files copied/moved across sites
solution: Open file using Office desktop client as workaround. Re-upload directly to destination instead of copy/move. Keep labeled+encrypted files under 12 MB for cross-site operations

symptom: Guide: Purview self-help diagnostics reference - available diagnostic tools for sensitivity labels, DLP, OME, eDiscovery, and audit
solution: See guide draft: guides/drafts/mslearn-purview-self-help-diagnostics.md

symptom: Guide: DLP policy tip HAR diagnostic - using HTTP Archive trace to diagnose DLP policy tip display issues in OWA
solution: See guide draft: guides/drafts/mslearn-dlp-policy-tip-har-diagnostic.md

symptom: Guide: Endpoint DLP diagnostic logs collection and analysis - using MDE Client Analyzer tool
solution: See guide draft: guides/drafts/mslearn-endpoint-dlp-diagnostic-logs.md

symptom: Retention policy error We cannot process your policy with ActiveDirectorySyncError
rootCause: Retention policy failed to sync with Microsoft Entra ID
solution: Retry via Purview portal or Set-RetentionCompliancePolicy -Identity name -RetryDistribution

symptom: Retention policy error Something went wrong with PolicyNotifyError
rootCause: Notification pipeline error in policy sync
solution: Retry via Purview portal or Set-RetentionCompliancePolicy -RetryDistribution

symptom: Retention policy error We ran into a problem with InternalError
rootCause: Unspecified internal error in policy sync
solution: Retry via Purview portal or Set-RetentionCompliancePolicy -RetryDistribution

symptom: Retention policy We are still processing with PolicySyncTimeout
rootCause: Policy sync did not finish within expected timeframe
solution: Retry via Purview portal or Set-RetentionCompliancePolicy -RetryDistribution

symptom: Retention policy You cannot apply a hold here with RecipientTypeNotAllowed
rootCause: Unsupported mailbox type added to retention policy
solution: Remove problematic locations. Use Set-RetentionCompliancePolicy without invalid mailbox types

symptom: Retention policy stuck in PendingDeletion state
rootCause: Unspecified error during policy deletion
solution: Remove-RetentionCompliancePolicy -Identity name -ForceDeletion

symptom: Retention policy not working after mailbox migrated from on-prem to EXO (hybrid)
rootCause: RetentionHoldEnabled set True by default during hybrid migration
solution: Set-Mailbox user -RetentionHoldEnabled $false

symptom: 401 RBAC check failed when managing disposition reviews
rootCause: User lacks RBAC permissions for disposition reviews
solution: Add to: Content Explorer Content/List Viewer, Records Management with Disposition Management role

symptom: External recipient cannot open encrypted email - blank body or Read the message link
rootCause: CA/MFA policy blocks AIP endpoint access, or sensitivity label restricts to internal
solution: Check sender tenant CA/MFA policies for external AIP access. Verify label scope. Use OME portal

symptom: Audit log events missing even with mailbox auditing on by default
rootCause: License-based retention (E5=1yr, non-E5=180d). Some events need Audit Premium. Bypass configured
solution: Get-AdminAuditLogConfig to verify. Check license retention. Run audit diagnostic. Check bypass association

symptom: Identify who deleted emails or why emails missing from mailbox
rootCause: User/admin deletion, mailbox rules, retention policies, shared mailbox access, migration
solution: Purview audit log search for deletion events. EXO PowerShell investigation. Requires Audit Logs role

symptom: eDiscovery compliance boundaries not enforced after UPN/site changes
rootCause: Attribute re-indexing takes up to 3 days. OneDrive rehoming breaks enforcement
solution: Wait 3 days. Set attributes via Set-Mailbox/Invoke-ComplianceSecurityFilterAction. No hold/DLP support

symptom: eDiscovery hold errors: dist group >1000, invalid email, mailbox not found, site inaccessible
rootCause: Data source config issues: oversized groups, stale references, access restrictions
solution: Split groups >1000. Verify addresses. Check EXO mailbox. Contact SPO admin. Retry policy

symptom: MRM retention tags not archiving/deleting emails as expected
rootCause: Wrong policy, personal tags override, compliance conflict, MFA not processing
solution: Get-MailboxFolderStatistics -IncludeOldestAndNewestItems. Check policy columns. Start-ManagedFolderAssistant

symptom: AD RMS 客户端连接 AD RMS 服务器失败，MSIPC 日志报 HTTP Status code 12157。禁用 TLS 1.0/1.1 后客户端无法获取许可证或认证。
rootCause: .NET Framework 默认使用 TLS 1.0；AD RMS 是 .NET 应用；禁用 TLS 1.0/1.1 后 .NET 无法协商 TLS 1.2，导致连接被拒绝。
solution: 1) 设置注册表：HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\.NETFramework\v2.0.50727 和 v4.0.30319 下设 SystemDefaultTlsVersions=1 和 SchUseStrongCrypto=1（64位同时设 Wow6432Node）。2) 在 SCHANNEL 注册表启用 TLS 1.2 Client/Server。3) 安装 .NET Framework 更新（< 4.6.2 需升级）。4) 重启 AD RMS 服务器。
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/AD RMS/How To: AD RMS/How to: Configure AD RMS for TLS 1.2

symptom: AD RMS 权限授予了 AD 组，但组成员访问权限间歇性失败——有时能访问，有时同一用户访问被拒绝。
rootCause: AD RMS 向 Global Catalog (GC) 服务器查询组成员关系。GC 只复制 domain global/local 组的成员关系给本域 DC，不向其他域 GC 复制。当 RMS 恰好查询到不含该组成员信息的 GC 时，结果为空导致访问失败。
solution: 1) 使用 Universal 组代替 domain global/local 组（Universal 组成员关系复制到所有 GC）。2) 或通过注册表控制 RMS 使用的 GC 列表：HKLM\Software\Microsoft\DRMS，值名 GC，类型 REG_SZ，逗号分隔的 GC FQDN 列表。
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/AD RMS/Troubleshooting Scenarios: AD RMS/Scenario: AD Group Lookups not Working as Expected

symptom: AD RMS 管理控制台显示 SCP 未注册；尝试注册 AD RMS Service Connection Point (SCP) 时报错。
rootCause: (1) AD RMS 管理员账号不是 AD Enterprise Admins 成员，无权写入 AD 配置容器；(2) AD 配置容器中 CN=RightsManagementServices 下 SCP 子容器缺失。
solution: 1) 确认账号已加入 Enterprise Admins。2) 若 SCP 容器缺失：ADSI Edit 连接 Configuration 命名上下文 → CN=Services\CN=RightsManagementServices → 右键新建 container，值名 SCP。3) 在 AD RMS MMC 服务器属性 SCP 选项卡完成注册。4) 验证 serviceBindingInformation 属性已填充。
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/AD RMS/Troubleshooting Scenarios: AD RMS/Scenario: Failed to register the AD RMS SCP

symptom: 配置 AD RMS 角色时出现 SQL Server 错误：The File is compressed but does not reside in a read-only database or filegroup. The file must be decompressed.
rootCause: SQL Server 数据库文件所在的 NTFS 文件夹启用了 NTFS 压缩（Compress contents to save disk space），SQL Server 不支持对运行中的数据库文件进行 NTFS 压缩。
solution: 找到 SQL 数据库文件目录（如 C:\MSSQL\MSSQL14.MSSQLSERVER\MSSQL\DATA\），右键属性 → 高级 → 取消勾选 Compress contents to save disk space，然后重新运行 AD RMS 配置向导。
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/AD RMS/Troubleshooting Scenarios: AD RMS/Scenario: Installation Error after Confirmation step

symptom: AD RMS 安装配置时报错：User does not have permission to alter the database。
rootCause: 安装账号缺少必要权限：需同时具备本地 Administrator、SQL SA（SysAdmin）、AD Enterprise Administrator 三项权限。
solution: 为安装账号授予本地 Admin + SQL SA + AD Enterprise Admin。安装完成后可撤销 SQL SA 和 Enterprise Admin 权限。
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/AD RMS/Troubleshooting Scenarios: AD RMS/Scenario: Installation Error after Confirmation step

symptom: 尝试更改 AD RMS 服务账号或重置其密码时出现权限错误，变更向导失败。
rootCause: (1) 执行账号权限不足（需本地 Admin + AD RMS Enterprise Admin + SQL SysAdmin）；(2) 新服务账号是 AD RMS 数据库 DB Owner（不允许）；(3) Admin Share 被禁用，向导无法访问 \\server\C$\inetpub\wwwroot\_wmcs\admin；(4) 未在集群每个节点各执行一次。
solution: 1) 确认执行账号具备本地 Admin + AD RMS Enterprise Admin + SQL SysAdmin。2) AD RMS MMC 连接到本地服务器名（非集群名）。3) 新服务账号不得是 AD RMS 数据库 DB Owner。4) 检查并启用 Admin Share。5) 在每个集群节点各执行一次。6) 若仍失败，抓取 Procmon 日志升级 SME。
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/AD RMS/Troubleshooting Scenarios: AD RMS/Scenario: Unable to change AD RMS Service account

solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-b-learn-default-iis-settings.md

symptom: AD RMS Health and Troubleshooting reports fail to open in the AD RMS MMC; the GUI link to Report Viewer attempts to access a non-existent location
rootCause: Report Viewer redistributable is not installed on the AD RMS server; .NET Framework 3.5 feature required by Report Viewer is not enabled on the server
solution: 1. Add .NET Framework 3.5 Features via Server Manager (Add Roles and Features Wizard). 2. Download and install Report Viewer Redistributable 2008 SP1 (GDIPLUS.DLL Security Update) from https://www.microsoft.com/en-in/download/details.aspx?id=3203. 3. Close and reopen the AD RMS MMC — Health and Troubleshooting reports will now be available.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/AD RMS/How To: AD RMS/How To: Add AD RMS Reports Functionality

solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-a-ds-lookup-caching.md

symptom: AD RMS Kerberos authentication configuration in IIS — SPN registration, delegation, and kernel-mode authentication setup
solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-a-iis-kerberos-configuration-for-ad-rms.md

symptom: Set-AipServiceTemplateProperty fails with error when attempting to set AD RMS-imported templates to Published status in AIP after AD RMS migration
rootCause: AD RMS templates imported via TPD are cryptographically tied to that imported AD RMS key; publishing them requires the AD RMS key to be active. AD RMS keys are often Crypto Mode 1 (1024-bit) which is not recommended as the active AIP key, creating a conflict.
solution: Recreate AD RMS templates as independent AIP templates: 1. Export template XML files from AD RMS cache (%localappdata%\Microsoft\MSIPC\Templates) or from archived AIP via Export-AipServiceTemplate. 2. Edit each XML: replace ANYONE with AllStaff-7184AB3F-CCD1-46F3-8233-3E09E9CF0E66@<tenant>.onmicrosoft.com. 3. Replace the first GUID in each XML with a newly generated GUID. 4. Import via Import-AipServiceTemplate -Path <xml>. 5. Publish via Set-AipServiceTemplateProperty -TemplateId <new-guid> -Status Published. New templates are independent of the archived AD RMS key.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/AD RMS/How To: AD RMS/How To: Importing AD RMS templates into AIP

solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-a-software-csp-based-ad-rms-key-migration.md

symptom: Desktop applications (Word, Excel, Outlook) fail to apply AIP encryption/sensitivity label protection; encryption is silently blocked even when the user has the correct label assigned
rootCause: An AIP Service Onboarding Control Policy (OCP) is configured with restrictions — e.g., SecurityGroupObjectId limits encryption to a specific Entra security group, or UseRmsUserLicense requires an RMS license — and the user does not meet those requirements
solution: Check OCP with `Get-AipServiceOnboardingControlPolicy | fl`. Adjust via `Set-AipServiceOnboardingControlPolicy` to include the user (add to security group, assign RMS license, or remove OCP). Note: OCP does NOT affect opening/consuming encrypted content, and does NOT affect web apps (OWA, Web Word).
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/AIP Service/How To: AipService/How To: Check AIP Service Configuration

symptom: AIP Service Super User feature not working — Super User account cannot decrypt AIP-protected content despite being configured as a super user
rootCause: Super user was configured using the user's UPN (User Principal Name) instead of the primary SMTP email address; AIP Service requires the primary SMTP address for super user configuration
solution: Reconfigure AIP Super User using the user's primary SMTP email address (not UPN). Use `Add-AipServiceSuperUser -EmailAddress user@domain.com` with the SMTP address. Verify current super users with `Get-AipServiceSuperUser`.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/AIP Service/How To: AipService/How To: Check AIP Service Configuration

symptom: Customer needs to move AIP Service BYOK encryption key from one Azure Key Vault (AKV) to a different AKV (e.g., different subscription or region); AIP service loses access to encrypted content after the old vault is decommissioned
rootCause: AIP service key identifier is associated with a specific AKV URL; migrating key material to a new vault does not automatically update the AIP service's reference to the new vault location
solution: 1. Leave original BYOK key and AKV intact. 2. Import the original key material from on-premises HSM into new AKV. 3. Configure new AKV to allow AIP service access. 4. Use `Convert-AipServiceKeyToKeyVault` to point the existing key identifier to the new AKV key. 5. Use `Set-AipServiceKeyProperties` to set the new key as active. 6. Use `Use-AipServiceKeyVaultKey` to configure RMS to use the new active key. If issues arise, use `Convert-AipServiceKeyToKeyVault` to repoint back to the old AKV URL as rollback.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/AIP Service/How To: AipService/How To: Migrate AIP Service BYOK key vault keys

symptom: After migrating from AD RMS to Azure AIP, newer Office clients (Office 16.x) cannot access AIP/RMS-licensed protected content; licensing redirection fails on modern Office builds
rootCause: Newer Office client versions use MIP SDK instead of MSIPC for rights management operations; MIP SDK has known issues with licensing redirection from AD RMS to AIP
solution: Create registry key `HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Office\16.0\Common\DRM` with Value Name `PreferredRmsPackage` (REG_DWORD = 1) on affected client machines to force the Office client to fall back to MSIPC for licensing operations instead of MIP SDK.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/AIP Service/How To: AipService/How to: AIP Service Tenant Migration

symptom: Users unable to decrypt/open AIP-protected files or emails after a complete EntraID tenant-to-tenant migration (tenant A → tenant B)
rootCause: One or more of: (1) AIP key from old tenant not imported to new tenant; (2) DNS domain from old tenant not added as verified domain in new tenant; (3) user ProxyAddress attribute does not include original email address; (4) DNS/Registry-based licensing redirection not configured on client machines
solution: Verify: (1) `Connect-AIPService` to new tenant + `Get-AIPServiceKeys` to confirm keys imported; (2) DNS domain from old tenant added as verified in new tenant; (3) user ProxyAddress contains original email; (4) configure DNS or registry-based licensing redirection (see: migrate-from-ad-rms-phase3 article for redirection steps, substituting AD RMS URL with old AIP tenant URL). To find old AIP URL: run `Get-AIPServiceConfiguration` on old tenant, or open old protected file in Notepad and inspect licensing URL.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/AIP Service/How To: AipService/How to: AIP Service Tenant Migration

symptom: After partial tenant migration (only users moved to a new EntraID tenant while email domain and AIP keys remain in the old tenant), migrated users cannot access old AIP-protected documents or emails; ad-hoc protected content (Do Not Forward / Encrypt Only) is completely inaccessible to migrated users
rootCause: Migrated users have new email addresses in the new tenant; ad-hoc protected files are encrypted with old email addresses and cannot be re-resolved by new user identities. For label-based protection, the new tenant domain is not included in the label encryption settings.
solution: 1. Edit encryption labels in the old tenant to add the new tenant domain in the label encryption setting. 2. For ad-hoc protected content (DNF/Encrypt Only): decrypt using an AIP Super User account from the old tenant, then reshare with migrated users' new identities. 3. If Conditional Access in the old tenant blocks access: invite migrated users as Guest users in the old tenant. CSS scope: assist on specific steps only; full migration planning requires Consulting Services.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/AIP Service/How To: AipService/How to: AIP Service Tenant Migration

symptom: PowerShell module (AIPService, or other Microsoft cloud PS modules) fails to connect to Microsoft cloud endpoints with TLS/SSL errors; error indicates connection to cloud service rejected
rootCause: .NET Framework version 4.6 or older is installed; old .NET Framework does not default to TLS 1.2, but Microsoft cloud endpoints reject SSLv3, TLS 1.0, and TLS 1.1 connections
solution: Upgrade .NET Framework to version 4.8 from https://dotnet.microsoft.com/en-us/download/dotnet-framework/net48 (download web installer, run, reboot). If upgrade is not possible, add .NET-specific registry settings to force TLS 1.2 — see TSG: 'Scenario: Connector issue after disabling TLS 1.0 and 1.1' at https://dev.azure.com/ASIM-Security/Compliance/_wiki/wikis/Information%20Protection/11298/.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/AIP Service/Learn: AipSerivce/Learn: .NET Framework

symptom: Connect-AIPService PowerShell command fails or not found; AIPService module missing or outdated
rootCause: AIPService PowerShell module is not installed or is on an outdated version
solution: Run `Get-Module -ListAvailable -Name aipservice` to check installation. Install/update with: `Install-Module -Name AIPService` from an administrative PowerShell. Verify build is current per PowerShell Gallery.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/AIP Service/Troubleshooting Scenarios: AipService/Scenario: Connect-AIPService fails

symptom: Connect-AIPService fails when run from PowerShell 7
rootCause: AIPService PowerShell module does not support PowerShell 7
solution: Run Connect-AIPService from a PowerShell 5 (Windows PowerShell) window instead of PowerShell 7.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/AIP Service/Troubleshooting Scenarios: AipService/Scenario: Connect-AIPService fails

symptom: Connect-AIPService fails with authentication error; user has Global Administrator role but connection still denied
rootCause: Connect-AIPService requires the user to be a DIRECT member of the Global Administrator role. GA privilege granted via group membership is NOT sufficient.
solution: Add the user as a direct member of the Global Administrator role in Entra ID. Alternatively, grant the user AIP service roles via the Add-AipServiceRoleBasedAdministrator cmdlet (https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/aipservice/add-aipservicerolebasedadministrator).
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/AIP Service/Troubleshooting Scenarios: AipService/Scenario: Connect-AIPService fails

symptom: Connect-AIPService fails with SSL or network error; credential prompt never appears (discover.aadrm.com unreachable); TLS handshake error
rootCause: TLS 1.2 not enabled on client machine, unsupported cipher suites, or firewall/proxy blocking connections to AIP endpoints (*.aadrm.com)
solution: 1) Enable TLS 1.2 or higher on the machine. 2) Verify required cipher suites are enabled. 3) Ensure firewall allows connectivity to AIP endpoints per requirements: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/information-protection/requirements#firewalls-and-network-infrastructure. 4) Collect Fiddler trace to inspect TLS handshake for *.aadrm.com. Run `Connect-AipService -Verbose` to capture detailed error and correlationID.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/AIP Service/Troubleshooting Scenarios: AipService/Scenario: Connect-AIPService fails

symptom: Users in a group that was deleted and recreated with the same email/UPN cannot open encrypted or protected files; group membership appears correct
rootCause: AIP service maintains a 30-day cache mapping email address to AAD ObjectID. When a group is deleted and recreated with the same email, the old ObjectID remains cached. The new group receives no rights because the cache returns the stale ObjectID.
solution: Option 1: Wait 30 days for the ObjectID cache to expire naturally. Option 2 (urgent): Create an ICM to the AIP PG to clear the cache manually. Provide: TenantID, Group Name, UPN/Alias, ObjectID of new group, Email ID. ICM template: https://portal.microsofticm.com/imp/v3/incidents/create?tmpl=x3SY2b
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/AIP Service/Troubleshooting Scenarios: AipService/Scenario: Recreated Group in Entra with Same Email Unable to Access Encrypted Items

symptom: Use-AipServiceKeyVaultKey or Set-AipServiceKeyProperties fails when switching to or activating a BYOK key; the BYOK key was previously deleted from Key Vault
rootCause: AIP service cannot access the previously active BYOK key because it was deleted from Key Vault. The service requires access to the active key to complete any key switch.
solution: If the BYOK key was deleted from Key Vault, escalation is required. Collect verbose error output and correlationID, then escalate the case to AIP PG.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/AIP Service/Troubleshooting Scenarios: AipService/Scenario: Unable to Activate AIPService keys (BYOK or MMK)

symptom: Use-AipServiceKeyVaultKey fails; AIP service cannot access BYOK key in Azure Key Vault even though the key exists
rootCause: AIP service does not have the correct permissions on the Key Vault key
solution: Verify and grant AIP service the correct Key Vault permissions. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/information-protection/byok-price-restrictions#enabling-key-authorization-using-powershell
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/AIP Service/Troubleshooting Scenarios: AipService/Scenario: Unable to Activate AIPService keys (BYOK or MMK)

symptom: Use-AipServiceKeyVaultKey fails; BYOK key in Key Vault is present but activation fails
rootCause: The BYOK key length is not supported by the AIP service
solution: Check supported key length requirements at: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/information-protection/byok-price-restrictions#key-length-requirements — use a key with a supported key length.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/AIP Service/Troubleshooting Scenarios: AipService/Scenario: Unable to Activate AIPService keys (BYOK or MMK)

symptom: Import-AipServiceTpd fails; error related to password or decryption when importing TPD from AD RMS or another AIP tenant
rootCause: Incorrect password was provided for the encrypted TPD file during import
solution: Re-export the TPD with a new known password, then retry Import-AipServiceTpd with the correct password. Always run with `-Verbose` to capture correlationID on failure.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/AIP Service/Troubleshooting Scenarios: AipService/Scenario: Unable to Import TPD to AIP Service

symptom: Import-AipServiceTpd fails when attempting to import a TPD to a second tenant; TPD was already imported to another tenant
rootCause: The same TPD cannot be imported to multiple tenants simultaneously
solution: Delete the TPD from the existing tenant first before importing it to the new tenant.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/AIP Service/Troubleshooting Scenarios: AipService/Scenario: Unable to Import TPD to AIP Service

symptom: User cannot open DKE (Double Key Encryption) protected file; authentication fails when consuming DKE protected content
rootCause: Misconfiguration in DKE appsettings.json (incorrect user email or LDAP server config) OR the AAD App Registration Application ID URI is not set to the DKE service URL
solution: 1) Verify user email and LDAP server configuration in DKE `appsettings.json`. 2) In AAD App Registration > Expose an API, set the Application ID URI to match the DKE service URL. 3) Ensure `JwtAudience` in appsettings.json matches the URL in AAD App Registration and in the Sensitivity Label. See guide: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-a-dke-consume-protected-content.md
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/AIP Service/Troubleshooting Scenarios: AipService/Scenario: Unable to consume DKE protected content

symptom: Cannot save DKE (Double Key Encryption) protected Office file; error: 'Word cannot save or create this file'; MSIPC log shows 'Double key is not flighted' and 'Client not part of any flighting ring'
rootCause: DKE flighting is not enabled in the Office/MSIPC registry on the client machine
solution:
Add the following registry keys on the client:
[HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\WOW6432Node\Microsoft\MSIPC\flighting] "DoubleKeyProtection"=dword:00000001
[HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\MSIPC\flighting] "DoubleKeyProtection"=dword:00000001
Reference: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-365/compliance/double-key-encryption#enable-dke-in-your-client
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/AIP Service/Troubleshooting Scenarios: AipService/Scenario: Unable to save DKE protected file

symptom: Cannot save DKE protected file; save operation fails; client cannot browse the DKE service URL
rootCause: DKE hosting service is down or client firewall/network is blocking access to the DKE service URL
solution: 1) Check the DKE hosting service (IIS or Azure App Service) is running. 2) Verify the client can successfully browse the DKE service URL from a browser. 3) Review client-side firewall and network rules to allow the DKE service URL.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/AIP Service/Troubleshooting Scenarios: AipService/Scenario: Unable to save DKE protected file

symptom: Cannot save DKE protected file; DKE URL configuration appears correct but save still fails
rootCause: DKE URL, KeyName, and JwtAudience are case-sensitive. A case mismatch between appsettings.json, the DKE Sensitivity Label setting, and Azure AD App Registration causes failures.
solution: Ensure that the KeyName and JwtAudience in appsettings.json match EXACTLY (case-sensitive) with the URL specified in the DKE Label setting and in the Azure AD App Registration.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/AIP Service/Troubleshooting Scenarios: AipService/Scenario: Unable to save DKE protected file

symptom: Cannot save DKE protected file; DKE service URL is configured in the sensitivity label but save fails
rootCause: The sensitivity label configuration is missing the key name at the end of the DKE service URL — only the base URL was entered, not the full key endpoint URL
solution: In the sensitivity label DKE configuration, enter the full endpoint URL including the key name appended at the end (e.g., https://yourservice.azurewebsites.net/yourKeyName). The documentation states to enter 'the endpoint URL for your key', meaning the key name must be included.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/AIP Service/Troubleshooting Scenarios: AipService/Scenario: Unable to save DKE protected file

symptom: DKE protected content cannot be consumed; authentication fails; multiple configuration areas need verification
solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-a-dke-consume-protected-content.md
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/AIP Service/Troubleshooting Scenarios: AipService/Scenario: Unable to consume DKE protected content

symptom: Activity Explorer event not showing for user with Admin Unit (AU) configured; user cannot see events outside their assigned AU
rootCause: By design: users assigned an Admin Unit can only see Activity Explorer events for users within their AU. Global Admins can see all events.
solution: Expected behavior. If the user needs to see all events: (1) make them a Global Admin, or (2) modify the AU configuration to include the relevant users/policies.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Activity Explorer/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Activity Explorer/Scenario: Event not showing in Activity Explorer

symptom: Export-ActivityExplorerData PowerShell command is not recognized or cannot be found
rootCause: ExchangePowerShell module is not installed on the machine
solution: Install the ExchangePowerShell module, then run Connect-IPPSSession with an admin account. Refer: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/exchange/connect-to-scc-powershell
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Activity Explorer/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Activity Explorer/Scenario: Export-ActivityExplorerData cmdlet not working

symptom: Export-ActivityExplorerData cmdlet returns error when using filters, or returns no data / incorrect data
rootCause: Incorrect filter parameters (unsupported filter type), wrong activity value string, or incorrect date/time format passed to the command
solution: (1) Check supported filters at https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/exchange/export-activityexplorerdata; (2) Run with -Verbose for detailed error output; (3) Check syntax and date/time format against documented examples; (4) Try with only user+date range first to isolate filter issue; (5) If UI shows data but cmdlet does not, collect Fiddler trace and escalate.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Activity Explorer/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Activity Explorer/Scenario: Export-ActivityExplorerData cmdlet not working

symptom: Number of labeled items in Activity Explorer is different (usually higher) than in Content Explorer
rootCause: By design: different metrics. Content Explorer counts files that currently have a label (one entry per file). Activity Explorer counts labeling actions/activities — one file can generate multiple events (applied, upgraded, auto-applied) and therefore multiple Activity Explorer entries.
solution: Expected behavior. Explain to customer: Content Explorer = current state (how many files have a label); Activity Explorer = activity log (how many labeling actions occurred).
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Activity Explorer/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Activity Explorer/Scenario: Information incorrect for an event in activity explorer

symptom: HowApplied field shows value 'none' in Activity Explorer event
rootCause: By design: for certain operation types (e.g., CopiedToUSB), the HowApplied field is not applicable and defaults to 'None'
solution: Expected behavior. The value 'None' is intentional for operations where HowApplied has no meaningful value.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Activity Explorer/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Activity Explorer/Scenario: Information incorrect for an event in activity explorer

symptom: LabelEventType field shows value 'none' in Activity Explorer
rootCause: By design: LabelEventType is 'none' when there is no label event associated with the operation (e.g., File Discover event)
solution: Expected behavior.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Activity Explorer/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Activity Explorer/Scenario: Information incorrect for an event in activity explorer

symptom: FileName field in Activity Explorer shows the email subject instead of an actual file name
rootCause: By design: when a DLP rule match occurs in Exchange Online and there is no attachment, the FileName field is populated with the email subject
solution: Expected behavior.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Activity Explorer/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Activity Explorer/Scenario: Information incorrect for an event in activity explorer

symptom: 'Policy' and 'Rule' columns are blank for an Endpoint event in Activity Explorer (e.g., file copied to removable media)
rootCause: 'File Created on Removable Media' event is an IRM (Information Rights Management) event for unclassified files — it does not trigger a DLP policy, so Policy/Rule columns are empty. 'File Copied to Removable Media' (for classified files) is a DLP event and shows Policy/Rule data.
solution: Expected behavior. Differentiate: 'File Created on Removable Media' (unclassified, IRM, no Policy/Rule) vs. 'File Copied to Removable Media' (classified, DLP, shows Policy/Rule).
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Activity Explorer/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Activity Explorer/Scenario: Information incorrect for an event in activity explorer

symptom: Activity Explorer displays label GUIDs instead of label display names for activities involving deleted labels
rootCause: When a label is deleted from the tenant, its GUID remains in the file header. Activity Explorer cannot resolve the display name for a deleted label and shows the raw GUID.
solution: Known behavior. The GUID will continue to appear until the item is relabeled with an active label.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Activity Explorer/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Activity Explorer/Scenario: Information incorrect for an event in activity explorer

symptom: Filter not working in Activity Explorer; filter shows no values or does not narrow results as expected
rootCause: A filter can only surface values that are present in the corresponding column. If the column has no populated values for that event, the filter dropdown will also be empty.
solution: Click 'Customize Columns' and verify that values appear in the column you are trying to filter. If the column is empty, the filter will also be empty (expected behavior). If incorrect data is showing in the column, refer to 'Scenario: Information incorrect for an event in activity explorer'.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Activity Explorer/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Activity Explorer/Scenario: Web page not functioning correctly for Activity Explorer

solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-b-activity-explorer-required-information.md

solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-b-client-side-auto-labeling-required-information.md
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Auto Labeling/Client Side Auto Labeling/Required Information: Client Side Auto Labeling

symptom: Client Side Auto Labeling (CSAL) not applying in Outlook, Word, Excel online, or OWA — label is not automatically applied or recommended
rootCause: User is not entitled for Auto Labeling — missing MIP_S_CLP2 capability. Requires Microsoft E5, Office 365 E5, Microsoft 365 E5 Compliance, or Office 365 Advanced Compliance plan.
solution: Verify user license via Assist365 (assist.microsoft.com or eu.assist.microsoft.com) — check for MIP_S_CLP2 capability and plans. If missing, customer must acquire Microsoft E5 / Office 365 E5 / M365 E5 Compliance / Office 365 Advanced Compliance. Reference: Microsoft 365 Compliance Licensing Comparison PDF, 'Apply Sensitivity Labels Automatically' feature section.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Auto Labeling/Client Side Auto Labeling/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Client Side Auto Labeling/Scenario: Client Side Auto Labeling not applying correctly

symptom: Client Side Auto Labeling in OWA: network trace shows label API response for the affected label is missing the AutoLabeling_SensitiveTypeIds field, even though conditions are set in IPPSSession PowerShell
rootCause: Label/DLP APIs are not publishing auto label settings correctly — conditions exist in policy configuration but are not reflected in the label API response consumed by OWA.
solution: Escalate to DLP team. Collect: (1) network trace showing the label API response (https://outlook.office365.com/compliancepolicy/api/v1.0/user/label) with missing AutoLabeling_SensitiveTypeIds, and (2) AugLoop WebSocket calls. Confirm policy Conditions field is set via IPPSSession before escalating.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Auto Labeling/Client Side Auto Labeling/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Client Side Auto Labeling/Scenario: Client Side Auto Labeling not applying correctly

symptom: OWA Client Side Auto Labeling not triggering: network trace shows no AugLoop_SecurityClp_LabellingAnnotation WebSocket calls being made, meaning the labeling service is never evaluated
rootCause: Either (1) user lacks the appropriate auto labeling license (MIP_S_CLP2), or (2) OWA client is not calling the sensitivity label service — a client-side OWA issue.
solution: Re-verify user license and auto-labeling capabilities via Assist365. If license is confirmed present, the issue is OWA not calling the labeling service — route to OWA team. Include failed AugLoop WebSocket connection screenshots in escalation.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Auto Labeling/Client Side Auto Labeling/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Client Side Auto Labeling/Scenario: Client Side Auto Labeling not applying correctly

symptom: Support routing and boundaries for Client Side Auto Labeling issues (Outlook, Word, Excel, OWA sensitivity label auto-application)
solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-a-client-side-auto-labeling-support-boundaries.md
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Auto Labeling/Client Side Auto Labeling/Support Boundaries: Client Side Auto Labeling
