solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-a-server-side-auto-labeling-support-boundaries.md
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Auto Labeling/Server Side Auto Labeling/Support Boundaries: Server Side Auto Labeling

symptom: Server Side Auto Labeling simulation not showing expected items: email missing from simulation results, or document not appearing in Items for Review
solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-a-server-side-auto-labeling-simulation-not-showing.md
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Auto Labeling/Server Side Auto Labeling/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Server Side Auto Labeling/Scenario: Server Side Auto Labeling Simulation not showing expected item

symptom: Server Side Auto Labeling policy scoped to SharePoint/OneDrive (SPO/ODB) with Admin Units assigned is broken or not applying labels correctly
rootCause: SharePoint/OneDrive does not support Admin Units in Auto Labeling policies. Assigning Admin Units to an SPO-scoped Auto Label Policy breaks the policy.
solution: Remove Admin Unit scope from the Auto Label Policy for SharePoint/OneDrive locations. Admin Units are only supported for Exchange and OneDrive when not combined with SPO. Reference: SPO doesn't support AUs per DLP Admin Units wiki.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Auto Labeling/Server Side Auto Labeling/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Server Side Auto Labeling/Scenario: Server Side Auto Labeling not applying correctly

symptom: Server Side Auto Labeling Exchange policy not evaluating emails at all — policy missing from AgentInfo in Extended Message Trace, email sender not getting auto-labeled
rootCause: Policy is in simulation mode (not Enforce), OR policy scope includes recipient instead of sender. External senders require policy scoped to 'All' — SSAL only evaluates when the email SENDER is in the policy scope.
solution: (1) Set policy mode to Enforce (auto labeling does not apply in simulation mode). (2) Verify policy is scoped to the email sender, not recipient. (3) For external senders, policy must be scoped to 'All'. Get policy GUID via: Get-AutoSensitivityLabelPolicy 'POLICYNAME' | fl Guid
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Auto Labeling/Server Side Auto Labeling/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Server Side Auto Labeling/Scenario: Server Side Auto Labeling not applying correctly

symptom: Server Side Auto Labeling policy matched (confirmed via AgentInfo or Test-DlpPolicies report) but existing manually-applied sensitivity label on email or document is not being overridden by the auto label policy
rootCause: SSAL override of manually applied labels is not supported for SharePoint/OneDrive by design. For Exchange, override requires the 'Override manually applied labels with lower priority' setting to be explicitly enabled on the policy.
solution: For Exchange: enable 'Override manually applied labels with lower priority' in the auto label policy settings. For SharePoint/OneDrive: this is by design — SSAL cannot override manually applied labels on SPO/ODB. Reference: learn.microsoft.com/en-us/purview/apply-sensitivity-label-automatically#will-an-existing-label-be-overridden
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Auto Labeling/Server Side Auto Labeling/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Server Side Auto Labeling/Scenario: Server Side Auto Labeling not applying correctly

symptom: Server Side Auto Labeling simulation is stuck and never completes; auto label policy simulation state does not progress after extended time
rootCause: Transient service issue causing simulation to stall.
solution: Restart the simulation from the Purview portal. Wait approximately 12 hours for completion. If still stuck after 12 hours, get assistance and escalate to PG — collect policy and rule output using Get-AutoSensitivityLabelPolicy and Get-AutoSensitivityLabelRule PowerShell commands (see Required Information guide).
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Auto Labeling/Server Side Auto Labeling/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Server Side Auto Labeling/Scenario: Server Side Auto Labeling simulation is stuck

symptom: Server Side Auto Labeling simulation metrics show incorrect file count; simulation results do not reflect all expected files
rootCause: Known platform limitation: simulation only shows up to 100 files per site/user per policy in simulation results
solution: Inform customer this is a known limitation. Simulation metrics are capped at 100 files per site/user per policy; this cannot be changed. Escalate with required information if business impact is significant.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Auto Labeling/Server Side Auto Labeling/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Server Side Auto Labeling/Scenario: Server Side auto labeling metrics are not correct

symptom: Server Side Auto Labeling 'Files to be labeled' count shows 0 or stale/incorrect number
rootCause: 'Files to be labeled' initially starts at 0 and is updated every 24 hours; auto label progress by top sites may also differ from total count
solution: Advise customer to wait 24 hours for the count to refresh. This is expected behavior by design.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Auto Labeling/Server Side Auto Labeling/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Server Side Auto Labeling/Scenario: Server Side auto labeling metrics are not correct

symptom: Content Explorer shows incorrect or missing count for sensitivity labels or sensitive information types; count is lower than expected
rootCause: Items with sensitivity labels or SITs may take up to 7 days to populate in Content Explorer; items labeled by server-side auto-labeling policies may take up to 14 days
solution: Verify that sufficient time has passed (7 days for general items, 14 days for server-side auto-labeled items) before expecting accurate counts in Content Explorer
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Data Explorer/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Data Explorer/Scenario: Content Explorer count incorrect

symptom: Content Explorer item count does not match live query results from Microsoft Graph or direct data source queries
rootCause: Content Explorer data is aggregated once per day and is not real-time; it reflects the state at the last daily update, not current live data
solution: This is expected behavior. Explain to customer that Content Explorer is not a real-time monitoring tool. If the discrepancy persists after 24 hours, collect network trace and verify /dgs/item/GetTypePathAggregatesItems API response
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Data Explorer/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Data Explorer/Scenario: Content Explorer count incorrect

symptom: Error when searching Exchange or Teams data in Content Explorer using a partial user email address
rootCause: Content Explorer search for Exchange/Teams workload requires the full mailbox email address; partial email is not supported
solution: Search with the full email address of the mailbox (e.g., user@domainname.com) instead of a partial address. Refer: Get started with content explorer - Filter section on Microsoft Learn
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Data Explorer/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Data Explorer/Scenario: Error while searching an item in Content Explorer

symptom: Error when searching SharePoint/OneDrive data in Content Explorer using a partial site name
rootCause: SPO/OD search in Content Explorer requires the full site name; partial site name search is not supported
solution: Search using the full site name instead of a partial name. Refer: Get started with content explorer - Filter section on Microsoft Learn
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Data Explorer/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Data Explorer/Scenario: Error while searching an item in Content Explorer

symptom: Export-ContentExplorerData cmdlet is not recognized when running in PowerShell
rootCause: The ExchangePowerShell module is not installed, or Connect-IPPSSession has not been run to establish a Security & Compliance PowerShell session
solution: Install the ExchangePowerShell module. Run Connect-IPPSSession with an administrator account to connect to Security & Compliance PowerShell. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/exchange/connect-to-scc-powershell
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Data Explorer/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Data Explorer/Scenario: Export-ContentExplorerData cmdlet not working

symptom: Export-ContentExplorerData returns no data, incomplete data, or truncated results; less than expected records returned
rootCause: Default query without -PageSize returns up to 100 records; maximum 10,000 records can be retrieved per single query; without -PageCookie parameter, pagination is not handled for large datasets
solution: Use -PageSize parameter when total records exceed 100. For datasets larger than 10,000 records, use -PageSize combined with -PageCookie in a loop script. Verify filters/parameters match documentation syntax. Collect Fiddler trace with -Verbose flag for debugging.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Data Explorer/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Data Explorer/Scenario: Export-ContentExplorerData cmdlet not working

symptom: User is unable to access the Content Explorer page in the Microsoft Purview compliance portal
rootCause: User does not have the required role assigned: Content Explorer List viewer (to see items in list view) or Content Explorer Content viewer (to view item contents)
solution: Assign 'Content Explorer List viewer' role for list access, or 'Content Explorer Content viewer' role for content preview access. Refer: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/purview/data-classification-content-explorer
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Data Explorer/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Data Explorer/Scenario: Information not accessible in content explorer

symptom: Encrypted file appears in Data Explorer/Content Explorer list but user cannot preview or view the file contents in the preview pane
rootCause: Encrypted files are shown in Data Explorer but their contents cannot be rendered in the preview pane by design; the encryption protects content from being displayed inline
solution: This is expected behavior for encrypted files. Verify user has appropriate decryption permissions if they should be able to preview. Collect network trace if the behavior appears abnormal.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Data Explorer/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Data Explorer/Scenario: Information not accessible in content explorer

symptom: Remove-FileLabel -RemoveProtection 无法从 PST 中移除加密保护
rootCause: 缺少 EnableContainerSupport 高级策略设置，或运行 cmdlet 的用户未被授权为 AIP Service Super User
solution: 1) 在最高优先级 MIP 标签策略上配置高级设置 EnableContainerSupport；2) 将用户 UPN 添加为 AIP Service Super User；参考：https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/information-protection/configure-super-users
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/MPIP Client/How to: MPIP Client/How To: Decrypt a PST with PowerShell

symptom: AIP Super User 无法解密外部租户加密的邮件（PST 中包含其他租户发送的加密邮件）
rootCause: AIP Service Super User 权限仅适用于本租户保护的内容，无法解密由外部租户保护的内容
solution: 这是已知限制（design limitation）。外部租户加密的邮件无法通过 Remove-FileLabel 解密。需联系源租户进行解密操作。PST 最大支持 5 GB。
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/MPIP Client/How to: MPIP Client/How To: Decrypt a PST with PowerShell

symptom: 需要使用 PowerShell 从 PST 中批量移除 MIP 保护（Remove-FileLabel -RemoveProtection）
solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-a-decrypt-pst-with-powershell.md
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/MPIP Client/How to: MPIP Client/How To: Decrypt a PST with PowerShell

symptom: Customer asks if MPIP client can be prevented from being automatically updated via Microsoft Update / Windows Update
rootCause: MPIP client does not provide any built-in option to enable or disable automatic updates; update behavior is entirely controlled by Windows Update settings on the device
solution: There is no option in MPIP client to control automatic updates. Available update methods: (1) Manual download from Microsoft Download Center (https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/download/details.aspx?id=53018), (2) Windows Update — new versions published ~1 month after initial release, (3) Enterprise tools: WSUS, SCCM, Group Policy, or third-party software update solutions.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/MPIP Client/How to: MPIP Client/How to update the Microsoft Purview Information Protection (MPIP) client?

symptom: Error when running MPIP PowerShell commands — 'is not recognized as the name of a cmdlet, function, script file, or operable program'
rootCause: MPIP PowerShell module (PurviewInformationProtection) not installed in correct path or missing from PSModulePath; or user is running PowerShell 7 which is not supported (requires Windows PowerShell 4.0+)
solution: Step 1: Verify command spelling against PurviewInformationProtection module docs (https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/purviewinformationprotection/). Step 2: Check module is installed in %ProgramFiles(x86)%\PurviewInformationProtection and appears in PSModulePath — run Get-Module PurviewInformationProtection. Step 3: Verify Windows PowerShell version >= 4.0 using $PSVersionTable — PowerShell 7 is NOT supported.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/MPIP Client/How to: MPIP Client/How to: Check MPIP Client PowerShell module is installed

symptom: MPIP Client fails to communicate with Azure RMS service; TLS connection appears to be intercepted or terminated before reaching the Azure RMS endpoint
rootCause: TLS client-to-service connections being terminated before reaching Azure RMS service, caused by: TLS 1.2 not enabled on client, network packet inspection intercepting TLS traffic, or proxy/firewall misconfiguration blocking RMS endpoints
solution: Run in PowerShell Admin: $request = [System.Net.HttpWebRequest]::Create('https://admin.na.aadrm.com/admin/admin.svc'); $request.GetResponse(); $request.ServicePoint.Certificate.Issuer. If the certificate issuer is unexpected (not from Microsoft), investigate: (1) Enable TLS 1.2 on client, (2) Check for network packet/TLS inspection devices, (3) Review proxy/firewall config per RMS requirements (https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/purview/rights-management-requirements#firewalls-and-network-infrastructure).
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/MPIP Client/How to: MPIP Client/How to: Check if Client Connection is terminated Before Azure RMS

symptom: User cannot apply sensitivity labels with MPIP Client; wrong user account is signed in, or sign-in is failing for MPIP Client
rootCause: Cached credentials contain incorrect account not scoped under the Label Policy, or user is attempting to authenticate with credentials from the wrong tenant
solution: Step 1: Right-click file > Apply Sensitivity Label > if sign-in prompted, use account scoped under Label Policy. Step 2: Click Help and Feedback > verify status is 'Connected' with correct user account shown. Step 3: If wrong account cached: click Reset Settings to clear config; run Clear-Authentication PowerShell command (https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/purviewinformationprotection/clear-authentication); sign out incorrect account from Windows Work/School accounts; re-sign in with correct account. For persistent sign-in failures, engage Entra ID team.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/MPIP Client/How to: MPIP Client/How to: Check the correct user account is signed-in to the MPIP Client

symptom: Need to verify whether a sensitivity label has encryption (protection) enabled
rootCause: Encryption status is determined by the 'Control Access' setting in the label's Protection Settings, visible only in the Purview admin portal when editing the label
solution: In Microsoft Purview portal: Edit the sensitivity label > go to Protection Settings page > check if 'Control Access' option is enabled. Disabled = non-encryption label. Enabled = encryption is configured; the next page shows the defined permissions.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/MPIP Client/How to: MPIP Client/How to: check a label is encryption enabled?

symptom: Sensitivity label applied to files via right-click 'Apply Sensitivity Label with Microsoft Purview' or PowerShell, but content markings (header, footer, watermark) are not visible in the file
rootCause: By design: content markings are only applied by Office applications when the file is opened and labeled within an Office app. Applying a label via MPIP right-click context menu or PowerShell does NOT add visual content markings.
solution: This is expected behavior by design. Content markings are added when the file is opened and labeled inside an Office application. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/purview/sensitivity-labels-office-apps#when-office-apps-apply-content-marking-and-encryption. Verify content markings are enabled on the label in Purview portal (edit label > Content marking section). If behavior is unexpected, collect MPIP Client logs (see Required Information guide) and escalate.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/MPIP Client/Troubleshooting Scenarios: MPIP Client/Scenario: Content markings are not applied to files

symptom: AIP/MPIP client installation fails with MSI Error 2753
rootCause: Registry fragments from previous AIP client versions remain under HKEY_CLASSES_ROOT\Installer\, blocking the new installation
solution: Run Microsoft MSI Troubleshooter (https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/help/17588/) to uninstall/reinstall; or use PowerShell to search HKEY_CLASSES_ROOT\Installer\Products\ for entries with ProductName 'Microsoft Azure Information Protection' and delete the orphaned GUID registry key
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/MPIP Client/Troubleshooting Scenarios: MPIP Client/Scenario: Failing to install MPIP Client

symptom: MIP client / AIP Office Add-in fails to download policy; error in log: 'Policy sync failed, HttpResponse.StatusCode=400' calling https://substrate.office.com/CompliancePolicy/ClientSyncFile/
rootCause: AIP Office Add-in retirement - customer is blocked by design from downloading MPIP policies after the AIP Office Add-in retirement
solution: This is by design. Customer is blocked from downloading MPIP policies post-AIP Add-in retirement. Refer to AIP retirement FAQ: https://dev.azure.com/ASIM-Security/Compliance/_wiki/wikis/Information%20Protection/11246/MPIP-Client
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/MPIP Client/Troubleshooting Scenarios: MPIP Client/Scenario: MIP client and AIP Office add-in fail to download policy

symptom: MPIP client cannot open AD RMS protected files after completing migration from AD RMS to AIP; EnduserLicenses request routed to on-premises AD RMS URL instead of api.aadrm.com
rootCause: _rmsdisc DNS SRV record is still configured and points to the on-premises AD RMS URL (common when customer used AD RMS Mobile Device Extension), causing MPIP client to route decryption requests to AD RMS instead of the AIP service
solution: Remove the _rmsdisc DNS SRV record after completing the migration to AIP. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/information-protection/active-directory-rights-manage-mobile-device#specifying-the-dns-srv-records-for-the-ad-rms-mobile-device-extension
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/MPIP Client/Troubleshooting Scenarios: MPIP Client/Scenario: MPIP Client fails to open AD RMS protected files after the migration to AIP

symptom: After upgrading to Windows 10 build 2004 or newer (including Windows 11), 'Apply Sensitivity Label with Microsoft Purview' option is missing from the right-click context menu, or the menu item displays as 'Microsoft.Azip.RightClick' without proper text/icon
rootCause: Registry values under HKEY_CLASSES_ROOT\AllFilesystemObjects\shell\Microsoft.Azip.RightClick get stripped or not migrated during the Windows in-place upgrade because the manifest files do not mark this registry key as required for migration
solution: (1) Export [HKEY_CLASSES_ROOT\AllFilesystemObjects\shell\Microsoft.Azip.RightClick] from a working system and import on the affected system (can be deployed via GPO/script); (2) Reinstall or upgrade the AIP/MPIP client; (3) For Windows 10 to Windows 11 22H2 upgrade, enable Dynamic Update to apply KB5027318 fix automatically. Note: no functionality impact - clicking 'Microsoft.Azip.RightClick' still works.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/MPIP Client/Troubleshooting Scenarios: MPIP Client/Scenario: MPIP is missing from Right click Context menu

symptom: Unable to apply sensitivity label on files using Set-FileLabel PowerShell command; label not found or command returns error
rootCause: Sensitivity label is not published to the user - the label exists but has not been included in a Label Policy scoped to the user's account
solution: Publish the sensitivity label to the user by creating or editing a Label Policy in the Microsoft Purview portal to include the label and scope it to the affected user. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/purview/create-sensitivity-labels#publish-sensitivity-labels-by-creating-a-label-policy
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/MPIP Client/Troubleshooting Scenarios: MPIP Client/Scenario: Unable to apply Sensitivity label using PowerShell

symptom: Unable to apply encryption-enabled sensitivity label using Set-FileLabel or right-click MPIP client; encryption label application fails
rootCause: One of: (1) AIP Service Onboarding Control Policy is not enabled; (2) underlying AIP/RMS template is not in published state; (3) user lacks valid AIP encryption license (requires M365 E3/E5, EMS E3/E5, etc.); (4) client machine does not meet network/firewall prerequisites for AIP
solution: Check: (1) Enable AIP Onboarding Control Policy if disabled; (2) Publish the AIP template in Purview portal; (3) Assign valid encryption license to the user; (4) Ensure firewall allows AIP endpoints. If all look good, collect and analyze MPIP client log.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/MPIP Client/Troubleshooting Scenarios: MPIP Client/Scenario: Unable to apply Sensitivity label using PowerShell

symptom: When right-clicking a file and selecting 'Apply Sensitivity Label with Microsoft Purview', user gets error: 'This file does not have an app associated with it for performing this action. Please install an app or, if one is already installed, create an association in the Default Apps Settings page'
rootCause: A file named 'Program' (or similar) exists in the root of the C:\ drive, interfering with the MPIP client app association
solution: Open C:\ drive and check for a file named 'Program' or similar. Delete that file. If the issue persists, refer to Missing Context Menu troubleshooting guide.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/MPIP Client/Troubleshooting Scenarios: MPIP Client/Scenario: Unable to apply label using Right click Classify and Protect

symptom: Remove-FileLabel PowerShell command fails to remove label or encryption from an encrypted file; user cannot remove protection even with correct syntax
rootCause: User running the PowerShell command does not have Full Control (Owner) permission on the encrypted file; AIP encryption rights restrict who can remove protection
solution: (1) Edit the label in Purview portal and confirm the user has 'Owner' rights configured, which grants Full Control including the right to remove labels; (2) If user cannot be given Owner rights, configure an AIP Super User account: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/information-protection/configure-super-users and use that account to remove/change the label.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/MPIP Client/Troubleshooting Scenarios: MPIP Client/Scenario: Unable to remove Sensitivity label or Encryption from files using PowerShell

symptom: Remove-FileLabel -RemoveProtection fails to remove encryption from emails inside PST or MSG files; command returns error or silently fails on container files
rootCause: ContainerSupport advanced setting is not enabled in the LabelPolicy; this setting is required to process .zip, .rar, .7z, and emails inside PST files with Remove-FileLabel
solution: Enable ContainerSupport in the LabelPolicy advanced settings: Set-LabelPolicy -Identity '<PolicyName>' -AdvancedSettings @{EnableContainerSupport='True'}. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/exchange/client-advanced-settings?view=exchange-ps#enablecontainersupport
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/MPIP Client/Troubleshooting Scenarios: MPIP Client/Scenario: Unable to remove Sensitivity label or Encryption from files using PowerShell

symptom: 'Delete Label' button is grayed out in MPIP client when trying to remove sensitivity label via right-click; user cannot remove any label from files
rootCause: Mandatory Labeling is enabled in the Label Policy scoped to the user; by design, users cannot delete a label from a file when Mandatory Labeling is enforced
solution: This is by design. When Mandatory Labeling is enabled, users can only change the label to another label - they cannot delete it entirely. To verify: edit the highest-priority Label Policy scoped to the user and check if 'Require users to apply a label to their email or documents' is enabled. If removal is genuinely needed, disable Mandatory Labeling or create an exception policy.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/MPIP Client/Troubleshooting Scenarios: MPIP Client/Scenario: Unable to remove or change label using Right click Classify and Protect option

symptom: Unable to remove or change encryption label from files using MPIP client right-click 'Apply Sensitivity Label with Microsoft Purview'; error or button non-functional for encrypted files
rootCause: User does not have Owner or Full Control usage right on the encrypted file; AIP Rights Management restricts label modification to users with Owner rights
solution: (1) Edit the label in Purview portal and confirm the affected user has 'Owner' rights (Full Control), which allows removing/changing the label; (2) If the user cannot be given Owner rights, configure an AIP Super User account via https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/information-protection/configure-super-users; (3) For PST/MSG files, use Remove-FileLabel PowerShell command with -RemoveProtection parameter instead.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/MPIP Client/Troubleshooting Scenarios: MPIP Client/Scenario: Unable to remove or change label using Right click Classify and Protect option

symptom: MPIP Add-in for Outlook issues - setup, installation, configuration and FAQ for Microsoft Purview Information Protection Add-in for Outlook on Exchange On-Prem
solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-b-mpip-add-in-for-outlook.md
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/MPIP Client/Troubleshooting Scenarios: MPIP Client/Scenario: MPIP Add-in for Outlook issues

symptom: MPIP Scanner DLP policy runs successfully (scan completes) but no actions are applied to matched files — files are not restricted, blocked, or quarantined despite DLP policy being configured
rootCause: DLP policy for MPIP Scanner is configured without a supported action type. 'Alert' actions are not treated as valid actions for Scanner DLP by design; only 'restrict file', 'block', or 'quarantine' type actions are supported
solution: Edit the DLP policy to include at least one action of type 'restrict file', 'block', or 'quarantine'. Alert-only actions will not cause Scanner to take any protective action on matched content. Refer: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/purview/deploy-scanner-configure-install?tabs=azure-portal-only#use-a-dlp-policy
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/MPIP Scanner/How to: MPIP Scanner/How to: Scanner DLP

symptom: MPIP Scanner connection fails with NetworkError/HTTP failure even though proxy is configured on the server. Errors include: 'WinHttpSendRequest: 12007: The server name or address could not be resolved', 'Unable to connect to the remote server', SocketException connection timeout. Scanner cannot reach endpoints like dataservice.protection.outlook.com
rootCause: MPIP Scanner service account lacks the 'Allow Logon locally' privilege on the Scanner Server. Without interactive logon capability, the service account has no user profile and therefore does not inherit the proxy settings configured for other accounts on the server
solution: 1. Grant 'Allow Logon locally' right to the MPIP Scanner service account on the Scanner Server. 2. Open Command Prompt using 'Run as Different User' with the service account credentials. 3. Run 'whoami' to verify the correct account. 4. Open IE via 'start iexplore' and configure proxy under Internet Settings > Connections > LAN Settings > Proxy Server. 5. Verify proxy is written to registry: HKEY_USERS\<SID of service account>\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Internet Settings\Connections\DefaultConnectionSettings. 6. Optionally revoke 'Allow Logon locally' after proxy is configured.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/MPIP Scanner/Troubleshooting Scenarios: MPIP Scanner/Scenario: MPIP Scanner doesn't use Proxy and the connection fails

symptom: Purview Portal reports DB error for AIP Scanner after upgrade. Error: 'The database owner SID recorded in the master database differs from the database owner SID recorded in database AIPScannerUL_...'. SqlException 0x80131904. Commands 'Failed to update scanner status in DB' and 'Failed to execute SQL command [[dbo].[GetSchema]]' appear in logs.
rootCause: After upgrading the AIP Scanner, the database owner SID of the AIPScanner database no longer matches the database owner SID recorded in the master database. This SID mismatch prevents SQL commands from executing against the AIPScanner database.
solution: IMPORTANT: MPIP support should NOT deliver SQL commands directly to customers — refer them to their DBA or open collaboration with SQL team. Resolution (for DBA): In SQL Server Management Studio, ensure the 'sa' account is enabled, then run: EXEC sp_changedbowner 'sa'; ALTER DATABASE [AIPScannerUL_<name>] SET TRUSTWORTHY ON. This aligns the AIPScanner database owner with the master database owner.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/MPIP Scanner/Troubleshooting Scenarios: MPIP Scanner/Scenario: SQL Error when upgrading the Scanner

symptom: Need to determine correct support owner, DfM Service Area Path (SAP) and routing for MPIP Scanner vs MPIP Client issues to avoid misrouting cases
solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-b-support-boundaries-mpip-scanner.md
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/MPIP Scanner/Support Boundaries: MPIP Scanner

symptom: MPIP Scanner policy download fails with 'provided uri is invalid' error when syncing policy from PsorWebService endpoint (e.g., https://dataservice.protection.outlook.com/PsorWebService/v1/ClientSyncFile/MipPolicies/DOMAIN\username)
rootCause: DelegatedUser account format is incorrect: domain\username format (e.g., CONTOSO\aipscanner) is used instead of email/UPN format (e.g., aipscanner@contoso.com) when running Set-AIPAuthentication
solution: 1) Delete MsalTokenCache or TokenCache from C:\Users\<Scanner user>\AppData\Local\Microsoft\MSIP\  (or use Clear-AIPAuthentication); 2) Re-run Set-AIPAuthentication with -DelegatedUser set to email format (e.g., scanner@contoso.com, not CONTOSO\scanner); Ensure email format appears correctly in the PsorWebService URL
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/MPIP Scanner/Troubleshooting Scenarios: MPIP Scanner/Scenario: Scanner policy download fails with 'provided uri is invalid' error

symptom: MPIP Scanner server reaches 100% CPU usage and high memory usage while running scan jobs
rootCause: By design: MPIP Scanner is configured to utilize maximum available server resources with no continuous CPU throttling; the scanner only checks CPU usage every 30 minutes
solution: 1) Deploy scanner as a standalone server (primary recommendation); 2) Add ScannerMaxCpu and ScannerMinCpu registry keys to limit CPU on best-effort basis (scanner checks and adjusts CPU every 30 min but usage will spike between checks); PG is aware and has plans to improve CPU limiting in a future release. Registry key references: ScannerMaxCpu and ScannerMinCpu in client advanced settings
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/MPIP Scanner/Troubleshooting Scenarios: MPIP Scanner/Scenario: Scanner utilizing high CPU

symptom: Get-RMSTemplate returns no results in Exchange Online; TemplateNotFound errors in Extended Message Trace (EMT) when sending encrypted messages via IRM
rootCause: IRM configuration (Get-IRMConfiguration) has multiple licensing locations in LicensingLocation and the AIP service URL (from Get-AipServiceConfiguration) is not listed first
solution: 1) Get AIP service URL: run (Get-AipServiceConfiguration).LicensingIntranetDistributionPointUrl in AipService PS; 2) Backup IRM config: Get-IRMConfiguration|Out-File IRMConfig.backup; 3) Remove then re-add AIP URL to force it to first position: Set-IRMConfiguration -LicensingLocation @{Remove=$url} then Set-IRMConfiguration -LicensingLocation @{Add=$url}; 4) Wait up to 1 hour; 5) Verify with Get-RMSTemplate - should now return templates
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Purview Message Encryption/How to: Purview Message Encryption/How To: Re-order IRM licensing Locations

symptom: Need to collect browser HAR trace (including pop-up windows) for diagnosing Purview/PME OWA portal issues, authentication failures, or API errors in browser sessions
solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-b-get-har-trace-including-pop-ups.md
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Purview Message Encryption/How to: Purview Message Encryption/How to: Get Har Trace (including Pop-Ups)

symptom: PowerShell cmdlet errors when troubleshooting Purview/PME encryption (e.g., 'Get-Mailbox is not recognized'); need to install ExchangeOnlineManagement and AipService PS modules and connect to Exchange Online, SCC, and AipService
solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-b-install-connect-powershell-modules.md
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Purview Message Encryption/How to: Purview Message Encryption/How to: Install and Connect to PowerShell Modules

symptom: OWA (Outlook on the Web) 中 Sensitivity 标签或加密模板不可见、不可选；Sensitivity 按钮缺失或灰显
rootCause: IRM 未在 EXO 启用（InternalLicensingEnabled=false）或 SimplifiedClientAccessEnabled=false，导致 OWA 无法渲染加密功能
solution: 1) Set-IRMConfiguration -InternalLicensingEnabled $true -AzureRMSLicensingEnabled $true；2) Set-IRMConfiguration -SimplifiedClientAccessEnabled $true；3) 确认 LicensingLocation 已设置为 Get-AipServiceConfiguration 返回的 LicensingExtranetDistributionPointUrl
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Purview Message Encryption/How to: Purview Message Encryption/How to: Verify and set the Information Rights Management (IRM) Configuration for Purview Message Encryption

symptom: EXO Transport Rules (ETR) 中无法选择加密模板（Do Not Forward / Encrypt-Only 不显示）；DLP 加密动作无法执行
rootCause: InternalLicensingEnabled=false 或 AzureRMSLicensingEnabled=false；或 IRM LicensingLocation 未配置正确的 AIP 端点
solution: 1) Set-IRMConfiguration -InternalLicensingEnabled $true -AzureRMSLicensingEnabled $true；2) $ep=(Get-AipServiceConfiguration).LicensingExtranetDistributionPointUrl; Set-IRMConfiguration -LicensingLocation $ep；3) 运行 Get-RMSTemplate 确认模板出现后 ETR 动作可用
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Purview Message Encryption/How to: Purview Message Encryption/How to: Verify and set the Information Rights Management (IRM) Configuration for Purview Message Encryption

symptom: IRM 模板无法同步到 Exchange，或 RMS 模板突然消失；Get-RMSTemplate 返回空或报错；存在多个 LicensingLocation 值
rootCause: IRM LicensingLocation 中存在多个值，但 AipServiceConfiguration 返回的 LicensingExtranetDistributionPointUrl 不在第一位，导致模板同步失败
solution: 按官方 TSG 重新排序 IRM Licensing Locations，确保 Get-AipServiceConfiguration 返回的 URL 排列第一；参考指南 ado-wiki-b-verify-set-irm-configuration-pme.md 中 Step 6 → Multiple Licensing Locations
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Purview Message Encryption/How to: Purview Message Encryption/How to: Verify and set the Information Rights Management (IRM) Configuration for Purview Message Encryption

symptom: Microsoft Teams 或 AAD 分组（Groups）中敏感度标签不显示；Teams 用户无法看到或应用标签
rootCause: IRM 中 AutomaticServiceUpdateEnabled=false，禁止了 AIP 新功能自动同步及 Azure AD 同步，Teams/AAD Groups 依赖此项
solution: Set-IRMConfiguration -AutomaticServiceUpdateEnabled $true；等待约 24h 同步生效后验证
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Purview Message Encryption/How to: Purview Message Encryption/How to: Verify and set the Information Rights Management (IRM) Configuration for Purview Message Encryption

symptom: Get-AipServiceConfiguration 显示 FunctionalState 或 IPCv3ServiceFunctionalState 为 Disabled；所有依赖 AIP 加密的功能均失效（IRM、标签加密、EO/DNF 模板不可用）
rootCause: AIP (Azure Information Protection) 服务或 IPCv3 服务被禁用
solution: 1) Enable-AipService（启用 AIP 服务）；2) Enable-AipServiceIPCv3（启用 IPC v3）；3) 运行 Get-AipServiceConfiguration|FL 验证，等待约 24h 同步后再配置 IRM
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Purview Message Encryption/How to: Purview Message Encryption/How to: Verify and set the Information Rights Management (IRM) Configuration for Purview Message Encryption

symptom: 使用 Encrypt-Only (EO) 模板发送加密邮件时，PDF 附件未被加密（收件人可直接打开）
rootCause: IRM 中 EnablePdfEncryption=false，PDF 附件加密功能未开启
solution: Set-IRMConfiguration -EnablePdfEncryption $true
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Purview Message Encryption/How to: Purview Message Encryption/How to: Verify and set the Information Rights Management (IRM) Configuration for Purview Message Encryption

symptom: 邮件日志（Journaling）的副本收件人收到的 journal 邮件仍然加密，没有附带可直接读取的解密副本
rootCause: IRM 中 JournalReportDecryptionEnabled=false，未启用加密邮件的 journal 解密功能
solution: Set-IRMConfiguration -JournalReportDecryptionEnabled $true；Journal 收件人将收到两份附件：一份加密原文、一份解密副本
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Purview Message Encryption/How to: Purview Message Encryption/How to: Verify and set the Information Rights Management (IRM) Configuration for Purview Message Encryption

symptom: OWA 中默认的 Do Not Forward 或 Encrypt-Only 选项不可用（被禁用）
rootCause: IRM 中 SimplifiedClientAccessEncryptOnlyDisabled=true 或 SimplifiedClientAccessDoNotForwardDisabled=true
solution: Set-IRMConfiguration -SimplifiedClientAccessEncryptOnlyDisabled $false（恢复 EO）；Set-IRMConfiguration -SimplifiedClientAccessDoNotForwardDisabled $false（恢复 DNF）
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Purview Message Encryption/How to: Purview Message Encryption/How to: Verify and set the Information Rights Management (IRM) Configuration for Purview Message Encryption

symptom: 发送加密邮件时收到 NDR："550 5.3.101 RmsSvcAgent; Cannot RMS protect the message because Encryption is disabled in Microsoft Exchange Transport"
rootCause: IRM 配置错误或 EXO Transport 无法从 AIP 获取 IRM 配置（AIP 服务禁用、LicensingLocation 配置错误或 IRM 禁用）
solution: 1) 获取 Extended Message Trace，确认 tenant_id 正确；2) 运行 Assist365 诊断 'Validate Office Message Encryption (OME) Configuration' 或手动执行 Get-AipServiceConfiguration + Get-IRMConfiguration 检查；3) 按照 IRM 配置 TSG 修复（参见 ado-wiki-b-verify-set-irm-configuration-pme.md）
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Purview Message Encryption/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Purview Message Encryption/Scenario: 550 5.3.101 RmsSvcAgent; Cannot RMS protect the message because Encryption is disabled

symptom: 收件人收到错误的或默认 OME 品牌模板，而非预期的自定义品牌；邮件 header X-MS-Exchange-CrossTenant-AuthAs 值为 'Anonymous'
rootCause: 加密邮件经第三方连接器（如 Proofpoint）路由后，以匿名身份到达 EXO，OME 对 Anonymous 来源的邮件统一使用默认品牌模板
solution: 调整邮件流，确保 OME 加密邮件不经过会将 AuthAs 设为 Anonymous 的连接器；检查 inbound/outbound connector 配置
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Purview Message Encryption/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Purview Message Encryption/Scenario: Branding Template not working as expected

symptom: 收件人看到的 OME 品牌模板与预期不符（应用了错误的自定义模板）；消息跟踪显示正确模板已被应用但收件人仍看到错误模板
rootCause: By design — 多条 Transport Rule 中另一条规则的优先级更高并率先匹配，应用了不同的品牌模板
solution: 1) 获取 message trace，查看哪条 rule 实际触发了品牌模板；2) 审查 Transport Rule 执行顺序（priority），重新排列 rule 优先级；3) 检查 rule 条件确保期望 rule 正确匹配
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Purview Message Encryption/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Purview Message Encryption/Scenario: Branding Template not working as expected

symptom: 本地 Office 桌面应用（Word/Excel/PowerPoint/Outlook Classic）中 Sensitivity 按钮消失、灰显，或敏感度标签不显示、显示信息不是最新的
rootCause: 本地机器上的标签缓存和加密策略数据过期或损坏，Office 应用读取了旧缓存而非从服务端重新拉取
solution: 使用 ComplianceUtility（原 UnifiedLabelingSupportTool）执行 Reset：关闭所有 Office 应用后运行 'ComplianceUtility -Reset Default'，清除本地标签/策略/模板缓存，强制 Office 重新下载；注意：此工具不适用于 New Outlook (Monarch)
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Purview Message Encryption/How to: Purview Message Encryption/How to: Use UnifiedLabellingSupportTool or ComplianceUtility to perform Label Reset on Office Desktop apps or local machine

solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-b-message-header-analyzer-tool.md
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Purview Message Encryption/How to: Purview Message Encryption/How to: Message Header Analyzer Tool

solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-b-complianceutility-label-reset.md

solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-b-required-information-pme.md

symptom: PME 加密邮件问题分诊入口：发送/接收/打开加密邮件、标签/模板问题、OTP、撤销、Journaling、NDR 等各类场景的分诊决策树
solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-b-mip-troubleshooter-guide.md
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Purview Message Encryption/Start Here - MIP Troubleshooter Guide

symptom: PME 支持边界参考：哪些问题归 Compliance 团队、哪些归 Exchange Online、哪些归 Outlook 或其他客户端团队
solution: 见排查指南: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-b-support-boundaries-pme.md
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Purview Message Encryption/Support Boundaries: Purview Message Encryption

symptom: Journaled encrypted message is missing the decrypted attachment; journal mailbox only receives encrypted copy
rootCause: JournalReportDecryptionEnabled is set to false in IRM configuration, so Exchange Online does not decrypt the message before journaling
solution: Run: Get-IrmConfiguration and check JournalReportDecryptionEnabled. If false, run: Set-IRMConfiguration -JournalReportDecryptionEnabled $true. Note: change only applies to messages received after the setting is enabled.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Purview Message Encryption/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Purview Message Encryption/Scenario: Encrypted email journaling is not working correctly

symptom: Journaled encrypted message does not include decrypted attachment even though JournalReportDecryptionEnabled is true
rootCause: The message was targeted by an Exchange Transport Rule (ETR) with action ApplyRightsProtectionCustomizationTemplate (OME branding). Per design, ETR-applied OME branding messages are not journaled with decrypted attachments — only the encrypted attachment is included.
solution: In EMT, search for RightsProtectMessageCustomization. If entry contains ETR (not ETRP or ETRI) with action=RightsProtectMessageCustomization, this is by design. The customer must avoid using ETR-based OME branding on messages that need decrypted journaling.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Purview Message Encryption/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Purview Message Encryption/Scenario: Encrypted email journaling is not working correctly

symptom: Journaled encrypted message from external sender does not include decrypted attachment
rootCause: Decrypted attachment in journaling only works when the encryption template/label belongs to the recipient organization. If the message was encrypted by another organization's template/label, the journal cannot decrypt it.
solution: In EMT, find the TemplateID in the S:IRMPL row. Run Get-RMSTemplate -Identity '<GUID>' to verify the template belongs to the org. If the template is from another org, decryption is not supported — this is by design.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Purview Message Encryption/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Purview Message Encryption/Scenario: Encrypted email journaling is not working correctly

symptom: Journaled encrypted message missing decrypted attachment; message directionality is not 'originating'
rootCause: Journal report decryption requires the message to be identified as 'originating' in Exchange. If connectors are misconfigured, outbound messages may be treated as 'incoming', preventing the journal from producing a decrypted copy.
solution: In EMT, check message directionality throughout the trace. If it changes from 'originating' to 'incoming' mid-trace, review connector configuration. Correctly configured connectors should not change the message directionality for internally sent messages.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Purview Message Encryption/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Purview Message Encryption/Scenario: Encrypted email journaling is not working correctly

symptom: Recipient cannot open encrypted attachment (Word/Excel/PowerPoint); error occurs when attempting to open encrypted file
rootCause: Conditional Access (CA) policies in the sender's organization require specific authentication methods (e.g., MFA) that the external recipient cannot satisfy, blocking access to the encrypted content via the RMS service.
solution: Option A: Create a guest account for the external recipient in the sender's tenant and have them complete the CA requirements (MFA etc). Option B: Exclude guest users from the CA policy (WARNING: applies to all guests in all applications). Check Entra configuration for Purview Information Protection: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/purview/encryption-azure-ad-configuration
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Purview Message Encryption/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Purview Message Encryption/Scenario: Recipient is not able to open Encrypted Attachment

symptom: External recipient (Gmail/Yahoo) not receiving One Time Passcode (OTP) to open encrypted message; OTP option not shown
rootCause: OTPEnabled is set to false in the OME Configuration, so the OTP sign-in option is disabled and not offered to recipients.
solution: Connect to EXO PowerShell. Run Get-OmeConfiguration to check OTPEnabled. If false, run: Set-OMEConfiguration 'OME Configuration' -OTPEnabled $true. Wait a few minutes for sync, then re-test.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Purview Message Encryption/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Purview Message Encryption/Scenario: Recipient is not receiving a One Time Passcode (OTP)

symptom: External recipient (Gmail/Yahoo/365) cannot find the OTP email in inbox; OTP email seems lost
rootCause: The OTP email may have been delivered to the Junk/Spam folder or quarantined by MDO policies. For Gmail/Yahoo, generic search does not search spam folder.
solution: 1. Search mailbox for 'one-time passcode'. For Gmail/Yahoo: use 'in:spam "one-time passcode"'. For M365 recipients, run: Get-MessageTrace -SenderAddress MicrosoftOffice365@messaging.microsoft.com [-RecipientAddress user@domain.com] to check if delivered or quarantined. If quarantined, release message; if persistent, engage MDO team.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Purview Message Encryption/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Purview Message Encryption/Scenario: Recipient is not receiving a One Time Passcode (OTP)

symptom: Recipient set up inbox rule to forward OTP email to another mailbox; forwarded OTP does not work
rootCause: By design: the OTP code email can only be used by the original recipient of the encrypted message. Forwarding the OTP code email via an inbox rule is not supported.
solution: This is by design. The OTP email cannot be forwarded. The original recipient must receive and use the OTP directly. Note: encrypted messages themselves can be forwarded — it is only the OTP code email that cannot be forwarded.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Purview Message Encryption/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Purview Message Encryption/Scenario: Recipient is not receiving a One Time Passcode (OTP)

symptom: NDR 554 5.2.0 STOREDRV.Deliver.Exception or NDR 550 5.7.162 when sending encrypted email; encrypted message fails to deliver
rootCause: Publishing license is too large. When a message is encrypted, recipient addresses (To/CC/BCC) and users/groups from the sensitivity label Access Control settings are all embedded in the publishing license metadata. There is a fixed size limit and no easy way to calculate it.
solution: For sensitivity label encryption: go to Purview Portal → label Access Control settings → minimize the Users and groups entries. For Encrypt-Only or Do Not Forward: reduce the number of To/CC/BCC addresses (use distribution groups instead of individual addresses). Keep reducing until the message can be sent successfully.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Purview Message Encryption/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Purview Message Encryption/Scenario: Remote server returned 554  5.2.0  STOREDRV.Deliver.Exception

symptom: Sender cannot revoke an encrypted message; Revoke button does not appear in OWA
rootCause: Sender does not have the BPOS_S_O365PremiumEncryption capability or MIP_S_CLP2 license required for message revocation.
solution: Check: (Get-Recipient $SenderMbx).Capabilities|? {$_ -match 'PremiumEncryption'} and (Get-Mailbox $SenderMbx).PersistedCapabilities|? {$_ -match 'MIP'}. If empty, assign a Premium encryption license (e.g., Microsoft 365 E5 or Microsoft Purview Information Protection) to the sender.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Purview Message Encryption/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Purview Message Encryption/Scenario: Sender is unable to revoke an encrypted message

symptom: Revoke button does not appear when message has multiple recipients or recipient is an O365/Microsoft consumer domain user
rootCause: By design: revocation requires exactly 1 recipient, and that recipient cannot be from another O365 tenant or Microsoft consumer domains (hotmail.com, live.com, msn.com, outlook.com/country variants). Sending to 2+ non-365 recipients or mixing 365 and external also disables revocation.
solution: This is by design. Revocation only works for exactly 1 non-365, non-consumer-domain recipient. Inform the customer of this limitation. No workaround exists for multi-recipient or O365/consumer domain recipient scenarios.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Purview Message Encryption/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Purview Message Encryption/Scenario: Sender is unable to revoke an encrypted message

symptom: Get-OMEMessageStatus shows IsRevocable = false; message cannot be revoked
rootCause: The message was not sent in a way that supports revocation (e.g., not using Premium encryption, or was sent before revocation feature was available for that message).
solution: Run: Get-OMEMessageStatus -MessageId '<Message-ID>' | FL and check IsRevocable field. If IsRevocable is false, the message cannot be revoked — this cannot be changed. If Revoked is true but needs to be un-revoked, run: Set-OMEMessageRevocation -MessageId '<Message-ID>' -Revoke $false (requires admin permissions).
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Purview Message Encryption/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Purview Message Encryption/Scenario: Sender is unable to revoke an encrypted message

symptom: Revoke button not visible in OWA Sent Items even though message is encrypted and recipient is valid for revocation
rootCause: The Revoke button (Remove external access) may take time to appear after the message is sent. Also, it only appears in OWA — no other clients support message revocation.
solution: 1. Confirm client is OWA (revocation is OWA-only, not Outlook desktop or other clients). 2. Wait at least 30 minutes after sending. 3. Force a hard refresh of OWA (CTRL + Refresh button) to clear cache. The 'Remove external access' button should then appear in Sent Items for the message.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Purview Message Encryption/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Purview Message Encryption/Scenario: Sender is unable to revoke an encrypted message

symptom: Error shown when attempting to preview Word/Excel/PowerPoint documents from Purview encrypted messages in OWA or OME portal
rootCause: Either the user lacks label permissions to view the document, Conditional Access policies are blocking communication to the RMS service, or there is an intermittent issue with Office Online Server/WEP (Web Editing Platform) when opening docs from OWA/portal.
solution: 1. Check if document is labeled and verify user has permission in the label configuration. 2. Check Conditional Access policies that may block RMS service access. 3. Try closing and re-opening the encrypted message. If the document eventually opens after retry, this is an Office Online issue — escalate to the Office Online team with PSR + HAR trace capturing all encryption and WEP calls.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Purview Message Encryption/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Purview Message Encryption/Scenario: Unable to preview Office documents from Purview encrypted messages

symptom: DLP rule encryption template dropdown is empty; user cannot select RMS template when creating or editing a DLP rule in Purview portal
rootCause: The user does not have permissions to execute Get-RMSTemplate and Get-IRMConfiguration cmdlets. The Purview UX queries Get-RMSTemplate to populate the dropdown, which requires these cmdlets to be accessible. The required role must be assigned in Exchange, not just in Purview.
solution: Assign the 'Information Protection Administrator' role group to the user — and ensure it is assigned in Exchange Online, not only in Purview. Verify by running Get-RMSTemplate and Get-IRMConfiguration from EXO PowerShell as the affected user.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Purview Message Encryption/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Purview Message Encryption/Scenario: User cannot configure encryption via DLP Rule

symptom: Get-RMSTemplate returns no templates; DLP rule template dropdown remains empty even though user has cmdlet access
rootCause: IRM configuration settings are incorrect or AzureRMSEnabled is not set to true, causing Get-RMSTemplate to return no results.
solution: Run Get-IRMConfiguration and verify AzureRMSEnabled = $true. If not, follow the IRM configuration verification guide: How to: Verify and set the Information Rights Management (IRM) Configuration for Purview Message Encryption.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Purview Message Encryption/Troubleshooting Scenarios: Purview Message Encryption/Scenario: User cannot configure encryption via DLP Rule

symptom: Sensitivity label not visible in Office apps (Word/Excel/PowerPoint/Outlook). User reports label is missing.
rootCause: The label is not published to the user via label policy — in the MIP cache the label has enabled=false. Or the label policy has not yet synced to the client cache.
solution: Check the MIP label cache at %localappdata%\Microsoft\Office\CLP\. Open the Policy.{UPN}.{ObjectID}_ADAL.gz file with 7-Zip. Parse the XML: if the label has enabled=false it is not published to the user. Verify the label policy in Purview Portal → Information Protection → Policies → Publishing Policies includes the user. Wait up to 24 hours for policy sync, or force sync by deleting the .gz cache file and reopening the Office app.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Sensitivity Labels/How to: Sensitivity Labels/How To: View M365 Office client label cache

symptom: Label policy settings (default label, mandatory labeling) not applying as expected despite being configured in a label policy.
rootCause: Multiple label policies are assigned to the user. The highest priority policy's settings override ALL lower priority policy settings. The only exception is label inheritance for emails, which can be set from lower priority policies.
solution: In Purview Portal → Information Protection → Policies → Publishing Policies: (1) Find all label policies scoped to the user (check group memberships). (2) Identify the highest priority policy (highest number = highest priority). (3) Verify the desired settings are configured in that highest priority policy. Note: a setting not configured in the highest priority policy means it is disabled, even if a lower priority policy has it enabled.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Sensitivity Labels/How to: Sensitivity Labels/How to: Determine Sensitivity Label and Policy priority

symptom: Purview Portal built-in diagnostic 'Email encryption isn't working as expected' shows a failed check for 'This test case checks the email encryption configurations', appearing as a failure.
rootCause: This is a known false positive in the diagnostic. The diagnostic searches for old/legacy license names that no longer exist in the tenant, causing it to report a failure even when email encryption is working correctly.
solution: Ignore the failure for the 'email encryption configurations' check — it is a known false positive. Focus on the other two checks in the diagnostic: 'Check if MIP Label feature is enabled' and 'Validate mail encrypt evaluation details'. If those pass, email encryption configuration is correct.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Sensitivity Labels/How to: Sensitivity Labels/How to: Information Protection Diagnostics

symptom: User in New Outlook or OWA cannot apply or change a sensitivity label on a reply to an encrypted email received from an external tenant. The operation is blocked.
rootCause: Each tenant only recognizes its own labels. The external tenant's protection/encryption is on the email. Without Export or Full Control usage rights, the user cannot remove/change the protection, and therefore cannot apply a new label. New Outlook (OWA) enforces this correctly. Classic Outlook (Win32) may incorrectly allow changing the label without removing protection.
solution: This is expected behavior in OWA/New Outlook. If the user needs to apply their own label: (1) Use Classic Outlook for Windows (known inconsistency - allows label change without removing protection). (2) Or ensure the user has Export/Full Control usage rights on the protected content. Note: label metadata from both tenants coexists — each tenant sees only its own label.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Sensitivity Labels/Learn: Sensitivity Labels/Learn: How do externally applied labels work?

symptom: Outlook automatic label inheritance from email attachments does not work — a more sensitive attachment label is not automatically applied to the email. MSIPOutlookAddin.iplog shows: 'PrivilegedRequiredException: Can't override privileged label'.
rootCause: The email already has a manually applied label (LabelingMethod=Privileged). Automatic labeling actions (LabelingMethod=Standard) cannot override Privileged labels. The attachment action setting is configured as 'automatic' which cannot override a manual label.
solution: Change the Outlook attachment action advanced setting from 'automatic' to 'recommended'. With 'recommended', users will be prompted to adjust the label based on attachments regardless of the existing label's LabelingMethod. Alternatively, if the email has no label or only a default label (Standard), automatic attachment labeling will work as expected.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Sensitivity Labels/Learn: Sensitivity Labels/Learn: LabelingMethod Attribute

symptom: User receives 'access denied' when trying to open a Word/Excel/PowerPoint document that has a sensitivity label with dynamic watermarking enabled.
rootCause: The user's Office client does not support dynamic watermarking. Minimum required versions: Windows 2407 (Build 17830.20000), Mac 16.87 (Build 24070110), or Office on the web. All users except the file owner must use a supported client version.
solution: Update Office client to a supported version: Windows ≥ 2407 (Build 17830.20000), Mac ≥ 16.87 (Build 24070110), or use Office on the web. Check supported versions at https://learn.microsoft.com/purview/sensitivity-labels-versions (Dynamic watermarks row). Note: Dynamic watermarking requires E5 license (E5 Compliance, IP&G E5, EMS E5, or F5) and the label must have 'Assign permissions now' encryption with 'Use Dynamic Watermarking' enabled.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Sensitivity Labels/Upcoming Features: Sensitivity Labels/Dynamic Watermarks

symptom: Test-IRMConfiguration fails with VerifyMachineCertificateChainFailedException when configuring on-premises Exchange to use the RMS Connector. Exchange event ID 3000 shows the same exception. Exchange presents a cryptographic mode 1 key during initial bootstrap instead of mode 2.
rootCause: Some Exchange servers present a cryptographic mode 1 key for initial bootstrap to the RMS Connector. Since the AIP tenant key is cryptographic mode 2, the mode 1 key fails verification, causing VerifyMachineCertificateChainFailedException.
solution: Set undocumented Exchange registry key to force cryptographic mode 2: For Exchange 2013/2016/2019: HKLM\Software\Microsoft\ExchangeServer\RMSServerCryptoModeOverride (DWORD=2). For Exchange 2010: HKLM\Software\Microsoft\ExchangeServer\v14\RMSServerCryptoModeOverride (DWORD=2). Reboot the Exchange server after applying. Do NOT use this if the active AIP tenant key is actually mode 1.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/RMS Connector/How To: RMS Connector/How To: Exchange cryptographic overrides

symptom: RMS Connector stops working after disabling TLS 1.0 and/or TLS 1.1 on the server. Also applicable to AD RMS. Connector errors appear in Windows Application event log.
rootCause: .NET Framework defaults to TLS 1.0. After TLS 1.0/1.1 are disabled at the OS level, the RMS Connector (.NET application) cannot negotiate a TLS connection to Azure, causing connectivity failure.
solution: 1) Set registry keys to enforce .NET strong crypto (TLS 1.2): SchUseStrongCrypto=1 and SystemDefaultTlsVersions=1 under HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\.NETFramework\v2.0.50727 and v4.0.30319 (also Wow6432Node on 64-bit). 2) Ensure TLS 1.2 is enabled in SCHANNEL: HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\SecurityProviders\SCHANNEL\Protocols\TLS 1.2\Client and Server (Enabled=1, DisabledByDefault=0). 3) Install .NET updates for strong crypto support. 4) Run IISReset.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/RMS Connector/Troubleshooting Scenarios: RMS Connector/Scenario: Connector issue after disabling TLS 1.0 and 1.1

symptom: DLP Endpoint cannot detect sensitivity labels on files. DLP for EXO/SPO/ODB and auto-labeling have difficulties detecting sensitivity labels on documents. Outside of Office applications, the file label is not visible.
rootCause: DRMEncryptProperty registry key (HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Office\16.0\Common\Security) is set to 1, which encrypts file metadata including sensitivity label information. This is not a default Office setting and is not compatible with MIP Labels.
solution: Set DRMEncryptProperty DWORD to 0 (plaintext metadata) under HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Office\16.0\Common\Security. Value 0 means metadata is stored in plaintext (default). This restores DLP Endpoint and auto-labeling visibility of sensitivity labels.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Sensitivity Labels/How to: Sensitivity Labels/How To: See if an item has a Sensitivity Label

symptom: Protection Policy created for Fabric but not applying protection to labeled data assets in Fabric workspaces
rootCause: Fabric Admin portal settings not enabled: 'Allow users to apply sensitivity labels for content' and/or Microsoft Fabric tenant settings (Data Activator, Users can create Fabric items, Users can create Fabric environments) not turned on
solution: In Fabric Admin portal (app.powerbi.com/admin-portal): 1) Enable 'Information Protection > Allow users to apply sensitivity labels for content', 2) Enable all Microsoft Fabric settings (Data Activator, Users can create Fabric items, Users can create Fabric environments). Also verify tenant has Fabric license or trial enabled.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Sensitivity Labels/Upcoming Features: Sensitivity Labels/Protection Policies/Protection policy support for Fabric

symptom: Cannot scope Protection Policy to both Microsoft Fabric and Azure/Amazon data sources in the same policy
rootCause: Microsoft Fabric is mutually exclusive with Azure and Amazon data sources in Protection Policies; they cannot be combined in a single policy by design
solution: Create separate Protection Policies: one scoped to Microsoft Fabric workspaces and another scoped to Azure/Amazon data sources. Each policy requires its own label with 'Files & other data assets' scope and 'Access Control' protection enabled.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Sensitivity Labels/Upcoming Features: Sensitivity Labels/Protection Policies/Protection policy support for Fabric

symptom: Sensitivity label does not appear as available option when creating a Protection Policy for Fabric workspaces
rootCause: Label is not scoped to 'Files & other data assets' and/or does not have 'Access Control' (Control access) protection turned on. Note: some tenants still show 'Files' instead of 'Files & other data assets' during UX string rollout - they are the same.
solution: Edit the sensitivity label: 1) Ensure scope includes 'Files & other data assets' (may still show as 'Files'), 2) Enable 'Control access' protection on the label, 3) Publish the label via a Label publishing policy. Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/purview/create-sensitivity-labels#publish-sensitivity-labels-by-creating-a-label-policy
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/Sensitivity Labels/Upcoming Features: Sensitivity Labels/Protection Policies/Protection policy support for Fabric

symptom: RMS Connector stops working after disabling TLS 1.0 and/or TLS 1.1 on the server. Also applies to AD RMS.
rootCause: Windows .NET Framework defaults to TLS 1.0. The RMS Connector is a .NET application, so disabling TLS 1.0/1.1 without enabling strong cryptography (TLS 1.2) in .NET causes connection failures.
solution: 1) Set registry keys: SchUseStrongCrypto=1 and SystemDefaultTlsVersions=1 under HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\.NETFramework\v2.0.50727 and v4.0.30319 (and Wow6432Node on 64-bit). 2) Ensure SCHANNEL TLS 1.2 Client/Server Enabled=1, DisabledByDefault=0. 3) Install .NET updates if using Framework 4.6 or earlier. 4) Run IISReset.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Compliance/Information Protection:/RMS Connector/Troubleshooting Scenarios: RMS Connector/Scenario: Connector issue after disabling TLS 1.0 and 1.1

symptom: After case is closed, customer accesses DTM workspace URL and receives confusing error messages: 'Connection error: Please try again later or contact your support agent for assistance' for Delete, 'The website declined to show this page' for Download, '<filename> - file upload failed' for Add Files
rootCause: DTM workspace access is revoked after case closure but the URL remains accessible; error messages are non-descriptive and confusing to customers
solution: Expected behavior — workspace access is correctly restricted post case-closure. Inform customer that the workspace is no longer active since the case is closed. Files are permanently deleted 90 days after case closure.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Information Protection/Information Protection Team Wiki:/General Onboarding Material/Case Management/Secure data transferring on cases with DTM

symptom: Customer cannot access DTM shared files or receives access denied when trying to open DTM workspace
rootCause: Customer is using a non-authorized account (not the account listed as the case contact) to access DTM files
solution: Ensure customer uses the account listed as contact for the case. Additional contacts can be added directly to the case to grant them DTM workspace access.
sourceRef: ASIM-Security/Information Protection/Information Protection Team Wiki:/General Onboarding Material/Case Management/Secure data transferring on cases with DTM

symptom: Customer cannot access files shared via DTM workspace, receives access denied or empty workspace
rootCause: Customer is using a different account than the one listed as the case contact; DTM access is tied to the contact email on the case
solution: Verify customer is using the account listed as contact for the case. If another person needs access, add them as an extra contact on the case directly — they will automatically gain DTM workspace access

symptom: Databricks Data Quality connector "Test Connection" fails with Error 403: "The Databricks access token has expired"
rootCause: Azure Databricks access token expired (valid for 90 days by default), or Purview MSI lacks required Key Vault permissions, or network connectivity issues to Databricks workspace
solution: 1) Create new Databricks access token and update the linked service (tokens expire after 90 days). 2) For Key Vault credentials: grant Purview MSI Object ID (not Application ID) required permissions in Key Vault Access Policy. 3) Verify network connectivity using nslookup (DNS) and telnet (port). 4) If connectivity passes, collect network traces. 5) Check logs for correlation IDs for further diagnosis.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/EEEs Section/CoPilot Troubleshooting Guides (AI generated TSGs) - Need further review/Databricks DQ connector "Test Connection" failure

symptom: Glossary terms not showing up in Discovery experience after enabling new Data Governance experience
rootCause: In the new experience, governance domains (previously called business domains) and business concepts including glossary terms must be created and published before they appear in Discovery
solution: Create governance domains → add business concepts and glossary terms under those domains → publish the terms. Only published terms will appear in the Discovery experiences.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Apps - Data Governance/Troubleshooting Guides/Governance Domains

symptom: Databricks DQ connector 'Test Connection' failure with Error 403: 'The Databricks access token has expired'
rootCause: Azure Databricks access token expired (default validity is 90 days)
solution: Create a new Databricks access token and update the linked service/connection with the new token
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/EEEs Section/CoPilot Troubleshooting Guides (AI generated TSGs) - Need further review/Databricks DQ connector "Test Connection" failure

solution: Grant Purview MSI the required permissions in Key Vault Access Policy. Use only the Purview MSI Object ID — do not include the Application ID

symptom: Login error when first accessing a newly deployed external Azure subscription for Purview testing/repro. Unable to sign in to the Azure portal.
rootCause: IPv6 connectivity issue prevents first-time login to newly provisioned external Azure subscriptions.
solution: Temporarily disable IPv6 in Network adapter settings (Control Panel > Network and Internet > Network and Sharing Center > Change adapter settings > Wi-Fi Properties > uncheck Internet Protocol Version 6). Log in for the first time, then re-enable IPv6.

symptom: Self-hosted integration runtime (SHIR) fails to connect in Azure Government (Fairfax) or Azure China (Mooncake) environment; connectivity errors to Service Bus, Data Movement frontend, Storage, or SQL endpoints
rootCause: Azure Government and Azure China sovereign clouds use different service endpoints than Azure public cloud. Standard public cloud endpoints (*.servicebus.windows.net, *.frontend.clouddatahub.net, *.core.windows.net, *.database.windows.net) do not resolve in sovereign clouds.
solution: Configure SHIR firewall rules to allow sovereign-cloud-specific endpoints on port 443 (1433 for SQL). Fairfax: *.servicebus.usgovcloudapi.net, *.frontend.datamovement.azure.us, *.core.usgovcloudapi.net, *.database.usgovcloudapi.net. Mooncake: *.servicebus.chinacloudapi.cn, *.frontend.datamovement.azure.cn, *.core.chinacloudapi.cn, *.database.chinacloudapi.cn. download.microsoft.com remains the same across all clouds.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Processes/Sovereign Cloud/USNAT & Fairfax - Government Access & Requirements

symptom: ADF copy activity lineage not being pushed or appearing in Purview after pipeline execution; lineage tab shows no data for ADF-processed assets
rootCause: The Integration Runtime (IR) executing the copy activity in ADF cannot access Purview due to missing private endpoint configuration. The IR needs network connectivity to Purview ingestion endpoint.
solution: Ensure the IR executing the copy activity in ADF can access Purview via Private Endpoint: (1) Use Managed Private Endpoint through Purview setup per https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/data-factory/how-to-access-secured-purview-account, or (2) Create a Private Endpoint in the VNet where the SHIR is hosted.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Lineage/ADF Lineage not being pushed to Purview

symptom: SSIS lineage in Purview only shows source and destination assets; no transformation, query, or stored procedure lineage is captured; on-premise data sources not supported for SSIS lineage
rootCause: Product limitation: SSIS lineage only supports source-to-destination mapping. No transformation, query, or stored procedure lineage is available. Only data sources connectable through Azure Integration Runtime are supported (no on-premise data sources).
solution: Inform customer this is a known product limitation. SSIS lineage only captures source-to-destination mapping. For transformation/stored procedure lineage or on-premise data sources, these are not currently supported.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Lineage/Assets do not have lineage/SSIS lineage

rootCause: Product limitation: Column lineage is not supported for Power BI data sources in Purview.
solution: Inform customer that Power BI column lineage is not supported in Purview. Only dataset-level lineage is available.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Lineage/Assets do not have lineage/Troubleshoot Power Bi Lineage issues

symptom: Power BI cross-workspace dataset lineage not appearing in Purview; workspace with a report from a shared dataset does not show lineage to the source dataset in another workspace
rootCause: Product limitation: Cross-workspace dataset (workspace which has a report from a shared dataset) to report lineage is not supported. PG is working on adding this feature.
solution: Inform customer that cross-workspace dataset lineage is not currently supported for Power BI in Purview. The Product Group is working to add this feature.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Lineage/Assets do not have lineage/Troubleshoot Power Bi Lineage issues

symptom: Power BI lineage in Purview only shows limited external data sources; external sources other than ADLSGen1/Gen2, Azure SQL, and Blob are missing from Power BI lineage
rootCause: Product limitation: Power BI scan currently only supports a few external data sources for lineage extraction: ADLSGen1, ADLSGen2, Azure SQL, and Azure Blob Storage.
solution: Inform customer that Power BI scan lineage support is limited to ADLSGen1/Gen2, Azure SQL, and Blob external sources. Other external data sources are not currently supported for lineage.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Lineage/Assets do not have lineage/Troubleshoot Power Bi Lineage issues

symptom: Power BI lineage not appearing in Purview until source schema is hard-coded with *** (e.g., Azure SQL DB source); lineage only shows after manual schema specification
rootCause: In some cases, Power BI does not expose source connection details for dynamic schema detection. Hard-coding the source schema forces lineage to be recognized by Purview.
solution: If lineage is not appearing for a Power BI external source (e.g., Azure SQL DB), try hard-coding the source schema with *** in the Power BI dataset definition. This workaround forces lineage recognition.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Lineage/Assets do not have lineage/Troubleshoot Power Bi Lineage issues

symptom: Purview account creation or deletion fails with Conflict error and RequestDisallowedByPolicy in Azure Portal; Azure Policy blocks operations on managed resources (Storage Account, EventHub Namespace)
rootCause: Azure Policy rules in the customer subscription deny create/update operations on managed resources (Storage Account, EventHub Namespace) in the Purview managed resource group. Purview creates these managed resources automatically and they must pass all policy checks.
solution: 1. Identify the blocking policy from Activity Logs or Kusto: cluster('babylon.eastus2').database('babylonMdsLogs').ProjectBabylonLogEvent | where * contains 'accountname' | where TracingLevel == 'Error'. 2. Ask customer to modify policy definition to allow managed resources, OR apply specific tags on the Purview account (tags propagate automatically to managed resources, granting policy exception). 3. Customer can create a policy exemption for the managed resource group.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Microsoft Purview Administration/Create or delete an Microsoft Purview instance/Cannot Delete Account
