symptom: Users unable to access Purview Governance Portal despite having Owner, Data Curator, Data Source Admin, and Reader roles assigned via IAM. Error: You do not have access to this Purview account. Contact an Owner or User Access Administrator (UAA) for access in Access control (IAM). Account was created before August 2021 and uses legacy Azure RBAC instead of collection-based access.
rootCause: Purview accounts created before 18 August 2021 use Azure RBAC for access management. New Purview accounts created after that date require collection-based access management via Purview Governance Portal. Legacy IAM roles alone are insufficient for portal access on newer permission models.
solution: Configure roles from Microsoft Purview data plane using collection access management. Use InPrivate browser and have the Resource Group Owner grant Purview Root Collection Admin via REST API: POST /subscriptions/{subId}/resourceGroups/{rg}/providers/Microsoft.Purview/accounts/{account}/addRootCollectionAdmin?api-version=2021-07-01 with body {objectId: userObjectId}. Ref: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/purview/catalog-permissions
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Security and Access Control/Opening Microsoft Purview Governance Portal/Unable to access Purview Governance Portal through the Portal or URL

symptom: Only Collection Admin has left the organization. Remaining users cannot access Purview Governance Portal or assign roles to other people. Error: You do not have access to this Purview account. Contact an Owner or Collection Administrator for access. InvalidAuthenticationTokenAudience error may also appear.
rootCause: In Microsoft Purview, Collection Admin role is required to assign roles to others. When the sole Collection Admin leaves the organization, no one else can manage access through the portal.
solution: Use Service Principal (SPN) with REST API to add new Root Collection Admin: 1) Create SPN in AAD App registrations, get client ID/tenant ID/secret. 2) Assign SPN as Owner of Resource Group + Contributor of Purview Account via IAM. 3) Generate Bearer token via POST to https://login.microsoftonline.com/{tenantId}/oauth2/token with grant_type=client_credentials, resource=https://management.core.windows.net. 4) Call POST https://management.azure.com/subscriptions/{subId}/resourceGroups/{rg}/providers/Microsoft.Purview/accounts/{account}/addRootCollectionAdmin?api-version=2021-07-01 with Authorization: Bearer {token} and body {objectId: newAdminObjectId}.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Security and Access Control/Opening Microsoft Purview Governance Portal/Unable to access Purview Governance Portal through the Portal or URL

symptom: Purview Governance Portal shows Not Authorized error when Purview account is configured with Private Endpoints (VNET). Client machine is not on the same virtual network as the Purview Private Endpoint.
rootCause: When Purview is configured with Private Endpoints, only clients within the same VNET (or peered VNET) can access the Purview Governance Portal. Clients outside the configured VNET are denied access.
solution: Access the Purview Governance Portal from a client machine in the same virtual network (VNET) that has been configured for the Purview account Private Endpoint. Ref: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/purview/catalog-private-link-troubleshoot#common-errors-and-messages
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Security and Access Control/Opening Microsoft Purview Governance Portal/Unable to access Purview Governance Portal through the Portal or URL

symptom: Customer unable to see Data Factory or Data Share under External Connections in Purview Governance Portal. External Connections section is missing or empty despite having data factory resources.
rootCause: User does not have both Owner role at the Subscription level and Purview Data Curator role at the Purview Account level. Both roles are required to view and manage External Connections (Data Factory and Data Share).
solution: 1) Under Subscriptions, verify customer has Owner or Account Admin role. If not, assign Owner role. 2) Under Purview Account, add customer as Owner and Purview Data Curator. 3) Close any existing Purview Governance Portal and relaunch. Customer should now see External Connections (Data Factory and Data Share) and be able to add existing resources. Note: When connections are added, Purview automatically creates resources with Purview Data Curator role.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/Troubleshooting Guides (TSGs)/Security and Access Control/Roles and Permissions in Microsoft Purview/Roles to View External Connectors (ADF & OnPrem)

solution: Temporarily disable LNA setting in browser. Chrome: see https://developer.chrome.com/blog/local-network-access. Edge: use LocalNetworkAccessRestrictionsEnabled policy. Purview Engineering engaged with Chrome and Edge teams for long-term fix.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Processes/Known Issues

symptom: Scan jobs initiated in late November run for unusually long time and transition to Cancelled status in portal without displaying any error message.
rootCause: Backend infrastructure upper execution limit for ingestion operations prevented new ingestion jobs from being picked up. Portal incorrectly reflected scan status as Cancelled.
solution: Backend execution limit removed across all regions on Dec 10. Scans after Dec 10 process normally. Some pre-Dec-10 scans may resume and complete with delay (expected). If issue recurs after Dec 10, raise new ICM.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Processes/Known Issues

symptom: Snowflake scans using Azure IR fail with JDBC driver internal error: /home/guest/snowflake_jdbc0.log.0.lck. MBI_JDBC4_D0223 error.
rootCause: JDBC driver internal error in Azure IR environment affecting Snowflake scans across multiple environments.
solution: Engineering investigating with highest priority (ICM 712598380). Attach related support tickets to ICM for centralized tracking. No workaround available.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Processes/Known Issues

symptom: Data Quality error records no longer publishing to configured Fabric or ADLS Gen2 storage location after November 26.
rootCause: Multi-region support for error-record publishing enabled globally post-November 26. Existing configurations no longer align with new region-aware logic.
solution: Reconfigure error-records storage location in alignment with asset region. See updated documentation: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/purview/unified-catalog-data-quality-error-records
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Processes/Known Issues

symptom: Schema tab missing for Resource sets when scanning parquet files using Azure IR or Managed VNET IR (Jan 2026). Error: NoSuchMethodError org.apache.logging.log4j.util.StackLocatorUtil.getCallerClassLoader or NoClassDefFoundError BridgeFileSystem.
rootCause: Mismatched log4j-api on classpath causes NoSuchMethodError, preventing Java parquet reader from opening file.
solution: Hot fix deployed in East US, East US2, Canada Central, West Europe on Feb 6. Remaining regions by Feb 13. Attach case to ICM 21000000830919. Verify with KQL: DataScanAgentLinuxEvent where Message contains NoSuchMethodError or NoClassDefFoundError.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Processes/Known Issues

symptom: Business assets Description shows raw HTML code (div, span, p tags) instead of rendered content (Feb 2026).
solution: Attach case to ICM 51000000889887. ETA for fix was Feb 23.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Processes/Known Issues

symptom: SQL on-premises scans using SHIR 5.51 complete successfully but ingest zero assets. Logs show FileLoadException: Could not load Microsoft.Bcl.AsyncInterfaces Version=1.0.0.0.
rootCause: SHIR version 5.51 has dependency issue with Microsoft.Bcl.AsyncInterfaces assembly causing ingestion module initialization failure.
solution: Upgrade to latest SHIR version 5.63.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Processes/Known Issues

sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Processes/Known Issues

rootCause: Certificate verification issue in IRCTL version 1.4.0.
solution: Upgrade to IRCTL version 1.4.1 which includes the fix. Attach case to ICM 21000000914207.

symptom: Creating new Oracle scans fails with error "The version of the linked service is not supported" while saving configuration. Test connection succeeds. Already configured scans continue working.
rootCause: Linked service version compatibility issue affecting new Oracle scan configurations.
solution: Attach case to ICM 51000000934177 and update Loop with customer details. ETA was March 31.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Processes/Known Issues

symptom: Business Custom Attribute with Date datatype shows Invalid Date on Custom Attributes page after saving value from Data Product (March 2026).
solution: Engineering identified issue; code fix in deployment pipeline expected EOW. Attach case to ICM 21000000964884.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Processes/Known Issues

symptom: EU DfM case linked to WW ICM RCRI: transferred/second case owner or collab case owner cannot access RCRI via CheckAccess API.
rootCause: ICM runs CheckAccess on the DFM instance matching the RCRI/ICM instance. EU DFM case ID does not exist in WW, so no one after the initial creator gets access.
solution: Only original RCRI creator has access via ACL. Known bug tracked in work items 35507191 and 35507130.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Processes/Supportability for Restricted CRIs (rCRI)

symptom: After taking ownership of a DFM case or receiving JIT approval, unable to view linked RCRI despite AccessCheck.
rootCause: AccessCheck does not immediately sync after ownership change or JIT approval.
solution: Log out from ICM portal and log back in. This forces access refresh and should allow viewing the RCRI.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Processes/Supportability for Restricted CRIs (rCRI)

symptom: Access issues when multiple DFM cases linked to a single RCRI.
rootCause: RCRI and AccessCheck design based on 1:1 relationship between RCRI and DFM case. ICM passes first case from SupportTicketId field to DfM for AccessCheck.
solution: Avoid linking multiple cases to one RCRI. This is a known design limitation.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Processes/Supportability for Restricted CRIs (rCRI)

symptom: Cannot see or access RCRI contents after creating via ASC using TSE/MID/AME account (post Oct 22).
rootCause: Bug in ASC passes incorrect format (UPN instead of alias) for TSE/MID/AME accounts. Account not added to ACL and DFM AccessCheck fails.
solution: Ask EEE/SEE/EE to add you explicitly to the RCRI. Bugs tracked: 35715517, 35743502.
sourceRef: Supportability/Azure Purview/Microsoft Purview:/[New wiki structure]Purview Data Governance/Processes/Supportability for Restricted CRIs (rCRI)

symptom: Support case added to Impact Assessment section of RCRI but not visible.
solution: Close the browser tab and reopen the RCRI. Navigate to Impact Assessment section to see the linked case.
